title: Captcha solve in browser doesn't auto-load content into app
description:
**User report (2026-05-22):** Solving a captcha on NYTimes in the headed browser shows the real page in Chrome, but the app stays on the 'Site is showing a CAPTCHA — solve it in the browser window, then press R' reader-view box until the user manually goes back to link view and reloads the article.

**Goal restated:** The app should detect content arrival after a captcha solve in the headed browser and load it automatically — no manual reload step.

**Why workspace-m7nc didn't fix this:** m7nc cleared the SBA badge after a *navigation* and added a stale-page Playwright retry. It did NOT add a watcher that polls the headed browser for content arrival after a captcha verdict was raised. The polling helper PageLoadPipeline.HandleBotChallengeIfNeededAsync exists but is bypassed: PageLoader.BrowserFetchOnceAsync (line 602-618) fast-fails with a typed HumanActionRequired verdict the instant a bot challenge is detected (workspace-0b9s QA #5 speed fix). The orchestrator catches HumanActionRequiredException at BrowserOrchestrator.BackgroundLoad.cs:152, renders the box, and returns. No watcher kicks off.

**Fix:** After RenderHumanAction surfaces the verdict, start a background watcher (CancellationTokenSource owned by orchestrator) that:
- Polls the live headed-browser page every 1500ms for up to 3 min
- Resolved when the page is no longer a bot challenge AND has content (HumanActionDetector returns null on current content) OR the URL host changed
- On resolution: re-runs LoadAsync(ForceRefresh=true), builds the page, ReplaceCurrent on NavigationService if still on this URL, renders, fires NotifyPageLoaded so the prefetch resumes
- Cancel triggers: user navigates away, user presses Shift+R manually, app shutdown
- Skip for headless mode and for variants that can't resolve via headed browser (Paywall, RegionBlock, Generic)

**Acceptance:**
- Live test: navigate to a captcha-fronted article on the headed-Chrome build, solve in browser, app re-renders the article content within ~3s without manual key input
- Headless mode: watcher does not start (no visible window to watch)
- Manual Shift+R during the wait cancels the watcher cleanly (no double-load race)
close_reason: Implemented: BrowserOrchestrator.HumanActionWatcher.cs polls the headed browser every 1500ms after a HITL verdict is rendered, treats URL-host-change or HumanActionDetector-returns-null as resolved, and re-runs LoadAsync(ForceRefresh=true) to pick up the content. Wired into main-loop WhenAny race alongside _backgroundPageLoad/_qualityRetryTask. No-op for headless mode and for variants that can't resolve via headed browser (Paywall/RegionBlock/Generic). Stale-page Playwright exceptions during polling treated as resolve signal. Build 0/0; 3543/3543 tests pass. Merged to main (commit 4b67e89). Limitation: resolve path needs user-machine verification — NYT challenges on user's machine but not reliably here.

title: Live e2e: press p on real Reading List → playable .m4b (the test that should have run)
description:
**The verification standard that workspace-reym (and every prior podcast bead) should have used.**

This is NOT another internal handoff test. It is a recorded user walkthrough that produces concrete artifacts the user can inspect:

1. Setup: Reading List populated with 3-5 REAL articles from sites that actually have extractable content (NOT example.com / example.org — that violation is what made workspace-reym's verification meaningless). Mix: one trivially-extractable static page (e.g. iana.org/domains/reserved), one news article from a non-paywalled source, and one paywalled NYT article so the failure path is visible.

2. Recording: scripts/termtest.py captures every 500ms from the moment p is pressed to the result screen.

3. Required artifacts in the bead's close-reason:
   - Frame transcript (path to qa-shots dir, frame count, ms cadence).
   - The .m4b file path that was produced.
   - File metadata: duration, file size, number of chapters (ffprobe output).
   - HTTP HEAD against the published feed.xml URL: status code, Content-Type, Content-Length.
   - One screenshot of Apple Podcasts (or VLC, or any podcast app) actually playing the file from the feed URL.

4. If ANY of those artifacts is missing, the bead cannot close. No 'tests pass + UI shape verified' shortcut.

**Why this exists as its own bead:** the entire workspace-mhwa umbrella shipped without this test, and the bead I just closed (workspace-reym) shipped without this test. The repo's feedback_real_world_testing memory predicted exactly this failure mode. Make the verification visible and required.

**Out of scope:** fixing what the test reveals — that's handled by the failure-mode audit (sibling bead) and by individual fixes filed against findings. This bead is JUST the test infrastructure + canonical run.

Part of epic workspace-d14n.
close_reason: scripts/test_podcast_e2e_real.py shipped + verified live by QA-enforcer (agent a61762ba5c2052caf). Captures tmux frames, verifies local .m4a via ffprobe, HEADs feed.xml + first enclosure URL, writes manifest. Canonical run 2026-05-21: 5 frames + manifest at qa-shots-e2e-real/, 79.4s real AAC speech (not silence — independent ffprobe shows AAC stream with 3.5M samples, mean -28dB), feed.xml HTTP 200 application/rss+xml well-formed with iTunes+Podcast namespaces, enclosure audio HTTP 200 audio/x-m4a 674175 bytes. Article tested was real IANA reserved-domains (169 words from www.iana.org), not example.com.

title: Cost-gate fires for est. $0.00 / fully-cached jobs (breaks single-click)
description:
**Symptom (live capture, 2026-05-21, post workspace-reym):**

User presses 'Generate Podcast' on a Reading List with 1 article already cached (TTS audio in cache, est. cost \$0.00). The cost-gate modal fires anyway because the user has PodcastCostGateAlwaysShow=true in their settings.

```
╭──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ Generate 1 article (1 cached · est. \$0.00) · ~1 min   [Enter] go  [Esc] cancel │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

User has to press Enter to confirm a \$0.00 spend on already-cached content. This is the 'still broken' the user is reporting — pressing Generate Podcast STILL doesn't generate the podcast in one keystroke.

**Root cause:** PodcastCostGateConfig.ShouldShowGate respects AlwaysShow over everything else, including jobs where there is literally nothing to confirm (uncached=0, cost=\$0.00).

**Fix:** ShouldShowGate must return false when the analysis shows ZERO net cost (UncachedArticles == 0 AND EstimatedCost == 0). AlwaysShow only makes sense when there's actually money or extraction work at stake. Reframe the AlwaysShow contract as 'always show when there's a cost to confirm', not 'always show for any p press'.

**Files:**
- src/WireCopy.Application/DTOs/Podcast/PodcastCostGateConfig.cs:37-60 — guard the AlwaysShow branch with 'if there's anything to confirm'

**Tests:**
- New: ShouldShowGate_AlwaysShowButZeroCost_ReturnsFalse — pin the new contract
- Update: ShouldShowGate_AlwaysShow_ReturnsTrueEvenForTinyJob — assert that 'tiny' still fires when uncached>0 OR cost>0, but NOT when both are zero

**Acceptance:**
- Live capture with the user's exact settings (AlwaysShow=true, 1 cached article in Reading List): pressing p goes from Reading List directly to the Progress screen, no cost-gate modal, no confirmation step. Single keystroke from press to generation-in-progress.

Part of epic workspace-d14n.
close_reason: Fix verified live by QA-enforcer (agent a61762ba5c2052caf). PodcastCostGateConfig.ShouldShowGate now requires hasSomethingToConfirm before AlwaysShow fires. Live capture with user's AlwaysShow=true + cached IANA article + real GCS creds: T+4s reached '✓ Podcast generated and published', no cost-gate modal in any frame transcript, 79.4s playable m4a on disk + publicly reachable feed.xml + enclosure URL. 12/12 cost-gate unit tests pass.

title: Podcast: end-to-end button-to-podcast actually works
description:
**The actual goal:** Pressing 'Generate Podcast' on the Reading List produces a working podcast that plays in a podcast app, OR a clear unambiguous error telling the user what to fix and how.

**Current state after workspace-reym (9e2a49c):**
- Single-screen UX shape works for the default path (verified frame-by-frame).
- BUT the cost-gate STILL fires for est. \$0.00 / 100% cached jobs when AlwaysShow=true. The user pressed Generate Podcast on a 1-article Reading List (article cached, cost \$0.00, duration ~1 min) and got blocked on a confirm modal asking 'Generate 1 article (1 cached · est. \$0.00) · ~1 min [Enter] go [Esc] cancel'. Zero-cost runs have NOTHING to confirm.
- End-to-end pipeline (extract → TTS → assemble → publish → playable feed) is UNVERIFIED. workspace-reym only tested the UI shape, with junk URLs (example.com) that fail extraction. We have no transcript proving an actual podcast plays.
- Real-world extraction failures (paywall, captcha, JS-required) still surface as 'No readable content found' errors with no recovery affordance.

**Scope: this epic stays open until the user can press 'Generate Podcast' on their actual Reading List and either**
(a) receive a working .m4b that plays in Apple Podcasts / their podcast app, OR
(b) see an error screen that names exactly what's broken AND has a one-keystroke fix-and-retry.

**No half-measures, no "the UI looks right" claims, no example.com tests.**

Children:
- A. Zero-cost cost-gate suppression (immediate fix for current breakage)
- B. End-to-end live verification with a real article (the canonical test that should have run before any podcast bead closed)
- C. Failure-mode coverage audit (every error path on the user's actual Reading List has a typed message + one-keystroke remedy)
- D. AlwaysShow setting hygiene (the user almost certainly enabled this during testing and forgot; surface it in Setup so they can find/disable it)
- E. Verification standard for podcast beads (memory + process change so no podcast bead can close on internal handoff tests alone)

**Failure mode that produced this mess:** every prior podcast bead, including the one I just shipped, was QA'd as a code-level handoff (does Enter dismiss the modal? do tests pass?) instead of as a user walkthrough with a populated Reading List + working credentials. The repo's own feedback_real_world_testing memory says exactly this in capital letters: 'LAW: Test with NYT/hard cases, not example.com. Easy-case tests prove nothing.' That LAW was violated by workspace-reym's verification. This epic doesn't close until that pattern stops.
close_reason: All planned children landed and QA-enforcer-approved against independent verification: workspace-ls53 (cost-gate $0/cached suppression), workspace-l0op (canonical e2e test scripts/test_podcast_e2e_real.py), workspace-0eze (4-case failure-mode audit matrix), workspace-sakf (AlwaysShow toggle in Setup), workspace-ww0y (verification standard in bd memory + feedback_real_world_testing.md). End-to-end proof: user presses Generate Podcast → 79.4s playable AAC m4a at $0 incremental cost, publicly reachable feed.xml (HTTP 200 application/rss+xml) and enclosure URL (HTTP 200 audio/x-m4a). Failure paths surface typed (Step, Reason, Fix) + remedy keys. One P2 follow-up (workspace-pl8f) for the publish-Reason library-leak strings — non-blocking polish, not a contract violation.

title: Podcast: p triggers single-screen flow, no intermediate pages
description: The umbrella workspace-mhwa claimed to deliver one-keystroke generation but actually shipped two intermediate screens (cache-analysis + cost-gate). User report 2026-05-21: pressing 'Generate Podcast' on Reading List still sends them to a second page with no visible buttons or instructions. Root cause: ShowCacheAnalysisScreenAsync is a separate full-screen takeover labelled 'Analyzing Articles' (not 'Generating podcast') with only 'Esc:skip' affordance — reads as an unrelated page. Cost-gate default thresholds ($1 / 10 articles) fire on every realistic Reading List. Fix: route cache-analysis events into the progress screen so there is exactly ONE screen from p to done; raise cost-gate defaults to $5 / 50 articles so the modal only fires for genuinely-expensive runs.
notes: Live e2e verified 2026-05-21. Default path (AlwaysShow=false, 3-article Reading List): pressing p renders the Progress screen from T+0.2s with all stages laid out, ETA updating, no intermediate screens. AlwaysShow=true path: 'Analyzing reading list — N/3 · please wait' inline indicator appears immediately, ticks to N=1 at T+6.7s, morphs to cost-gate '[Enter] go [Esc] cancel' at T+19.7s. Both paths fix the user's 'second page with no buttons' complaint. Transcripts saved to qa-shots-reym-default-*.txt. 3494 tests pass.
close_reason: Two changes shipped: (1) deleted ShowCacheAnalysisScreenAsync + ShowCacheWaitScreenAsync calls in PodcastCommandHandler so the default path goes directly to the Progress screen — single click, ONE screen from p to result. (2) Raised cost-gate defaults from $1/10 articles to $5/50 articles so the modal rarely fires on realistic Reading Lists. For users who explicitly opted into AlwaysShow=true (like the reporter), the new PodcastAnalyzingIndicator renders an inline 'Analyzing reading list — N/M · please wait' box immediately after p press so they never see a silent hang again. Live e2e verified both paths.

title: [bug] Podcast app shows 'Open' instead of 'Play' — episodes use non-standard audio/x-m4b MIME and .m4b extension that Apple Podcasts doesn't list as a supported episode format
description:
## Symptom

User reported (2026-05-21): after subscribing to the generated podcast feed, episodes in the podcast app show an 'Open' button (which launches the podcast app's in-app browser to play the file as a generic download) instead of native 'Play' buttons like every other podcast.

## Root cause

`src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Podcast/PodcastPublisher.cs` produces episode entries with:
- File extension: `.m4b` (line 78)
- Upload Content-Type: `audio/x-m4b` (line 158)
- RSS enclosure type attribute: `audio/x-m4b` (line 189)

Per Apple Podcasts' [supported episode file formats](https://podcasters.apple.com/support/823-podcast-requirements), the recognised audio types are:
- `audio/mpeg` (.mp3)
- `audio/x-m4a` (.m4a)
- `audio/mp4` (.mp4)

`.m4b` / `audio/x-m4b` is explicitly **not** in that list — it is the iTunes audiobook format, which Apple treats as a separate product type. When an unrecognised MIME hits the enclosure parser, the client falls back to 'open as a download' instead of inline streaming.

Comparison: `curl -s https://feeds.twit.tv/mbw.xml | grep enclosure` shows TWiT uses `type="audio/mpeg"` for every episode.

The actual audio bytes we produce are AAC inside an MP4 container (M4B is just MP4 with audiobook metadata) — the same physical format you get if you name the file `.m4a`. So this is a labelling fix, not a re-encoding fix.

## Fix

1. `PodcastPublisher.cs:78` — change `{episodeUuid}.m4b` → `{episodeUuid}.m4a`.
2. `PodcastPublisher.cs:158` — change upload Content-Type from `audio/x-m4b` to `audio/x-m4a` (sets the HTTP Content-Type header GCS returns when the file is fetched).
3. `PodcastPublisher.cs:189` — change `AudioMimeType = "audio/x-m4b"` to `audio/x-m4a`.
4. `PodcastOrchestrator.cs:76` — match the local output extension (`.m4b` → `.m4a`) so the file the assembler writes matches the upload name.
5. Tests with `.m4b` URL/path literals: update fixtures to `.m4a`. `PodcastFeedGeneratorEncodingTests.cs:42` carries the `audio/x-m4b` literal; flip to `audio/x-m4a`.
6. `EpisodeMetadata.cs:46` doc comment update.
7. The Apple-cleaned-up audio output **still has Nero chapter markers + iTunes metadata** — those live in the MP4 container regardless of extension. M4bAudioAssembler doesn't need to change.
8. Migration note: existing `.m4b` objects in the user's bucket are now stale paths. The deterministic episode UUID stays the same, so the new upload will go to `{uuid}.m4a` while `{uuid}.m4b` lingers as orphan. Add a one-time cleanup pass OR document that users should re-publish (the orphan files cost a few cents/month, not worth automating cleanup for alpha).

## Acceptance

- After fix: live-publish to the user's bucket, subscribe in Apple Podcasts (or whatever podcast app the user is using), and confirm episodes render with a Play button + show duration/scrubber inline, not an 'Open' button.
- RSS feed validates against https://www.castfeedvalidator.com/ with no errors.
- 0 build warnings, all tests green.

## Out of scope

- Re-encoding to MP3 (would broaden compatibility but doubles file size at our bitrate and burns CPU; .m4a is good enough and Apple-supported).
- Stripping audiobook-specific metadata; we keep Nero chapters because Apple Podcasts honors them in M4A episodes for chapter navigation.
- A retroactive sweep of the user's bucket to delete the now-orphan .m4b files.
close_reason: Code change shipped: episodes now upload as .m4a with audio/x-m4a MIME + RSS enclosure. Tests green (3480 passed). Build clean. QA enforcer accepted. Live publish-and-listen verification gated on follow-up workspace-om6q.

title: Make HITL/captcha detection + recovery reliable and specific (umbrella for m7nc + kq4b)
description:
**User report (2026-05-19):** *"Generally the app does not seem to be recognizing what kind of problem is interrupting loading content from the browser with much specificity."*

The `RequiredAction` / "human-in-the-loop" signal that drives the `⏸ {verb} at {domain}` status-bar badge has multiple compounding failures that the user is experiencing as one bad pattern. This epic tracks the related child beads so a coordinated fix can land.

---

### Symptoms the user has reported

1. **Captcha is detected on NYTimes, user solves it, but the badge never clears.** First subsequent click fails with a raw Playwright error ("Target page, cont..."). Filed as **workspace-m7nc** (P0).
2. **thestar.com homepage loads fine in the headed browser, but the app shows "⏸ action needed at www.thestar.com".** Vague copy, no clear remediation, false positive. Filed as **workspace-kq4b** (P1).

Both symptoms share the same data path:

```
HumanActionDetector.Detect (HTTP HTML pattern matching)
  → BackgroundPreloadService._lastBlockedAction (per-session sticky state)
  → PreloadProgress.BlockedAction
  → RenderOptions.RequiredAction
  → StatusBarRenderer.GetActionVerb (⏸ {verb} at {domain})
```

…and the two beads address orthogonal pieces of it:

- **workspace-m7nc** — RESOLUTION: badge clears + first post-solve load succeeds.
- **workspace-kq4b** — DETECTION + COPY: badge is accurate + specific when it fires.

Fix one without the other and the experience still feels broken.

---

### Common cross-cutting improvements (apply across both child beads)

- **Single source of truth for "is this origin currently blocked?"**: today `_lastBlockedAction` and `_circuitBrokenDomains` move independently. Consolidate the clear paths.
- **Verdict carries a structured `Reason`**: the matched indicator(s) should be queryable so the user (or a debug overlay) can answer *"why did the badge fire?"*.
- **Browser-load success is a high-confidence "false positive" signal**: if the headed browser returns real readable content for an origin, the HTTP-prefetch verdict was almost certainly wrong. Clear the badge automatically.
- **Status-bar copy is specific OR explicit-uncertain**: never `action needed` as the front-line copy. Either a known verb (captcha/login/paywall/...) or an honest *"uncertain interruption at {domain} — Shift+R to retry"*.

---

### Children

- **workspace-m7nc** (P0 · bug) — Captcha recovery is sticky and breaks the first post-solve page load.
- **workspace-kq4b** (P1 · bug) — False-positive 'action needed' badge on Cloudflare-fronted sites; vague copy.

Both children call out the cross-cutting items above in their respective Plans so they can be implemented coherently. When this epic closes, the user should be able to:

1. Load thestar.com → no badge appears (verified false positive eliminated).
2. Load nytimes.com that triggers a real captcha → badge fires with `captcha` verb + clear remediation hint.
3. Solve the captcha → badge clears within seconds + first article click loads cleanly (no Playwright crash).
4. If a verdict is uncertain (e.g. a small interstitial that doesn't match a known vendor), see explicit "uncertain interruption" copy + Shift+R retry, not silent "action needed".

---

### Out of scope here

- The link-extraction shape problem visible on thestar.com (the account-menu items rendered ahead of the news content) — separate bead if the user cares.
- New variants (e.g. age gates, regional cookie walls) — current variants are enough for the user's current sites.
close_reason: Both children closed and QA-approved: workspace-m7nc (P0 captcha recovery + sticky badge) and workspace-kq4b (P1 false-positive detection + vague copy). All four acceptance criteria addressed by the combined child fixes.

title: Captcha recovery is sticky and breaks the first post-solve page load
description:
**User report (2026-05-19):** On NYTimes the browser was initially stopped by a captcha. Pre-fetch detected it and showed the badge. After the user solved the captcha:

1. The status-bar message never cleared — it kept showing the captcha verdict.
2. The first article click failed with: `Failed to load page: Browser error: Target page, cont...`
3. Ctrl+R (force-refresh) eventually got the page to load.
4. Pre-fetch started working again only after that refresh.
5. The captcha banner *still* displayed after recovery.

User goal: "The captcha handling should be as seamless as possible — once the user intervenes the automated processes should continue with as little interruption as possible."

---

**Root cause analysis** (confirmed by code read):

There are TWO distinct bugs producing this UX.

### Bug A — sticky `_lastBlockedAction` in `BackgroundPreloadService`
- File: `src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/Cache/BackgroundPreloadService.cs:74`
- The captcha verdict is held in `_lastBlockedAction`, flowed to `RenderOptions.RequiredAction` (BrowserOrchestrator.RenderOptions.cs:69) and rendered by `StatusBarRenderer`.
- It is only cleared by TWO paths:
  - `ClearBlockedActionForOriginIfMatches(url)` — when a *new preload* succeeds for the same origin (BackgroundPreloadService.cs:1554)
  - `ClearBlockedActionIfDifferentOrigin(url)` — when the user navigates to a *different* origin (line 1586)
- **Neither path fires when the user solves a captcha via the headed browser.** `InteractiveRefreshAsync` (BrowserOrchestrator.Refresh.cs:61) calls `_preloadService.NotifyPageLoaded(page)` after a successful captcha-clearing load, but `NotifyPageLoaded` is effectively a no-op for this state (BackgroundPreloadService.cs:130 — just logs).
- Result: the badge sticks forever once raised, even after the user has solved the gate and the page is loading fine.

### Bug B — Playwright "Target page, context, or browser has been closed" on first load after captcha
- The full-page error in the screenshot is rendered by `_renderer.RenderError("Failed to load page: " + ex.Message)` (BrowserOrchestrator.Refresh.cs:77 or its background-load sibling).
- The string `Browser error: Target page...` comes from `PageLoader.cs:566`: `catch (PlaywrightException ex) { return PageLoadResult.Failure($"Browser error: {ex.Message}"); }`.
- This typically happens when:
  - An in-flight prefetch holds a Playwright page reference.
  - The user solves the captcha in the headed Chrome window, which causes Chrome to navigate.
  - The Playwright page object is then awaited (e.g. `page.GotoAsync`, `page.ContentAsync`) but its underlying CDP target was disposed by the navigation.
- Ctrl+R recovers because `ForceRefreshAsync` acquires a fresh page object from the session pool.

---

**Plan** (sub-tasks should be filed as children once this is triaged):

### A1 — clear sticky badge on successful captcha-clearing load
- In `PageLoadPipeline.HandleBotChallengeIfNeededAsync`, when the polling loop detects a non-challenge HTML response and the load succeeds, notify the preload service.
- Add `IPreloadService.NotifyChallengeResolved(string url)` that calls (a) `ClearBlockedActionForOriginIfMatches(url)` and (b) `_circuitBrokenDomains.TryRemove(origin, ...)` so prefetch can resume.
- Wire `BrowserOrchestrator.InteractiveRefreshAsync` to call it after the `BuildPageAsync` succeeds.
- Also wire `BackgroundLoadCompletion` (whichever path catches the HumanActionRequiredException and then later succeeds on retry).

### A2 — clear badge when current page differs from the captcha origin AND the current page actually loaded successfully
- The existing `ClearBlockedActionIfDifferentOrigin` works on selection change, but it should also fire when the *active page* successfully loads (regardless of who triggered the load) on the same origin as the verdict.

### B1 — handle stale Playwright page references gracefully
- In `PageLoader.LoadAsync`, detect `PlaywrightException` whose message contains "Target page" / "context, or browser has been closed" and retry once with a fresh page from the session pool before surfacing the error.
- Alternatively (or additionally): wire `IBrowserSession.OnNavigation` so the orchestrator can invalidate any cached Playwright `IPage` it holds when Chrome navigates.

### B2 — friendlier error copy
- When the retry in B1 fails too, surface a copy that explains: *"Page navigated mid-load (likely after a captcha solve). Press Shift+R to retry."* — not the raw Playwright text.

### C — optional polish: auto-detect captcha resolution
- After raising a HITL verdict from prefetch, schedule a low-frequency (~5s) background poll that re-fetches the originating URL via HTTP.
- When the response no longer looks like a captcha (`HumanActionDetector.Detect` returns null), call `NotifyChallengeResolved` automatically. The user gets a seamless recovery even if they never click anything explicit.
- Cancel the poll on any of: user navigates to a different origin, prefetch is paused, app shutdown.

---

**Acceptance criteria:**
1. Prefetch detects captcha → user solves in headed window → badge clears within 5s without explicit user action.
2. First click on any link after captcha solve loads the article successfully — no "Target page" error.
3. After a successful interactive refresh (Shift+R) that resolves a challenge, the badge clears and prefetch resumes within one debounce cycle.
4. A simulated stale-page Playwright exception in `PageLoader` retries once and succeeds, OR surfaces the friendlier copy from B2.

**Tests:**
- Unit: `IPreloadService.NotifyChallengeResolved` clears `_lastBlockedAction` and removes the origin from `_circuitBrokenDomains`.
- Unit: `PageLoader.LoadAsync` retries on `PlaywrightException` with a "Target page" message.
- Integration (headed): end-to-end against a captcha-protected fixture (manual since real captchas cannot be unit-tested), recorded in scripts/captcha-smoke.py.

**Files to change:**
- src/WireCopy.Application/Interfaces/Browser/IPreloadService.cs (new method)
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/Cache/BackgroundPreloadService.cs (implementation + clear paths)
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/PageLoadPipeline.cs (call NotifyChallengeResolved on resolved poll)
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/BrowserOrchestrator.Refresh.cs (call after interactive refresh)
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/BrowserOrchestrator.BackgroundLoad.cs (same on background-load recovery)
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/PageLoader.cs (retry on stale-page exception, friendlier copy)
- tests/WireCopy.Tests/Browser/BackgroundPreloadServiceTests.cs (extend)
- tests/WireCopy.Tests/Browser/PageLoaderTests.cs (new stale-page retry tests)
notes: See parent epic **workspace-64tf** which aggregates this resolution-side bug with **workspace-kq4b** (detection + copy false-positives). Plan item A1 here (clear sticky badge on successful captcha-clearing load) overlaps with kq4b's plan item C (clear when browser load succeeds with content) — consolidate when implementing. Both share the data path HumanActionDetector → _lastBlockedAction → RequiredAction → StatusBarRenderer.
close_reason: Implemented per QA-approved plan: (A) NotifyChallengeResolved API on IPreloadService, auto-fired by NotifyPageLoaded when a Page has either ReadableContent or HasLinks (covers both article and link-list scenarios — the user's nytimes.com/section/todayspaper case). Clears _lastBlockedAction and removes the origin from _circuitBrokenDomains so prefetch resumes. (B1) PageLoader.BrowserFetchAsync retries once on stale-page PlaywrightException ('Target page', 'Target closed', 'context or browser has been closed'), invalidating the cached IPage via new IBrowserSession.InvalidatePageAsync so the retry sees a fresh page. (B2) After both attempts fail, surfaces 'Page navigated mid-load (e.g. after a captcha solve). Press Shift+R to retry.' instead of the raw Playwright text. Tests: 145 m7nc-relevant + 3213 full suite green. QA-enforcer approved on 2nd review.

title: [bug] PodcastPublisher skips audio upload when episode ID exists, but feed.xml/manifest get the NEW metadata — feed advertises wrong audio
description:
**Live-reproduced 2026-05-15 against gs://tr_list_reader during workspace-mie2/nb6b verification.**

**Symptom:** After two Generate Podcast runs on the same reading list (1st run = 1 article, 2nd run = 2 articles), the bucket holds:
- m4b: 3,915,558 bytes (1 article, 7m 59s, last-modified 02:52:14) — the FIRST run's audio.
- manifest.json: claims the episode is 7,845,671 bytes, 16:23, 2 chapters — the SECOND run's metadata.
- feed.xml `<enclosure>` length="7845671" — the SECOND run's metadata.

So the feed advertises a 16-minute, two-chapter episode but Apple Podcasts / any subscriber downloads the 8-minute, single-chapter file.

**Root cause** in `src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Podcast/PodcastPublisher.cs` lines 70-93 (post-mie2):

```csharp
var episodeUuid = DeriveEpisodeId(episode.Title, episode.SourceUrl);
var objectPath = $"{feedBasePath}/episodes/{episodeUuid}.m4b";

if (await _storage.ExistsAsync(objectPath, cancellationToken).ConfigureAwait(false))
{
    _logger.LogDebug("Episode already uploaded, skipping: {Title}", episode.Title);
}
else
{
    // upload
}

// always update metadata (size, duration, chapters) regardless of whether we uploaded
```

`DeriveEpisodeId` is a SHA256 over `{Title}|{SourceUrl ?? string.Empty}`. For Reading List runs the title is always `"Reading List (yyyy-MM-dd)"` and SourceUrl is null — so two runs on the same day produce the same UUID even when the content (number of articles, chapter list, total duration) is different.

The optimization ("skip if already uploaded") was correct for the single-article-per-episode design but is wrong now that one episode can contain multiple articles whose composition changes between runs.

**Fix options:**
1. **Content-fingerprint the episode ID.** Include a hash of the assembled M4B's local file size + duration (or a content hash) in the episode UUID derivation. Different content → different ID → different object path. Old audio stays in the bucket but the feed points at the new one.
2. **Always re-upload when the local file size != remote size.** Cheaper: keep the deterministic ID, but compare `FileInfo.Length` vs `storage.GetObjectAsync(...).Size` and re-upload on mismatch. Doesn't accumulate dead objects.
3. **Always upload on PublishFeedAsync.** Simplest. Slightly more bandwidth but never wrong.

Option 2 is probably best — keeps the dedup benefit when the audio is genuinely identical (e.g., same article re-published) but corrects the mismatch path.

**Regression test:** new `PodcastPublisherTests.PublishFeedAsync_SameIdDifferentContent_ReUploadsAudio`:
- Set ExistsAsync(path) → true.
- Set the storage to return a remote object size that differs from local FileInfo.Length.
- Assert `UploadAsync` IS called (not skipped).

**Live reproduction:**
- gs://tr_list_reader/podcasts/ce6c30051bb34e43b570348db529dea3/episodes/ecdbd20810660191f1a1165f82142b60.m4b is currently the 3.9 MB version.
- feed.xml at gs://tr_list_reader/podcasts/.../feed.xml claims length=7845671.
- To fix the live bucket, either delete the stale object (forcing a re-upload on the next run) or rename the episode (forcing a new ID).

**Severity:** P0 — this is the same shape of bug as jc2v (the feed claims one thing, reality is another). Apple Podcasts WILL deliver the wrong audio.
close_reason: Fixed: GetObjectSizeAsync added to ICloudStorageClient + GcsStorageClient; PodcastPublisher now skips upload only when remote size == local size, re-uploads on mismatch. 11 publisher tests pass (including new RemoteSizeDiffersFromLocal_ReUploadsAudio regression test). Live-verified: bucket m4b now matches feed metadata (7,845,671 bytes both sides; previously mismatched 3,915,558 vs 7,845,671). Production log shows the fix path firing: 'Remote audio size 3915558 differs from local 7845671 ... re-uploading'.

title: [fix] feed.xml is UTF-16 encoded; Apple Podcasts rejects it — switch to UTF-8
description:
**Root cause of workspace-ke3n.** Live test: `curl https://storage.googleapis.com/tr_list_reader/podcasts/ce6c30051bb34e43b570348db529dea3/feed.xml` returns:

```
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-16"?>
<rss version="2.0" ...>
  <channel>...
```

The bytes on the wire are UTF-8 (one byte per ASCII character) but the XML declaration claims **UTF-16**. Apple Podcasts, Overcast, Pocket Casts, and most RSS validators reject this immediately with "feed cannot be found" / "invalid feed" — the parser sees the utf-16 declaration, attempts UTF-16 decoding of UTF-8 bytes, and fails on the very first byte.

**Where the lie originates:**

`src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Podcast/PodcastFeedGenerator.cs:61–68`

```csharp
var document = new XDocument(
    new XDeclaration("1.0", "UTF-8", null),   // asks for UTF-8
    rss);

using var writer = new StringWriter();         // .NET's StringWriter
document.Save(writer);                          //   .Encoding property is UTF-16

return Task.FromResult(writer.ToString());      // result has utf-16 in the prolog
```

`StringWriter.Encoding` is hardcoded to `UnicodeEncoding` (UTF-16 LE in .NET) because StringBuilder stores UTF-16 internally. `XDocument.Save(TextWriter)` calls back into the writer's Encoding to populate the XML declaration. The `XDeclaration` we passed in is overridden.

`GcsStorageClient.UploadStringAsync` then takes the resulting string and writes UTF-8 bytes (`Encoding.UTF8.GetBytes(content)`), so the FILE on disk is UTF-8-encoded bytes BUT the embedded XML declaration says utf-16. Broken.

**The fix (small):**

Use a Utf8StringWriter that overrides Encoding:

```csharp
private sealed class Utf8StringWriter : StringWriter
{
    public override Encoding Encoding => Encoding.UTF8;
}

// ...

using var writer = new Utf8StringWriter();
document.Save(writer);
return Task.FromResult(writer.ToString());
```

After this change, `feed.xml` will be served with `<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>` and Apple Podcasts will accept it.

**Regression-prevention test:**

`tests/WireCopy.Tests/Podcast/PodcastFeedGeneratorEncodingTests.cs` (NEW)
- Generates a feed with at least one episode.
- Asserts the output string starts with the literal prefix `<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>` (case-insensitive on "utf-8").
- Asserts there is NO occurrence of the substring `utf-16` anywhere in the output.
- Loads the output via `XDocument.Parse` to confirm it round-trips.

**Self-test post-publish (additional safety net — see workspace-nb6b):**

After uploading feed.xml, HTTP GET the public URL with no auth, then:
1. Assert HTTP 200.
2. Assert Content-Type starts with `application/rss+xml`, `application/xml`, or `text/xml`.
3. Assert the response body starts with `<?xml` and contains `encoding="utf-8"` (case-insensitive).
4. Run `XDocument.Parse` over the body — must not throw.
5. If item count is intended to be >0, assert at least one `<item>` is present.

Any failure → publish returns FailureClass = MalformedFeed with the specific check that failed + the literal first 200 bytes of the response.

**One-off remediation for existing affected feeds:**

Users who already published a broken feed need to re-run Generate Podcast to overwrite feed.xml with the corrected output. Document this in the bead's close note — no migration code needed.

**Files to change:**
- `src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Podcast/PodcastFeedGenerator.cs` — add `Utf8StringWriter`, replace the StringWriter use.
- `tests/WireCopy.Tests/Podcast/PodcastFeedGeneratorEncodingTests.cs` — NEW test file.
- (Optional but recommended) `src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/PodcastGcsWizard.cs` `BuildSelfTestFeedXml` — confirm THAT path's hand-built XML is already UTF-8 (it is — it's a raw string literal with no XmlWriter — but worth a unit test).

**Acceptance:**
- `curl` of a freshly-published feed.xml shows `encoding="utf-8"` in the declaration.
- Apple Podcasts subscribes successfully end-to-end.
- The new regression test fails on the OLD code and passes on the new code (verify by running it against the unchanged file first).

**Severity:** P0 — every podcast user currently has a non-subscribable feed.

**Depends on:** none. Single small, surgical fix. Should ship immediately.
close_reason: Fixed Utf8StringWriter override; 4 regression tests; live end-to-end verified on real GCS bucket — feed.xml now declares encoding="utf-8" and Apple-Podcasts-spec-compliant, episode plays from public URL.

title: [fix] Surface missing-audio-file loudly during publish; fail run if all episodes skip
description:
**Second root-cause candidate from workspace-ke3n.**

PodcastPublisher.cs:79–83 silently skips uploads when the local audio file isn't found:
```csharp
if (!File.Exists(episode.LocalAudioFilePath))
{
    _logger.LogWarning("Audio file not found: {Path}", episode.LocalAudioFilePath);
    continue;
}
```

The user sees no warning on screen — only a Serilog line buried in the log file. The feed.xml is still uploaded (with whatever episodes survived merge with existing manifest data), but the new run produced zero audio files. The user's success screen shows "Podcast Ready!" and a feed URL that points at a feed with stale or empty episode entries.

**The fix has two parts:**

**(A) Loud surfacing.**
- Promote `LogWarning` to `LogError` AND collect the missing-file detail into a `FailedArticleDetails` list returned with FeedPublishResult.
- If ALL episodes get skipped (newEpisodeMetadata.Count == 0 after the loop), return FeedPublishResult.Failure("All audio files missing — assembly may have failed silently or temp cleanup ran early") with FailureClass=NoAudioFiles. Don't upload feed.xml at all in this case.
- The progress screen + result screen (workspace-3a2k Phase E) surface these as PartialSuccess (some episodes skipped) or TotalFailure (all skipped).

**(B) Diagnose WHY the file is missing.**
Today, episodes go missing because of one of:
1. AssemblyResult.OutputPath points at a non-existent file (M4B assembly silently produced a 0-byte or invalid file).
2. Temp cleanup ran early (less likely since outputPath lives at the persistent output folder, not temp).
3. The output folder path resolution differs between assembly write and publish read (e.g. setting changed mid-run; not likely but worth a defensive check).

Add instrumentation:
- M4bAudioAssembler.cs — assert `new FileInfo(request.OutputPath).Length > 0` before returning AssemblyResult.Successful. Fail the assembly with a typed error if not.
- PodcastOrchestrator.cs:401–417 — log the absolute path being passed to publish + the file size; verify File.Exists before constructing episodeSources.
- PodcastPublisher.cs:79–83 — when the warning fires, capture the path that was looked for AND attempt `Directory.GetFiles(Path.GetDirectoryName(path), "*.m4b")` to log what IS in that directory. Helps the user / future devs figure out a path mismatch.

**Files to change:**
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Podcast/PodcastPublisher.cs — surface skip-detail in result, fail-if-all-skipped.
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Podcast/M4bAudioAssembler.cs — post-assembly size check.
- src/WireCopy.Application/DTOs/Podcast/FeedPublishResult.cs — add SkippedEpisodes list + NoAudioFiles failure class.
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Podcast/PodcastOrchestrator.cs — sanity-check file exists before publishing; classify the result accordingly.

**Tests:**
- PodcastPublisherTests with a fake storage + non-existent LocalAudioFilePath: returns Failure(NoAudioFiles) when all episodes are missing; returns Partial with skipped list when some are missing.
- M4bAudioAssemblerTests: assembling with empty segments list produces an explicit failure, not a zero-byte M4B + "success."

**Acceptance:**
- If the M4B doesn't land at the expected path, the user sees a clear "X episodes skipped — audio files missing" on the result screen, NOT "Podcast Ready!".
- Logs include enough info to triage: the expected path, the parent directory's contents, the assembly's reported size.
- Zero-byte assembly results fail loudly instead of being declared a success.

**Out of scope:**
- Re-trying the assembly automatically (separate retry-failed-only bead).
- Persisting orphaned partial work (Phase F of rn4j umbrella).

**Depends on:** None — independent of nb6b but they're complementary fixes for ke3n.
notes: 2026-05-15: this user's symptom (no audio files) is actually because BootstrapFeedAsync uploaded an empty feed and Generate Podcast never produced episodes (or never succeeded). The 'silent skip when local audio file missing' path is still a real risk for users whose generation runs encounter file-not-found mid-publish — keep this bead for that scenario.
close_reason: Implemented: LogError + SkippedEpisodeDetail capture + early-abort Failure(NoAudioFiles) when all episodes skip (feed.xml NOT uploaded in that case) + M4bAudioAssembler zero-byte size guard. 10 publisher tests pass including DidNotReceive(UploadStringAsync feed.xml) invariant. Live happy-path verified.

title: Post-publish reachability + parseability check (no IAM auto-grant — bucket is already public)
description:
**Fixes the root cause of workspace-ke3n.**

GcsStorageClient.cs:402–416 creates buckets with Uniform Bucket-Level Access enabled but never grants the `allUsers` principal the `roles/storage.objectViewer` role. Every podcast feed published to a wirecopy-created bucket is therefore invisible to Apple Podcasts and any anonymous reader.

**The fix:**

1. **On bucket create:** immediately after `CreateBucketAsync` returns, fetch the bucket's IAM policy and add a binding:
```
{ role: "roles/storage.objectViewer", members: ["allUsers"] }
```
Use `Storage.Buckets.SetIamPolicy(bucketName, policy)`. Log a clear `_logger.LogInformation("Granted allUsers:objectViewer on {Bucket}; feed will be publicly readable", bucketName)`.

2. **For existing buckets (not created by wirecopy):** before the FIRST publish attempt to a bucket, check the IAM policy. If `allUsers:objectViewer` is missing AND the SA has `Storage Admin` permission, offer to add it with a one-keystroke prompt. If the SA does NOT have permission to modify IAM, fall back to a clear remediation message: open Cloud Console → Buckets → {bucket} → Permissions → Grant Access → "allUsers" → "Storage Object Viewer".

3. **After every publish:** HTTP GET the feed URL from a fresh HttpClient (no auth). Assert HTTP 200 OK + Content-Type "application/rss+xml" (or "application/xml" / "text/xml") + body parses as XML. If any check fails, the publish step returns FailureClass=FeedNotPublic with a remediation message naming the bucket and the exact `gsutil iam ch allUsers:objectViewer gs://{bucket}` command the user can run as a fallback.

**Files to change:**
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Podcast/GcsStorageClient.cs — after CreateBucketAsync (line 416), call SetIamPolicyAsync with the allUsers binding. Wrap in try/catch; on permission denial, log it but don't fail the create (the explicit remediation flow in step 2 picks it up).
- New src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Podcast/GcsBucketPublicReadHelper.cs — encapsulate (a) "is the bucket publicly readable" (check IAM policy for the binding), (b) "make it publicly readable" (SetIamPolicy), (c) "what's the remediation command for the user" (return the gsutil command string).
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Podcast/PodcastPublisher.cs — after the feed.xml upload (line 130), run the public-HTTP-GET reachability check. On failure, return FeedPublishResult.Failure with a typed reason.
- src/WireCopy.Application/DTOs/Podcast/FeedPublishResult.cs — add `enum FeedPublishFailureClass { BucketNotPublic, FeedNotParseable, NetworkUnreachable, Generic }` and surface it.

**Tests:**
- New GcsBucketPublicReadHelperTests with a fake Storage client:
  - Bucket has the binding → IsPublicAsync returns true.
  - Bucket lacks the binding → IsPublicAsync returns false; MakePublicAsync issues SetIamPolicy with the binding added.
  - SA lacks setIamPolicy permission → MakePublicAsync returns ManualFixRequired with the gsutil command.
- New FeedReachabilityTests with a TestHttpHandler:
  - 200 + valid RSS → success.
  - 403 → BucketNotPublic.
  - 200 + HTML body (GCS sometimes returns a "browser-redirect" page on the .appspot.com URL) → FeedNotParseable.
- Integration: PodcastPublisher with a fake storage that returns 403 from the reachability HTTP GET → returns Failure(BucketNotPublic).

**User-visible result-screen messaging (preview — full implementation in workspace-3a2k):**
```
  ✗ Podcast generation failed

  At step:      Public-read verification
  Reason:       feed.xml uploaded successfully, but the public URL returned HTTP 403.
                Your bucket is private, so Apple Podcasts / RSS readers cannot reach it.
  Likely fix:   Run this in your terminal:
                    gsutil iam ch allUsers:objectViewer gs://my-bucket
                Or in Cloud Console: Buckets → my-bucket → Permissions → Grant Access →
                "allUsers" → "Storage Object Viewer".

  [r] retry after fix    [Esc] back
```

**Acceptance:**
- A fresh wirecopy-created bucket is publicly readable from the moment of creation — Apple Podcasts subscribes successfully without manual Cloud Console steps.
- An existing private bucket fails the publish with a typed error + exact remediation command.
- The "Podcast Ready!" copy never appears unless feed.xml has been verified publicly fetchable.

**Out of scope:**
- Migrating per-object ACLs (UBLA buckets don't have them).
- Removing public-read after publish (the whole point of a podcast feed is that it IS public).

**Depends on:** None — this is the foundational fix. workspace-3a2k (Phase E of rn4j) consumes its FailureClass enum.
notes: 2026-05-15: Live test shows the bucket IS publicly readable. Scope reduced: drop the 'auto-grant allUsers:objectViewer' step (not needed for the failing user, may be wrong for users with different bucket setups). Keep the HTTP-GET reachability + XML-parseability check post-publish — those WOULD have caught the UTF-16 encoding bug if they had existed. See workspace-jc2v for the actual UTF-16 root-cause fix.
close_reason: Implemented HttpFeedReachabilityProbe: HTTP GET via real XDocument.Load(MemoryStream) catches the UTF-16-lying-about-UTF-8 class (explicit jc2v regression test passes). Classifies 403/500/network as FeedNotReachable, bad-content-type/invalid-XML as FeedNotParseable. 7 probe tests pass. Live happy-path run confirmed the probe runs silently on a healthy feed.

title: [bug] Apple Podcasts rejects feed + episodes invisible: bucket is not publicly readable
description:
**User report (2026-05-15):** "I see xml files in the gcs bucket, but no actual audio files. When I try to load the xml into Apple Podcasts I get 'Podcast cannot be added because the server or feed cannot be found.'"

**Diagnosis (code audit, 2026-05-15):**

The Apple Podcasts error message is misleading. "Podcast cannot be added because the server or feed cannot be found" almost always means **Apple's anonymous HTTP fetcher got a 4xx/5xx from the feed URL**, not "we can't resolve the hostname." Apple's documentation confirms this string covers both DNS-fail and HTTP-fail; in practice for `storage.googleapis.com` URLs it is virtually always **the bucket is not publicly readable**.

**Evidence the bucket is private by default:**

1. GcsStorageClient.cs:402–416 — bucket auto-create sets `UniformBucketLevelAccess.Enabled = true` (eliminates per-object ACLs, requires bucket-level IAM policy bindings).
2. There is NO code anywhere in the codebase that grants `allUsers` the `roles/storage.objectViewer` role on the bucket. (`grep -rn 'allUsers\|MakePublic\|PredefinedAcl' src/` returns zero hits.)
3. The publish path (PodcastPublisher.cs:85–130) uploads with the SA's credentials, which succeed because the SA has write access — but the resulting objects are accessible ONLY to authenticated callers with read permission. Anonymous callers (Apple Podcasts, browsers, RSS readers) get HTTP 403.

**Why the user CAN see XML files in the Cloud Console:**

Cloud Console uses the logged-in user's credentials, which have read access regardless of bucket IAM. The user sees ALL files in the bucket via Console, while Apple sees NONE via anonymous HTTP. So the user's report — "I see xml files but no audio files" — is potentially explained two ways:
- (a) The user IS authenticated via Console and sees BOTH xml and audio files, but is browsing only the top-level `podcasts/feed-index.json` (root) and `podcasts/{feedUuid}/feed.xml` paths — the `podcasts/{feedUuid}/episodes/*.m4b` files exist but live one level deeper and weren't expanded in the Console view.
- (b) PodcastPublisher.cs:79–83 silently skips uploads when `File.Exists(episode.LocalAudioFilePath)` returns false. If the local M4B path is wrong (e.g., temp directory cleanup ran early), every episode is skipped with a `LogWarning` that the user never sees, leaving feed.xml uploaded but no audio.

We cannot confirm (a) vs (b) from the audit alone — both fit the report. Both need to be addressed.

**Bug 2 (Apple "feed cannot be found"):** definitely the public-read IAM gap. Fix is to grant `allUsers:objectViewer` on the bucket.

**Bug 1 (missing audio files):** either a Console-navigation misread (educational) or a silent-skip on missing local file (real bug needing loud surfacing). Need diagnostics to disambiguate.

**Plan filed as sub-beads:**

- **workspace-TBD-1** (P0): Grant public-read IAM during bucket creation + add a self-test reading via anonymous HTTP after every publish.
- **workspace-TBD-2** (P0): Surface "audio file missing — episode skipped" loudly during publish (not just LogWarning); fail the run if all episodes get skipped.
- **workspace-TBD-3** (P1): Remediation path for existing private buckets — detect on next publish, offer one-keystroke "make public" or guide to Console.
- **workspace-TBD-4** (P1): User-facing diagnostics command (`:podcast-doctor`) that prints the current bucket's IAM bindings, the feed URL's public-fetch status, and which (if any) episodes are missing.

**Linked existing work:**
- workspace-3a2k (Phase E of rn4j umbrella) — feed-reachability HTTP GET as the last step before "success." This is the SAME diagnostic the user needs RIGHT NOW; fast-tracking that bead would have caught this in the wild.
notes:
**Diagnosis CORRECTED (2026-05-15 after live test):**

Bucket IS publicly accessible — verified by curl of https://storage.googleapis.com/tr_list_reader/podcasts/feed-index.json → HTTP 200 + JSON content, and the actual feed.xml URL also returns HTTP 200 + RSS content.

**Actual root cause:** the feed.xml has `<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-16"?>` in its declaration. PodcastFeedGenerator.cs:65 writes the document via StringWriter, whose Encoding property defaults to UTF-16. Even though we asked for UTF-8 in XDeclaration line 62, XDocument.Save honours the writer's Encoding and overwrites the declaration to utf-16. GcsStorageClient.UploadStringAsync then sends UTF-8 bytes — so the bytes are UTF-8 but the prolog lies. Apple Podcasts (and most strict RSS parsers) reject this with 'feed cannot be found.'

**Second observation:** the bucket's feed.xml has ZERO `<item>` elements, only channel metadata. This is the BootstrapFeedAsync output (called by PodcastGcsWizard.ValidateAndBootstrapBucketAsync during bucket setup). The user has likely never had a successful Generate Podcast run — only bucket setup. Explains 'no audio files in the bucket': they were never produced.

**Fix beads:**
- workspace-3pne (NEW, P0): Fix UTF-8 encoding in PodcastFeedGenerator.
- workspace-nb6b (UPDATED): Keep parseability check post-publish; drop the IAM auto-grant assumption.
- workspace-mie2 (UPDATED): The 'silent skip' code path still bites when episodes ARE generated but audio paths break. Keep the loud-surfacing fix; recategorize away from THIS user's symptom.
close_reason: Root cause was the UTF-16 encoding declaration (now fixed in workspace-jc2v). Confirmed live: new feed.xml at gs://tr_list_reader/podcasts/.../feed.xml declares encoding="utf-8", contains 1 valid <item> with playable audio enclosure, parses cleanly. Bucket was already publicly readable — IAM auto-grant scope dropped from workspace-nb6b. Original user-visible 'feed cannot be found' will now resolve on the next publish.

title: GCS setup flow: previous fix was a terrible failure — start over with a real beginner-first wizard
description:
Marking workspace-cgnt's fix (commit 21651a8) an absolute failure on every criterion the user gave.

WHAT THE LIVE SCREEN ACTUALLY SHOWS (reproduced via tmux harness 2026-05-09 on commit 5b0253d):

```
  ╭─ GCS Service Account  ───────────────────────────────────╮
  │ Paste your service account JSON below.                   │
  ╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
[ NINE BLANK LINES ]
  Paste your GCP service account JSON here (the full file contents starting with { and ending with }
  Find or create one at console.cloud.google.com/iam-admin/ser
  ╭──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
  │ test123                                                  │
  ╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
  JSON starts with { · paths support ~/ for your home di
```

WHY THE FIX FAILED ON EVERY CRITERION THE USER GAVE:

1. **'Text input is in the wrong place' (failure)** — the agent read this as 'cursor lands on the border column' and fixed inputCol 2→4. The ACTUAL wrong-place is the giant 9-line vertical void between the header card at the top and the input down at row ~14. The user's eye lands on the header, then has to hunt downward for the field. The agent introduced this new bug while 'fixing' a different one.

2. **'Super unclear what the field actually wants' (failure)** — three overlapping/redundant labels: card title 'GCS Service Account', card body 'Paste your service account JSON below', label 'Paste your GCP service account JSON here (the full file contents starting with { and ending with}' (TRUNCATED), and a hint 'JSON starts with { · paths support ~/ for your home di' (TRUNCATED). All three say the same thing badly, two are cut off mid-sentence. Subtitle 'Find or create one at console.cloud.google.com/iam-admin/ser' is cut off mid-URL — the user can't even copy the link.

3. **'Assumes knowledge of GCS that the user may not have' (failure)** — copy says 'service account JSON' / 'GCP service account' / 'console.cloud.google.com/iam-admin/serviceaccounts' with zero on-ramp. A user who has never opened Google Cloud Console has no idea what any of those words mean. There is no 'do you have a Google Cloud account?' gate, no plain-language explanation of what a service account is, no prompt to sign up if they don't have one. The previous fix's verbatim copy ('Find or create one at console.cloud.google.com/iam-admin/serviceaccounts → your service account → Keys → Add Key → JSON') reads as expert hieroglyphics to a beginner.

4. **No width fit-testing.** New copy strings are ~95-100 chars and get truncated on a 100-col terminal — a width that is not extreme. Nobody resized to 80 cols, nobody counted columns, nobody asserted 'no string exceeds visible width'.

5. **The QA close criterion was 'borders survive typing'.** That's necessary but pathetically insufficient. It said nothing about copy fitting, copy readability, beginner-friendliness, or layout coherence. The QA enforcer rubber-stamped a screenshot that contained THREE lines of obviously-truncated copy.

6. **The history review saw all 14 prior failures and the new attempt still recapitulated them.** The pattern is: a fix targets one narrow symptom (cursor column, paste-acceptance, persistence, the wrong probe), ships with a unit test of an internal helper, screenshots a partial happy state, closes. Nobody runs the flow from a blank fresh install with a fresh non-technical user persona.

WHAT THE NEW PLAN MUST DO:

A. **Beginner-first wizard.** Step 0: 'Have you used Google Cloud before?' [y]es / [n]o. If no, show a short plain-language explanation of what GCS is and what a service account key is, with a pointer to a one-page setup guide (could be a markdown file the app can open, or a URL). If yes, jump straight to paste.

B. **Layout coherence.** Eliminate the 9-line void. Either drop the header card entirely (the active wizard step is the header) or place the input ADJACENT to the card. Probably move to a single-column form where prompt → tip → input flow vertically with no gap.

C. **Width-aware copy.** All strings fit min(terminalWidth, 80) cols minus indent. Wrap or shorten — never truncate. Add a runtime check or unit test: 'every visible string in the wizard has length <= effective width.'

D. **Real verification close criteria.** Author MUST run termtest at widths 80, 100, 120, 140 and paste each screenshot in the close note. Author MUST walk a synthetic 'never-touched-GCS' persona through the flow and produce a written log of 'where I got stuck.' Author MUST exercise the verify step with a real (or mocked-as-real) failure path including IAM-missing and BillingDisabled. None of those are met by 'borders survived typing.'

E. **Honest scope.** This is closer to a redesign than a tweak. Drop all assumptions inherited from prior fixes. Walk the flow from cold in a notebook before writing code.

This bead supersedes workspace-cgnt's claimed fix. The two should be linked.
design:
Inline-overlay redesign matching OpenAI baseline.

ROOT CAUSE:
HandleSetKey and HandleSetBucket call Console.Clear() then render their own wizard chrome at the top, forcing input to land 9-14 rows below. HandleSetApiKey (OpenAI) does NOT clear — it overlays the centered FormField onto the live Settings screen, keeping context visible.

PLAN:
1. Remove Console.Clear from HandleSetBucket (line ~794), HandleSetKey (~1381), ShowPrerequisiteGateAsync (~888), and the verify-rerun panel (~454).
2. Drop the wizard header card (RenderWizardStepHeader) for these flows — the active edit row + a centered FormField IS the wizard step.
3. HandleSetKey: same pattern as HandleSetApiKey — startRow = (WindowHeight/2)-3, fieldWidth = min(WindowWidth-6, 60), no clear, no header, no subtitle. Label fits 60-6=54 chars; HelpText fits ~54 chars; both width-asserted in tests.
4. HandleSetBucket: same pattern; subtitle removed (label is enough); ? overlay still works via OnExtraKey.
5. Beginner on-ramp: add OnExtraKey('?') to the SA-key field rendering a 12-line plain-language overlay (what GCS is, what a service account key is, signup link, where to download key, role to grant). Mirror the existing bucket overlay structure.
6. Prerequisite gate: replace Console.Clear screen with an inline status block rendered above the bucket field (or as an error-style FormField with a single 'A' to set service account / Esc to back).
7. Width-aware copy: wrap subtitle/label/help to min(terminalWidth, 80)-6; never truncate. Use a helper WrapToWidth.

New ConsoleClearFreeTest asserts the four call sites no longer use Console.Clear (or, more practically, an integration-style test that drives HandleSetKey/HandleSetBucket against a fake input handler and asserts the Settings header is still on screen after entry).
