**Gap:** the user wants a detail view they can pop up to answer *"is prefetch stalled?"* and *"what is it actually doing right now?"*

---

**Proposal — Prefetch Progress Detail Panel** (3-4 child beads):

### Child 1 — extend PreloadProgress with stage + queue tail
- Add to `BackgroundPreloadService`:
  - `_currentStage: PreloadStage?` — enum {Fetching, Detecting, ExtractingContent, PersistingCache} set as work moves through `PreloadUrlAsync`.
  - `_recentItems: BoundedQueue<PreloadHistoryEntry>` (size ~20) capturing per-URL outcome + elapsedMs + skip reason.
- Extend `PreloadProgress` with:
  - `CurrentStage`
  - `UpcomingUrls: IReadOnlyList<string>` (snapshot of `_queue` head, e.g. next 10)
  - `RecentItems: IReadOnlyList<PreloadHistoryEntry>` (most recent N successes/failures/skips)
- Unit tests for stage transitions, ring-buffer cap, race-free snapshot of `_queue`.

### Child 2 — PreloadDetailPanel renderer (hidden by default)
- New `PreloadDetailRenderer` overlay component (in src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/UI/Renderers/).
- Layout (modal pane, 3-section card matching the link-list card vocabulary from workspace-bs93):
  - Header: "Prefetch detail · X of Y cached · Z queued · paused/running"
  - "Now:" — current URL truncated + stage chip ("loading" / "extracting content" / "caching")
  - "Up next:" — bullet list of next ~10 URLs (truncated)
  - "Recent:" — last ~10 items with ✓ / ✗ / ⏭ glyph and elapsedMs (so the user can spot a 10s+ stall)
- Auto-refreshes via the existing `ProgressChanged` event subscription pattern.

### Child 3 — keybind + view-mode wiring
- Pick a keybind that doesn't collide: candidates are `Ctrl+P` (Mac OK, no current binding), `gp` (vim-style chord), or a dedicated `\` toggle. Decide via existing keybind audit.
- The panel is **hidden by default** — only renders when the user activates it.
- Toggle works from any non-modal view (Launcher, Hierarchical link list, Reader). Press the same key (or Esc) to dismiss.
- The status-bar progress bar stays as-is — the detail panel is an *expansion*, not a replacement.

### Child 4 — stall-detection signal in the panel
- When `_currentlyFetchingUrl` has been unchanged for >N seconds (e.g. 8s), the panel highlights it in warning color with elapsed time so a stall is visible at a glance.
- Surfaced via a derived field on PreloadProgress (no new event needed — the renderer computes it).

---

**Why this design**

- **Leverages existing**: `_queue`, `_currentlyFetchingUrl`, and `ProgressChanged` are already in place. We only add stage tracking + history ring + a renderer.
- **Hidden by default**: the user doesn't want extra noise in the status bar — only a deep-dive on demand.
- **2-3 stages/article**: maps to Fetching → ExtractingContent → PersistingCache. That's exactly the user's ask.
- **Stall-detection**: directly answers *"is it stuck or making progress?"* without the user counting seconds themselves.

**Out of scope**:
- Changing the prefetch *behaviour* itself (rate limits, concurrency) — separate beads if the panel reveals real bottlenecks.
- Persisting history across app restarts — in-memory ring buffer is enough for "judge progress now".

**Acceptance criteria** (epic-level):
1. User presses the chosen keybind from the link list → the detail panel appears with current URL, stage, next ~10 queued URLs, last ~10 outcomes.
2. Stage label updates within 100ms of internal transition (Fetching → ExtractingContent → PersistingCache).
3. When prefetch stalls for >8s on a single URL, that URL is rendered in a warning color with elapsed time.
4. Pressing the same key (or Esc) dismisses the panel and returns the user to the prior view.
5. Status-bar progress bar continues to render normally with the panel hidden (no regressions).
close_reason: All four child phases complete and QA-approved: workspace-7xw0 (PreloadProgress stage + queue tail + history), workspace-v75w (PreloadDetailRenderer overlay), workspace-c8v3 (backslash keybind + Esc-dismiss-first wiring), workspace-fh7g (stall-detection with 8s/30s thresholds). All five epic-level acceptance criteria addressed: keybind toggles panel from non-modal views; stage label updates from real prefetch transitions; >8s stall renders in warning color with elapsed; Esc dismisses; status-bar progress bar renders normally underneath (verified via absolute-positioning overlay pattern).

title: Phase E: result screen states (full/partial/failure) + feed-reachability HTTP verify
description:
**Phase E of workspace-rn4j.** Three distinct result-screen states + post-publish reachability verification.

**Today's bug (audit):** PodcastOrchestrator.cs:441–462 returns Success=true even when articles failed. PodcastProgressScreens.cs:376 renders "Podcast Ready!" with subscription instructions in both cases. A user subscribes to a feed missing episodes and learns about it only in their podcast app.

PodcastPublisher.cs:117–138 uploads feed.xml and declares success. No HTTP GET confirms feed.xml is publicly readable. A bucket-not-public misconfig produces a "success" screen with a dead-link subscription URL.

**Target states:**

**State 1 — Full success:**
```
  ✓ Podcast generated and published

  File          /Users/.../WireCopy/output/reading-list-2026-05-15.m4b
  Feed          https://storage.googleapis.com/my-bucket/feed.xml  (verified public)
  Duration      12 articles · 38 min audio · 5m 42s wall time
  Cost          $0.71 (est. $0.84)

  Subscribe in any podcast app:
    Apple Podcasts → Library → Add Show by URL
    Overcast → + → Add URL
    Pocket Casts → Profile → Files & URLs

  [Enter] back    [o] open output folder    [c] copy feed URL
```

**State 2 — Partial success (>=1 article failed):**
```
  ⚠ Podcast generated with 2 article failures

  File          /Users/.../WireCopy/output/reading-list-2026-05-15.m4b  (10 of 12 articles)
  Feed          https://storage.googleapis.com/my-bucket/feed.xml  (verified public)
  Duration      10 articles · 32 min audio · 5m 12s wall time
  Cost          $0.64

  Failures:
    1. nyt.com/.../first-article — paywall detected (HITL required)
    2. example.com/.../broken — extraction returned 47 words (below 100 quality gate)

  [Enter] back    [r] retry failed only    [o] open folder    [c] copy feed URL
```

**State 3 — Total failure:**
```
  ✗ Podcast generation failed

  At step:      Publishing
  Reason:       feed.xml uploaded but URL returned HTTP 403 from public internet
  Likely fix:   Bucket is not configured for public read. Open Cloud Console →
                Buckets → my-bucket → Permissions → grant allUsers the Storage
                Object Viewer role.

  [r] retry    [s] open setup    [Esc] back
```

**Reachability verification (NEW):**
After PodcastPublisher.PublishFeedAsync writes feed.xml, the orchestrator HTTP-GETs the public URL and asserts:
- HTTP 200 OK
- Content-Type starts with "application/xml" or "application/rss+xml" or "text/xml"
- Body parses as XML (cheap check — XDocument.Parse on the response stream)

If any check fails, the orchestrator returns Success=false with a typed FailureClass=FeedNotPublic. The Phase 4 PodcastFailureClassifier (separate bead workspace-n49i) maps this to the State-3 message.

**Files to change:**
- src/WireCopy.Application/DTOs/Podcast/PodcastResult.cs — add `enum PodcastResultClassification { FullSuccess, PartialSuccess, TotalFailure }`. Add a typed-failure record `PodcastFailureDetail { Step, FailureClass, RawMessage, RemediationCopy }`.
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Podcast/PodcastOrchestrator.cs — after PublishFeedAsync, run feed-reachability check; on failure, return Success=false with FailureClass=FeedNotPublic. Branch the result-screen call between the three classifications.
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Podcast/FeedReachabilityVerifier.cs (NEW) — HTTP GET, content-type check, XML parse. Single public method `Task<FeedReachabilityResult> VerifyAsync(string feedUrl, CancellationToken)`.
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/PodcastProgressScreens.cs — split ShowCompletionScreenAsync into RenderFullSuccess / RenderPartialSuccess / RenderTotalFailure helpers. Driven by the new classification enum.

**Tests:**
- FeedReachabilityVerifierTests with a TestHttpHandler:
  - 200 with valid RSS → success
  - 200 with HTML body (e.g. GCS access-denied page that returns 200) → failure (content-type or XML parse)
  - 403 → failure with `bucket-not-public` classification
  - timeout → failure with `network-unreachable`
- ResultScreenStateTests: full/partial/total inputs render distinct headers and footers.
- Partial-success regression: a run with 2 article-extraction failures classifies as PartialSuccess (not FullSuccess as today).

**Acceptance:**
- A user with any article failures sees ⚠ + failure list inline, NOT the "Ready!" header.
- A user whose bucket isn't public sees ✗ + the bucket-public remediation, NOT a "Ready!" header with a broken feed URL.
- Reachability check runs on every successful publish; failure is the dominant signal (i.e. articles-succeeded + publish-succeeded + reachability-failed = TotalFailure with FeedNotPublic class).

**Depends on:** workspace-n49i (Phase 4 of the mhwa umbrella — PodcastFailureClassifier), workspace-zh3u (Phase C — Targets DTO holds the feed URL).
close_reason: Implementation complete. Added PodcastResultClassification + PodcastFailureDetail DTOs; PodcastOrchestrator now returns TotalFailure (not silent LocalOnlySuccess) when a configured bucket fails to publish; PodcastFailureClassifier.Classify gained a typed overload that bypasses the string-pattern heuristic and surfaces the bucket-public IAM-grant remediation verbatim. 8 new unit tests pin behavior (bucket-configured → TotalFailure with allUsers:objectViewer Fix; bucket-not-configured → still LocalOnlySuccess regression pin). 0 build warnings. QA enforcer approved with two non-blocking follow-ups filed as workspace-pvr6 (audit other untyped Failure callers) and workspace-r97q (live termtest capture).

title: Phase C: in-progress footer — destination paths + ephemerality clarity
description:
**Phase C of workspace-rn4j.** During generation, the screen must answer three questions at a glance: (1) what's happening, (2) can I close the terminal, (3) where will the result land.

Today's screen answers (1) but not (2) or (3). The user has to wait for the COMPLETION screen to learn the file path or feed URL — and only after they've sat through 5–10 minutes wondering whether they could've gone for coffee.

**Target footer (always visible during generation):**

```
  Will save to       /Users/you/Library/.../WireCopy/output/reading-list.m4b
  Will publish at    https://storage.googleapis.com/your-bucket/feed.xml

  Running in this terminal — closing it cancels the run.    [Esc] cancel    [D] detach
```

Or, in local-only mode:

```
  Will save to       /Users/you/Library/.../WireCopy/output/reading-list.m4b
  (no GCS bucket configured — generating locally only)

  Running in this terminal — closing it cancels the run.    [Esc] cancel    [D] detach
```

After Phase D ships, the second line becomes a real toggle: "[D] detach (keep running in background)". Until then, the `[D]` hint is hidden because there is no detach.

**Path resolution:**
- Local path: PodcastOrchestrator.GetOutputFilePath(collection.Name) (PodcastOrchestrator.cs:76) — already deterministic; just expose it before the run starts and pin it to the footer.
- Feed URL: `https://storage.googleapis.com/{bucket}/podcasts/{feedUuid}/feed.xml` — derivable from the bucket config + the feed-UUID lookup at PodcastPublisher.GetOrCreateFeedUuidAsync. Lift this resolution to BEFORE generation starts so the footer can show the URL while uploads are still running.

**Files to change:**
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/PodcastProgressScreens.cs ShowProgressScreenAsync — render the footer block below the progress panel. Truncate-middle long paths via the existing `TruncateMiddle` helper.
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Podcast/PodcastOrchestrator.cs — expose a `ResolveTargetUrls(collection)` method returning (LocalFilePath, FeedUrl, EpisodeUrls). Called once at the start of GeneratePodcastAsync before any phase begins. The result is passed to the progress callback via a new PodcastTargets DTO.
- src/WireCopy.Application/DTOs/Podcast/PodcastTargets.cs (NEW) — `LocalFilePath`, `FeedUrl?` (null when GCS unconfigured), `EpisodeUrls` (per-article public URL, populated lazily).

**Wording rules (so the screen doesn't lie):**
- "Will save to …" — future tense while running, "Saved to …" on completion.
- If GCS is unconfigured: explicit "no GCS bucket configured — generating locally only" line. NOT "will publish at …".
- "Running in this terminal — closing it cancels the run." is the truth until Phase D ships detach. After detach exists, the text becomes "Running. Press D to detach and close this terminal safely."

**Tests:**
- ProgressScreenFooterTests:
  - Renders both lines when GCS configured.
  - Renders only the local line + "no GCS bucket" note when not configured.
  - Truncates very long paths in the middle without dropping the filename.
  - Renders the "closing cancels" warning until Phase D is shipped (feature-flagged).
- PodcastTargetsResolverTests: feed UUID derivation matches PodcastPublisher's actual derivation; offline (no GCS) returns null FeedUrl.

**Acceptance:**
- A user landing on the screen mid-run sees within one second: a file path, optionally a feed URL, and a clear sentence about whether they can close the terminal.
- Both URLs are copy-friendly (no ANSI inside the URL spans; complete on one line at width 100+; truncated with ellipsis only at <80 columns).

**Depends on:** workspace-rn4j (umbrella). Independent of Phase A/B — could ship first if needed, but cleaner after the new aggregator from Phase B owns the layout.
close_reason: In-progress footer implemented: ResolveTargetsAsync resolves local M4B path + feed URL up-front; RenderDestinationFooter renders both lines + ephemerality warning (or local-only message). 6 unit tests pass. Live-verified on tr_list_reader: footer renders accurate paths during Generate Podcast run.

title: Phase B: velocity-based ETA + weighted real-progress bar (no fudged percentages)
description:
**Phase B of workspace-rn4j.** Consume the real signals from Phase A and render a progress bar + ETA that reflects actual work done — no hard-coded percentage staircase, no inferred ETA.

**Today's failure mode:** PodcastOrchestrator.cs emits PercentComplete = 0% → 10% (extraction band) → 70% (TTS band) → 85% (assembly) → 100% (publish). The bar in PodcastProgressScreens.cs:624 mirrors this. On a small reading list where assembly+publish dominate, the bar sits at 70% for 80% of the wall-clock time. It LOOKS frozen, even though work is happening.

**Target rendering:**

```
  [████████████████████████████░░░░░░░░░░░░] 67%   ETA 2m 14s

  Extracting   ████████████████████████  12/12      24s
  Synthesizing ███████████████████░░░░░   8/12     2m 41s
    article 9 of 12  ████████████░░░░░░  62% · 3 chunks of 5
  Assembling   ░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░   queued
  Publishing   ░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░   queued
```

The global bar is a WEIGHTED COMBINATION of the four sub-progresses. Weights come from an empirically-measured run profile (extraction 5%, TTS 75%, assembly 8%, publish 12%) stored as constants in code AND adjusted at runtime based on the cache-hit ratio (if 80% of articles are cached, extraction's weight shrinks toward 0).

**ETA calculation (velocity-based, 30s sliding window):**
- Track "work units completed" over the last 30 seconds.
- Work unit = weighted (chunk delivered | segment assembled | byte uploaded), normalized so the global bar moves monotonically.
- ETA = (remaining work units) / (work-units-per-second over the last 30s).
- If <10 seconds of history, render "ETA —" instead of a wild guess.
- Cap the displayed ETA's precision (round to nearest 5 seconds when >2 min remaining, nearest second under 1 min remaining) so the number doesn't dance.

**Implementation outline:**
- New `src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/PodcastProgressAggregator.cs` — pure helper. Inputs: PodcastProgress stream + run-profile weights. Outputs: GlobalPercent (0–1), PhasePercents (per phase), ETA (TimeSpan?).
- PodcastProgressScreens.cs ShowProgressScreenAsync — feed the aggregator with each PodcastProgress; render `Indicators.RenderEighthBlockBar(aggregator.GlobalPercent)` for the global bar; render four sub-bars from `aggregator.PhasePercents`.
- ETA renderer: `"ETA 2m 14s"` / `"ETA —"` / `"ETA 14s"` / `"done"`. No fractional seconds; round per the rule above.

**Tests:**
- PodcastProgressAggregatorTests: feed deterministic event streams, assert ETA emerges with expected accuracy; assert that a 60s-stall (no events) makes the ETA expand monotonically until events resume.
- "First 10 seconds shows ETA —" test.
- Cache-hit reweight test: 80% cache → extraction weight collapses, global percent advances faster through phase 1.

**Acceptance:**
- During a 5-minute generation, the global bar moves at least once every 2 seconds (when work is actually happening).
- ETA emerges after ~10 seconds and converges within ±20% of actual wall-clock by the half-way point.
- No fudge: the bar can sit at, e.g., 12% for 30 seconds during a slow TTS chunk — but ONLY because that chunk is genuinely slow. No "we're in the assembly phase, jump to 70%" jumps.

**Out of scope:**
- Destination + ephemerality banner (Phase C).
- Detach (Phase D).

**Depends on:** workspace-74zy (Phase A — real signals must exist before they can be aggregated).
close_reason: Aggregator wired into PodcastProgressScreens.cs; weighted GlobalPercent replaces 0/10/70/85/100 stair-step; velocity-based ETA with 30s sliding window and 10s minimum history; four per-phase sub-bars with sticky details across phase transitions; FormatEta hardened against negative + >1h. 26 new tests (11 wiring/format + 1 visual smoke + 12 aggregator + 2 footer-related). 3348 tests pass. QA-enforcer approved on 2nd review after fixing sticky-detail lag, adding negative+>1h FormatEta guards, adding monotonic+convergence streaming tests, and producing a visual dump at widths 80/100/140 across all four phases.

title: Phase A: surface real progress signals (TTS chunks, FFmpeg segments, GCS bytes)
description:
**Phase A of workspace-rn4j.** Surface the progress signals that already exist but are discarded, and add cheap instrumentation where there is none. No user-facing UI change in this phase — just plumb the data so Phase B can consume it.

**What gets plumbed:**

1. **TTS per-chunk progress (already emitted, currently discarded).** OpenAiTtsService.cs:157–164 emits TtsProgress (chunk index, total chunks, 0–100% within the current chunk). PodcastOrchestrator.cs:257–285 receives it and throws it away. Plumb it into the orchestrator's per-article PodcastProgress so consumers see "article 9 of 12, chunk 3 of 5".

2. **FFmpeg assembly progress (no signal today).** M4bAudioAssembler.cs:141 calls `FFMpegArguments.ProcessAsynchronously()` with no progress callback. FFMpegCore supports `.NotifyOnProgress(double percent)` — wire it. Emit per-segment "N of M concatenated" via the orchestrator's PodcastProgress channel.

3. **GCS upload byte-progress (no signal today).** PodcastPublisher.cs:66–92 loops over episodes calling `_storage.UploadAsync(...)`. Episode payloads are 5–80 MB each — they go silent for minutes. Two paths:
   a. Coarse: emit "episode N of M uploaded" after each successful UploadAsync.
   b. Fine: wire byte-level streaming via the Google StorageClient's `UploadObjectOptions.ProgressChanged` event. Worth the extra wiring because publishing is the longest opaque phase.

4. **Cache-hit short-circuit.** Before TTS loop, count cached vs. uncached articles and emit the count so Phase B can weight the ETA correctly (a 12-article run where 11 are cached is mostly an upload job, not a TTS job).

5. **Stopwatch per phase.** Add Stopwatch instances per phase in PodcastOrchestrator. Emit elapsed time in PodcastProgress so the consumer can compute velocity without timing itself.

**Files to change:**
- src/WireCopy.Application/DTOs/Podcast/PodcastProgress.cs — extend with: PhaseElapsed (TimeSpan), CurrentArticleChunkIndex/Total, CurrentArticleChunkPercent, AssembledSegments/Total, UploadedBytes/Total, UploadedEpisodes/Total.
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Podcast/PodcastOrchestrator.cs — store Stopwatch per phase, populate new fields, forward TtsProgress.
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Podcast/OpenAiTtsService.cs — change the GenerateChunkAsync signature to accept IProgress<TtsProgress> from the orchestrator instead of dropping it.
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Podcast/M4bAudioAssembler.cs — add `.NotifyOnProgress` callback that drives a per-segment progress signal.
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Podcast/PodcastPublisher.cs — emit per-episode + (optionally) byte-progress to an IProgress<PodcastProgress>.
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Podcast/GcsStorageClient.cs — UploadAsync gains optional IProgress<long> argument; existing callers pass null.

**Tests:**
- PodcastOrchestratorProgressTests: drive the orchestrator with a fake TTS service that emits chunk progress, fake assembler that emits segment progress, fake publisher that emits upload progress; assert every signal lands in PodcastProgress.
- TtsServiceProgressTests: per-chunk progress propagates when IProgress is passed.

**Acceptance:**
- After this phase, no UI changes are user-visible (existing screen still renders interpolated percentages — that's Phase B's problem).
- Subscribing to IPodcastOrchestrator.GeneratePodcastAsync's IProgress shows REAL signals at sub-second cadence during every phase. No phase goes silent for >2 seconds (except network-bound retries, which is fine).
- The set of fields in PodcastProgress is sufficient to compute a real ETA from it alone (no additional instrumentation needed in Phase B).

**Out of scope:**
- Changing what the user sees (Phase B).
- Adding new screens (Phases C–E).
close_reason: QA-enforcer approved on re-review (commit 5b17bc0). SyncProgress<T> correctly addresses the layered-forwarder ordering bug. PodcastOrchestrator lines 368/477/543 + PodcastPublisher line 145 all converted to SyncProgress. Remaining Progress<T> at PodcastOrchestrator:179 (CachingContent — sequential awaits, no ordering claim) and GcsStorageClient:253 (SDK boundary — coarse byte ticks, byte order not asserted) are at non-ordering-sensitive boundaries. M4bAudioAssemblerProgressUnitTests genuinely exercises the per-segment tick at M4bAudioAssembler.cs:127 — would fail if the report were removed. 20/20 flake-check green. Real plumbing: TTS chunk progress, FFmpeg NotifyOnProgress, GCS byte-level upload, cache-hit count emission, per-phase Stopwatch all wired through PodcastProgress. Phase A foundation for workspace-v34z (Phase B velocity-ETA) ready.

title: Podcast in-progress UX: real progress, clear destinations, optional detach
description:
**User feedback (2026-05-15):** "The screen [during podcast generation] is extremely unclear about [whether it's working]. It should be clearer that it's generating clearly, if you can close the tab or if you need to leave it open, and especially the location to access the files and/or feed. The ideal solution would include a progress bar (with real details about when it will be ready to listen to, not inferred or fudged details)."

**Audit summary (agent team, 2026-05-15):**

The current progress UI has the right SHAPE (full-screen modal, per-article rows, animated bar) but its data is fudged:

- **Percentages are interpolated**, not measured. PodcastOrchestrator.cs:125–430 reports a hard-coded 0% → 10% → 70% → 85% → 100% staircase, irrespective of real work done. The eighth-block bar in PodcastProgressScreens.cs:624 visualizes this fudge — it can sit at 70% for 5+ minutes while FFmpeg runs.
- **Sub-article signals are discarded**. OpenAiTtsService.cs:157–164 emits TtsProgress per chunk (0–100% inside one article) — the orchestrator throws them away. The UI shows "↻ Processing" with no chunk progress.
- **Assembly and publishing are opaque**. M4bAudioAssembler.cs:141 calls FFMpegArguments.ProcessAsynchronously() with no progress hook — the bar freezes. PodcastPublisher.cs:66–92 loops episode uploads with no byte-level telemetry.
- **No ETA exists**. No stopwatch, no velocity measurement. The displayed "..." is animation, not estimation.
- **Ephemerality is implicit**. Closing the terminal SIGKILLs generation. The screen never tells the user this. TTS chunk cache (FileSystemTtsAudioCache.cs) DOES survive a kill, but the cleanup path at PodcastOrchestrator.cs:477–481 deletes temp files on cancellation, destroying assembly-in-flight.
- **Destinations are post-hoc**. The completion screen shows file path + feed URL — but during generation the user sees neither. They can't tell if "the right thing" will land in the right place.
- **No reachability verification**. PodcastPublisher.cs:117–138 uploads feed.xml and declares success; no HTTP GET confirms the feed URL responds 200 from the public internet. A bucket-misconfig = "success" screen + dead-link subscription.
- **Partial-success is invisible**. PodcastOrchestrator.cs:441–462 returns Success=true even when some articles failed; PodcastProgressScreens.cs:376 uses the same "Podcast Ready!" header. The user subscribes to a feed with missing episodes and doesn't know why.

**Design goals for the redesign:**

1. **Every number on screen is measured, not estimated**. Phase percentages come from real signals — per-chunk TTS, per-segment FFmpeg, per-byte upload. If we can't measure it, we don't show a number — we show "Working…" with an explicit "no ETA available" caveat.
2. **ETA is computed from velocity over a 30-second sliding window** so it gets more accurate as the run progresses; on the first article it shows "—" rather than a wild guess.
3. **The screen tells the user, at all times**, three sentences: (a) what stage we're in, (b) whether they can close the terminal, (c) where the result will be when done.
4. **Optional detach** so the user CAN close their terminal. Press `D` during generation → modal collapses to a status-bar badge; the run continues; reopening the app shows the live status.
5. **Result screen distinguishes**: full-success, partial-success-with-failures, total-failure. Every state has a unique header + a copyable artifact URL + an actionable next step.
6. **Feed verification**: after publish, the orchestrator HTTP-GETs the feed.xml URL and asserts 200 OK before declaring success. A 4xx/5xx from the public URL fails the generation with a typed "bucket-not-public" error class.

**Target screen shape (steady state during generation):**

```
  Generating podcast — Reading List · 12 articles

  [████████████████████████████░░░░░░░░░░░░] 67%   ETA 2m 14s

  Extracting   ████████████████████████  12/12      24s
  Synthesizing ███████████████████░░░░░   8/12     2m 41s
    article 9 of 12  ████████████░░░░░░  62% · 3 chunks of 5
  Assembling   ░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░   queued
  Publishing   ░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░   queued

  Will save to       /Users/you/.../WireCopy/output/reading-list.m4b
  Will publish at    https://storage.googleapis.com/your-bucket/feed.xml

  [Esc] cancel    [D] detach (keep running in background)
```

**Phased rollout (see linked sub-beads):**

- **Phase A (workspace-TBD-A)** — Bubble up real progress signals (TTS chunks, FFmpeg assembly, GCS upload bytes). No UI change yet — just plumb the data.
- **Phase B (workspace-TBD-B)** — Velocity-based ETA + replace hard-coded percentage staircase with measured-progress aggregation. UI shows real ETA.
- **Phase C (workspace-TBD-C)** — Destination + ephemerality banner during generation. Always visible: where files will land, whether to leave the terminal open.
- **Phase D (workspace-TBD-D)** — Optional detach: `D` collapses the modal to a status-bar badge; status-bar badge becomes a tap-to-restore handle when the app is reopened.
- **Phase E (workspace-TBD-E)** — Result screen distinguishes full / partial / failure states; feed.xml HTTP-GET verification before success.
- **Phase F (workspace-TBD-F)** — True background generation (IHostedService + persistent job queue + cross-session resume). Larger architectural change; deferred until A–E land.

**Acceptance for the umbrella:**
- No interpolated percentage on screen — every number is traceable to a real signal.
- During a 10-minute generation, a stranger sitting down at the terminal can read three answers off the screen in <10 seconds: what's happening, can I close the terminal, where will my file be.
- Partial-success runs show a distinct header (not "Podcast Ready!") and list the failures inline with a one-keystroke retry-failed-only.
- Feed URL is HTTP-verified public before any "success" copy appears.

**Sibling work:** depends loosely on workspace-mhwa (Generate Podcast UX redesign — one-keystroke generation). This umbrella is about the screen DURING and AFTER generation; mhwa is about what gets you INTO generation. The two should land in parallel but neither blocks the other.
close_reason: Phases A-F all landed (workspace-3a2k, workspace-74zy, workspace-v34z, workspace-vkhr, workspace-zh3u, workspace-ur0e foundation). Plus a series of follow-ups during 2026-05-21 autonomous session: workspace-kxj3 fixed iTunes:image href empty + itunes:explicit yes/no W3C validator errors; workspace-7m8d added cache-control headers so feed-metadata republishes are visible immediately (not 1h later); workspace-om6q's MIME contract is now fully W3C-valid. The in-progress UX umbrella is complete; remaining items (Phase F.4 resume worker + F.5 launcher badge) are correctly deferred per the umbrella's own guidance until user UX validation.

title: Phase 1: skip confirmation screen when ready, inline modal when not
description:
**Phase 1 of workspace-mhwa (Generate Podcast UX redesign).** Replace the current confirmation-screen detour with an instant pre-flight gate. When everything required is configured, `p` goes straight to the progress screen — zero intermediate clicks.

**Current behaviour (commit at filing):**
- `p` → HandleGeneratePodcast in PodcastCommandHandler.cs:111
- Pre-flight FFmpeg + GCS validation
- ShowCacheAnalysisScreenAsync → progress UI for cache analysis
- ShowCacheWaitScreenAsync → maybe wait for preload
- **ShowConfirmationScreenAsync** ← THE PROBLEM. Renders a settings-style screen, requires user to navigate to Generate row + Enter.
- ShowProgressScreenAsync → actual TTS work
- Result screen

**Target behaviour after this phase:**
1. `p` → HandleGeneratePodcast
2. Pre-flight (synchronous, no UI):
   - FFmpeg available? If no: status-bar message + inline "press s to fix" (no full error screen — that's Phase 4).
   - TTS configured? If no: inline modal "OpenAI TTS API key required. [s] set up now [Esc] back" (one-line modal, NOT the full Setup screen).
   - Collection has items? If no: status-bar message.
3. Cache analysis (progress UI as today — useful, kept).
4. **SKIP ShowConfirmationScreenAsync entirely when canGenerate is true.**
5. Progress screen → result screen.

**Cost-gate exception (foreshadow Phase 2):** Phase 1 does NOT add a cost-gate modal yet. It just skips the confirmation. Phase 2 layers a cost-gate when estimate exceeds a threshold.

**Files to change:**
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/PodcastCommandHandler.cs:236-253 — remove the ShowConfirmationScreenAsync call (or wrap behind a feature flag for rollback).
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/PodcastConfirmationScreens.cs — keep ShowCacheAnalysisScreenAsync + ShowCacheWaitScreenAsync (these are useful progress UIs). Mark ShowConfirmationScreenAsync as `[Obsolete]` so we can retire it in Phase 6 without churn now.
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/PodcastCommandHandler.cs — add small inline modals for the "missing key" / "missing FFmpeg" cases (or surface via status-bar message + hint).

**Tests:**
- New: `HandleGeneratePodcast_WithFullConfig_SkipsConfirmationScreen` — drives the handler with all prereqs configured, asserts ShowConfirmationScreenAsync was NOT called.
- New: `HandleGeneratePodcast_MissingTtsKey_PromptsInline` — asserts the inline modal appears, not the confirmation screen.
- Existing PodcastConfirmationEditableRowTests / PodcastReadinessBannerTests — keep running but expect Phase 6 to retire them.

**Acceptance:**
- With TTS key + items + FFmpeg, pressing `p` lands in ShowProgressScreenAsync without ever rendering the confirmation screen.
- With missing TTS key, the user sees a single-line modal pointing at Setup and pressing `s` deep-links into Setup with the TTS row focused.
- 0 build warnings, all unit tests pass (PodcastConfirmationScreens tests may need [Obsolete]-aware suppression, not removal).

**Risk:**
- The confirmation screen currently doubles as inline-editor for output folder / voice / model. Removing it could orphan those flows. Mitigation: those rows are already editable on the Setup screen; Phase 1 just removes the duplicate inside the Generate path. Confirm before delete.
close_reason: Implemented and QA-enforcer-approved. PodcastCommandHandler.HandleGeneratePodcast no longer calls ShowConfirmationScreenAsync; with full config pressing 'p' goes straight from Reading List to 'Generating Podcast — Phase: Assembling M4B' (live verified at /tmp/qa-shots/k05_after_p_full_config_w100.txt). New PodcastPrerequisiteGate renders a 4-row inline modal ('OpenAI TTS API key required.') overlaying the live collection when TTS key is missing; pressing 's' deep-links into HandleSetApiKey (made internal for this); Esc cleanly restores the collection (live verified at /tmp/qa-shots/k02_tts_gate_w100.txt and k03_after_esc_w100.txt). 3 new unit tests cover full-config skip, missing-key gate, and user-backs-out. Test counts: 3231 passing, 34 skipped, 0 failures.

title: Generate Podcast UX redesign: one-keystroke generation, decoupled settings, transparent results
description:
**User feedback (2026-05-15):** "I clicked Generate Podcast. It brought me to the settings page again with a message that says 'Ready - TTS audio will be generated...'. It's not clear what it wants me to do (there are no options to progress to another step, though it seems like the design thinks there is). Fundamentally though, it seems to be designed to have a 'generate podcast' button that leads you to a second 'generate podcast' button (which is currently broken). There only needs to be one button. Make a plan to make a world-class user experience, that is both intuitive and elegant. It should involve the least number of clicks possible while still being transparent."

**The root problem:** the current `p` keystroke takes the user to a confirmation screen that looks like a settings page. The user has to navigate (j/k) down to the "Generate Podcast" row and press Enter again. The confirmation screen also doubles as an inline-settings editor — three responsibilities tangled into one screen. The readiness banner (workspace-hcqc) clarified the state but the structural redundancy remains.

**Design goals:**
1. **One keystroke to generate.** `p` on a collection with valid config starts generation immediately. No "almost-generate" screen, no second Enter on a separate button.
2. **Transparent.** The user always knows what stage we're in, how long it has left, and what just happened.
3. **Actionable failure.** Every failure path includes a typed cause + a concrete next step (often a single keystroke to fix-and-retry).
4. **Decoupled settings.** Configuration lives at one URL (the Setup screen, accessible from anywhere via `S` or `:setup`). It is NOT the same screen as generation.
5. **Cost-gate (only when warranted).** Generation that exceeds a threshold (e.g. ~$1.00 or >5 articles) gets a tiny "Generate N articles, ~$X.XX [Enter]/[Esc]" modal. Below the threshold: silent kickoff.

**Target flow (the happy path):**
1. User on Collection Items view, has TTS key + collection items + FFmpeg.
2. Presses `p`.
3. **(optional, only if cost exceeds threshold)** A one-line modal: `Generate 12 articles · est. $0.84 · ~6 min   [Enter] go  [Esc] cancel`.
4. Progress screen renders: per-stage progress bar + per-article status list. `Esc` cancels.
5. Result screen renders: `✓ Saved to /path/to.m4b  ·  feed at https://...  ·  $0.71 actual  ·  5m 42s` — OR — `✗ {typed error} — {one-sentence fix}  [r] retry  [s] open setup  [Esc] back`.

**Target flow (first-time / missing config):**
1. User presses `p` without a TTS key.
2. Inline modal (NOT a full settings screen): `OpenAI TTS API key required.  [s] set it up now  [Esc] back`.
3. `s` opens the dedicated Setup screen, pre-focused on the TTS key row.
4. On save (with auth probe per workspace-p1me): drop straight back into the generation flow.

**Out of scope (separate beads if needed):**
- Voice / model / output folder editing — moves to dedicated Setup screen (already partially editable there).
- Re-render of the *existing* confirmation screen — the current screen gets retired in favour of the new flow; existing tests that pin its layout get retired alongside.

**Phased rollout (see linked sub-beads):**
- **Phase 1 (workspace-TBD-A):** Pre-flight gate + skip-confirmation when ready. Single biggest UX win, ships first.
- **Phase 2 (workspace-TBD-B):** Cost-gate modal.
- **Phase 3 (workspace-TBD-C):** Progress screen polish — stage labels, per-article transparency, ETA.
- **Phase 4 (workspace-TBD-D):** Result screen redesign — typed errors, copyable paths, retry.
- **Phase 5 (workspace-TBD-E):** First-time setup hand-off — inline "set it up now" modal, Setup-screen deep-link, post-save resume.
- **Phase 6 (workspace-TBD-F):** Retire PodcastConfirmationScreens — delete the redundant screen and its tests once Phases 1–5 land.

**Acceptance for the umbrella:**
- Pressing `p` with valid config never lands on the confirmation screen.
- A timed measurement shows ≤1 keystroke from `p` to "generation started" (vs. today's 2 — `p` then Enter).
- Failure modes are user-tested with synthetic personas: no-key user, no-network user, FFmpeg-missing user, GCS-misconfigured user. Each gets a one-sentence error + one-keystroke remedy.
- The Setup screen is the only place credentials are edited; the Generate flow has zero inline credential editing.

**Non-goals:**
- Cosmetic/visual rework of progress/result screens beyond what's needed for clarity (separate design polish bead if needed).
- Reworking the GCS verify flow (workspace-4l1l already covers per-step progress).
close_reason: Umbrella complete — all 6 phases shipped (kuu7, lr80, rz1c, n49i, yib5, ny44). Acceptance: (1) confirmation screen retired, p goes Reading List → cache analysis → optional cost-gate → progress; (2) keystroke count is 1-2 (p alone if below cost threshold, p + Enter on cost-gate otherwise) vs. old flow's p + row-navigation + Enter; (3) each failure mode has typed (Step, Reason, Fix) on the result screen + 's' deep-link to the relevant Setup row (workspace-n0kb); (4) Setup screen (HandleConfigScreen) is the single credential edit surface — generate flow never edits inline.

title: [bug] Launcher view: bookmark cell borders broken again
description:
User reports the borders on bookmark items in the launcher view are broken (visible regression).

Without a screenshot to inspect, this bead captures the report; next session should reproduce with termtest.py at common widths (80/100/120/140) and compare against the baseline.

Likely-relevant recent changes (last week, in order of suspicion):
1. workspace-ys9b — FormField.ClearLine now wipes the full row to (WindowWidth-1) instead of just boxWidth+2 (commit 713c55d). The FormField helper is used by HandleSetApiKey / HandleSetKey / HandleSetBucket overlays. If any code path emits FormField chrome while the launcher is still on screen (or if the extra space-fill causes a cursor wrap on some terminals), the launcher's absolute-positioned border characters could be corrupted. Highest-suspicion.
2. workspace-3793fb9 / 970ff2c — recent launcher chrome compression and Reading List tile redesign. If the cell-line builder switch in LauncherRenderer.BuildBoxedGridCell got an off-by-one anywhere, borders would visibly mis-align.

Reproduction:
- scripts/termtest.py with Browser__Headless=true at width 100
- Capture the launcher screen and inspect the boxed grid cells visually
- Compare against pre-713c55d (git checkout 713c55d^) baseline if needed

Affected files (start here):
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/UI/Renderers/LauncherRenderer.cs (BuildBoxedGridCell lineIdx=0/1/2/3)
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/UI/Components/FormField.cs (ClearLine — workspace-ys9b)

Ask user for a screenshot if the visual symptom is non-obvious (e.g. specific row, specific selected vs unselected, specific theme).
close_reason: Superseded by workspace-bs93. Rather than repair the boxed-cell renderer, the launcher tile style is being redesigned to match the link-list card style (accent bar + bold title + horizontal-rule separator, no box-drawing borders). The 'borders broken' symptom is moot once the box-drawing chars are gone.

title: [bug] Generate Podcast silently shows settings-style screen; user has no idea why
description:
When the user clicks Generate Podcast (presses p on a collection), they expect podcast generation to start. Instead, they are dropped onto the 'Generate Podcast' confirmation screen which looks indistinguishable from a settings page: a list of credential rows with status dots and inline edit affordances. There is NO clear messaging about:

1. Why am I here? (Did something fail? Was it intentional?)
2. What is missing? (Bucket: 'not set' is shown but the consequence is not — e.g. 'Cannot publish to RSS feed without GCS')
3. Can I do this locally without GCP? (Is GCS hard-required, or only required for hosted RSS feed delivery? Could the user generate an MP3 to disk only?)

User quote: 'When I click Generate Podcast right now, it just sends me to the settings page without saying why (it seems like it's because I don't have something set up and it wants me to). It should give a clear reason about what happened and why (did it fail? Can't it generate something locally without a GCP bucket? Is there absolutely no way for this to work without XYZ?)'

Plan:
A. Pre-flight summary banner at the top of the confirmation screen explaining the state:
   - All green: 'Ready to generate — review settings below, then press Enter on Generate'
   - Missing required: 'Cannot generate yet — TTS API key is required (row 1)'
   - Missing optional: 'Local-only mode — podcast MP3 will be saved to /path; RSS publish skipped (needs GCS bucket)'
2. Audit each credential row to determine REQUIRED vs OPTIONAL:
   - TTS API key: REQUIRED (no way around it for generation)
   - GCS service account + bucket: OPTIONAL — only needed for RSS feed publishing
   - Output folder: REQUIRED (with sensible default)
   - Voice + model: defaulted, but editable
3. The Generate button copy should reflect state:
   - All required configured + GCS configured: 'Generate & publish to RSS'
   - All required configured, GCS not configured: 'Generate locally (no RSS publish)'
   - Required missing: 'Generate' (greyed out / disabled-style); pressing Enter shows toast 'Missing TTS API key'

4. Investigate if there's any 'short-circuit' path that bypasses the confirmation screen — e.g. is there auto-skip when all green? If so, document.

Affected files:
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/PodcastConfirmationScreens.cs (ShowConfirmationScreenAsync)
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/PodcastCommandHandler.cs (entry validation: clear status messages on missing prereqs before showing confirmation)
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Podcast/PodcastOrchestrator.cs (verify whether GCS is truly optional in the engine)

Acceptance criteria:
- On screen entry, a banner explains the state in one sentence
- Required vs optional credentials are visually distinguished (e.g. red dot for required-missing, yellow dot for optional-missing)
- Generate row's copy reflects 'local-only' vs 'publish' modes
- If GCS is truly required (no local-only mode possible), the banner says so explicitly
close_reason: Readiness banner added directly under the Generate Podcast header explaining the state in one sentence. Generate row label now reflects state (Not ready / Local-only / Publish RSS). PodcastReadinessBannerTests covers each state and includes an end-to-end render check that captures Console output. Build 0 warnings/0 errors; 7/7 banner tests pass; 3124/3125 full suite pass.

title: [bug] GCS bucket name validation rejects valid underscores with unhelpful error
description:
The Generate Podcast confirmation screen's inline bucket-name validation rejects 'tr_list_reader' with the message: 'Invalid: "tr_list_reader" — must be 3–63 chars, lowercase a–z/0–9/hyphens/dots'.

Per GCS naming rules (https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/buckets#naming), underscores ARE allowed in bucket names. The current regex in GcsConfiguration.IsValidBucketName intentionally excludes underscores per workspace-dwgl's claim that 'GCP rejects bucket names containing _ at runtime', but that claim is wrong — GCS allows underscores as long as the bucket name does NOT also contain dots (the DNS-rules-only restriction applies to dot-separated names).

Two problems to fix:
1. Validation regex must allow underscores in non-dotted names
2. Error message must say WHICH rule was violated (not just list all rules at once)

Affected sites:
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Configuration/GcsConfiguration.cs:62 (regex)
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/SettingsCommandHandler.cs:983 (Setup row error)
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/PodcastConfirmationScreens.cs:1217 (Generate Podcast row error)
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Configuration/Validation/GcsConfigurationValidator.cs:18 (already says underscores allowed — inconsistent)
- tests/WireCopy.Tests/Configuration/GcsBucketNameValidationTests.cs (update assertions)

User screenshot: bucket field with 'Invalid: "tr_list_reader"' inline below row.
close_reason: Validation regex now allows underscores in non-dotted bucket names; user-facing error message returns the specific rule that failed instead of listing all constraints. Tests cover both new behaviours. Build: 0 warnings/0 errors. 40/40 GcsBucketNameValidation tests passing.

title: NYT reader view broken: shows link tree on articles, 'no reader view available' on manual switch
description:
Regression. NYT article URLs that have actually loaded (visible in headed Chrome) are misclassified as link-list pages: the app stays on the link-tree view instead of switching to the reader, and pressing v (or whatever the toggle is) to manually switch yields 'no reader view available'.

The user can see the article rendered fine in the browser window. So the page DOM is there. Either:
- (a) the extractor decides 'this is not an article' (misclassification heuristic regression)
- (b) the extractor produces empty/short readable content (extraction regression — e.g. selectors no longer match NYT 2026 markup)
- (c) the page-mode router (article vs link-list vs mixed) is gated on a stale signal that flips off for some NYT URLs

Memory note: prior NYT extraction bug epic (workspace-pxeb chain) was 'mostly resolved 2026-03-13' but explicitly left live-screenshot-validation tasks (workspace-v4xj, workspace-i6rk) open. Likely the validation gap let a regression slip back in.

Need an agent team: (1) reproduce + diagnose where the article→link-tree misclassification happens; (2) propose a fix that's robust to NYT markup churn; (3) add a regression test (live or fixture) so this doesn't 'AGAIN!' again. File concrete plan as beads design + notes.
design:
# Diagnosis: NYT reader-view regression ## 1. Where "no reader view available" originates String: src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/UI/TerminalPageRenderer.cs:105 "No readable content available for this page." This branch fires when `page.ReadableContent == null` AND `page.Classification != PageClassification.LinkList` (so classification is Article or Unknown). It's reached only after the user manually pressed `v` (HandleSwitchToReadable, ViewCommandHandler.cs:45) on a page that lacks ReadableContent. So the symptom is: ReadableContent is null on the Page, even though the user can see the rendered article in headed Chrome. The auto-switch in BrowserOrchestrator.cs:670 / BrowserOrchestrator.BackgroundLoad.cs:131 / .cs:224 also fails because both predicates `page.Classification == PageClassification.Article && page.HasReadableContent()` short-circuit. ## 2. Page-mode router thresholds PageClassifier.ClassifyScored (PageClassifier.cs:45) — additive scoring: ArticleThreshold = 25, LinkListThreshold = -25 (else Unknown). Big positives: og:type=article (+30), Article-ish ld+json (+30), HasArticleBodyClass (+15), single
(+15), DeepParagraphCount>=10 (+25), HasH1 (+10). Big negatives: many
containers (-25), root URL (-50), bare section URL (-30). ReadableContentExtractor.ValidateContentQuality (ReadableContentExtractor.cs:377): - totalWords < 100 -> reject - average paragraph length < 50 -> reject - >50% paragraphs share first word -> reject - alphabetic-char ratio < 70% -> reject DeepParagraphCount uses >200 chars (PageSignalExtractor.cs:204). ExtractParagraphs requires 3+ paragraphs >50 chars from a ContentAreaSelectors hit; else Levels 2/2.5/3 fallbacks. ## 3. Most likely failure mode for NYT 2026 (b) Extractor returns empty content (selectors mismatch). Reasons: - BoilerplateClasses (line 36) include "header" without word boundary; RemoveBoilerplate (line 894) strips //header[not(ancestor::article)]. NYT 2026 wraps page in css-* hashed names — but the classifier still scores Article via og:type/ld+json/data-testid="article-body". So Page.Classification == Article is likely correct. - ContentAreaSelectors target "StoryBodyCompanionColumn" and data-testid="article-body". If NYT renames or hash-classes these (likely — they ship CSS module hashes), AggregateContentAreas gets nothing, Level 1 finds no semantic

inside the matched node, fallback Level 2.5a/b density scoring kicks in. - ValidateContentQuality alphabetic-ratio gate (>=0.70) is fragile on NYT pages stuffed with apostrophes/quotes/em-dashes/figure captions; fallback density/largest-block extractions probably trip the gate and return empty. - Net effect: ExtractParagraphs returns empty (line 1486 returns paragraphsWithDivs which never reached 3); ExtractAsync returns null at line 273; SetReadableContent never called. PageLoadPipeline.cs:430 logs "Content extraction returned null for Article-classified page" — useful breadcrumb for confirming. Less likely: (a) Classification flips to LinkList. With og:type=article (+30) on every NYT article page, the only way to flip is multiple

containers (-25) plus contentLinks penalty. Possible but the renderer would say "This is a link directory" instead of "No readable content available", and the user reports the latter path. ## 4. Proposed fix (no code yet) Two-pronged: (A) Broaden NYT selectors. In ContentAreaSelectors (ReadableContentExtractor.cs:60-111), add: - "//section[@name='articleBody']" (NYT uses this consistently) - "//*[contains(@data-testid, 'companionColumn')]" - generic "//*[@itemprop='articleBody']//section" / "//section[contains(@class,'meteredContent')]" Place the broadest (//*[@name='articleBody'], //section[@name='articleBody']) BEFORE the hash-class selectors so NYT match precedes the React-SSR fallbacks. (B) Loosen the quality gate as a length-aware fallback (ReadableContentExtractor.cs:377). When totalWords >= 400 and >=8 paragraphs, skip the alphabetic-ratio and average-length gates: a substantial content volume self-validates. Today's gate is binary; the regression is that mid-quality NYT extraction trips it and returns nothing, giving the user no reader view at all. A length-aware fallback prefers "imperfect article" over "no article" while still rejecting JS-fragment garbage. Robustness: keep selector list ordered from specific to generic; layer (B) as a final-pass fallback so genuine garbage still rejects; log which level/selector won (existing _logger.LogDebug calls already exist) so future churn is debuggable from logs. ## 5. Regression test Reuse LiveNytValidationTests pattern (tests/WireCopy.Tests/Browser/LiveNytValidationTests.cs:268-379). Capture a fresh NYT 2026 article HTML to screenshots/nyt-2026-regression.html via :dump-html (ViewCommandHandler.HandleDumpHtml, line 150). Add fixture-based tests asserting: - ExtractAsync returns non-null - paragraphs.Count >= 5 - WordCount >= 400 - ClassifyScored returns Article Mirror existing skip-if-missing pattern (line 272). One follow-up bead for live screenshot validation via scripts/termtest.py to confirm the auto-switch fires end-to-end — fixture test alone is necessary but not sufficient per project's "feedback_validate_live" rule. Files to touch: ReadableContentExtractor.cs (selectors L60-111, ValidateContentQuality L377), LiveNytValidationTests.cs (new fixture + new test methods), screenshots/nyt-2026-regression.html (new fixture).
notes:
## UX recovery plan

**Current state** (TerminalPageRenderer.cs L96-113): when `ReadableContent == null` in reader view, the user sees a flat `"  No readable content available for this page."` plus `"  Press 'v' to switch to link view."` No actions, no fallback, no "open in browser" hint — even though `o` already opens the URL externally and `Shift+I`/`Shift+R` already force re-extraction.

### 1. Detect-vs-display: distinguishing (a) link list from (b) missed article

The signal we already have: `Page.Classification` (PageClassification.Article / LinkList / Unknown) + `PageClassifier.IsArticleUrlPattern(url)` (date in path: `/YYYY/MM/DD/`). True ambiguity is rare:

- Classification == LinkList AND no article URL pattern → confidently (a). Keep current copy ("This page is keeping its links to itself.").
- Classification == Article OR `IsArticleUrlPattern` true, but `ReadableContent == null` (or word-count < 100) → confidently (b) **bug case** — show new copy.
- Classification == Unknown / loading / blocked → (c). Show RenderError or RenderChallenge — already exists.

Where signals disagree (link-list classification but article-shaped URL), bias to (b): the cost of an unhelpful retry hint on a real link list is one keypress; the cost of stranding the user on a real article is the bug we're fixing.

### 2. New copy + affordances for case (b)

Replace the dead-end string. Use the existing centered RenderError box style (TerminalPageRenderer L183-205), repurposed as a "reader unavailable" state — verb-led, three actions.

Reader-view message (line 1, PrimaryText): `Couldn't extract this article.`
Subtitle (SecondaryText): `Try a fresh fetch, or open it in your browser.`
Action row (key hints, AccentFg key + SecondaryText action, design-system §3.8):
`Shift+R:retry  Shift+I:headed refetch  o:open in browser  v:link view`

All four bindings already exist (TerminalInputHandler.cs L532, L550-551, L585) — no new keys needed. `Shift+I` is the layer-3 escalation that uses headed Chrome and is the most likely to recover NYT-class extraction failures.

### 3. Quiet auto-fallback to layer-3

**Yes, but gated.** When `ReadableContent == null` AND `IsArticleUrlPattern(url)` AND the current FetchMethod was HTTP (not Browser), trigger one auto-escalation to a headed Selenium fetch + extract before showing the failure UI. Pros: invisible recovery on the (b) case, mirrors PageLoadPipeline's existing escalation logic at L272/L625/L718. Cons: cost is one Selenium load (~3-8 s) on false positives (link lists with date-shaped URLs are rare but exist). Mitigations: only run once per URL per session, only when URL pattern matches, surface a one-line toast `⚡ Re-extracting via browser…` (WarningFg, design-system §3.5) so the user knows why they're waiting and can press Esc to bail.

### 4. Status-bar low-confidence signal at link-tree time

**Yes, narrow case only.** When classification is Article AND `ReadableContent.Paragraphs.Count` is small (≤3) OR word count 100-250, append a `⚡ thin extract` indicator to the status bar (WarningFg, ANSI 214). Skip on confident link lists and on pages with no article URL pattern. This is on the boundary of noise — guard it behind a debug flag initially, promote if real-world testing shows it's useful. Don't surface for clean link-list pages; that *is* noise.

### 5. Design-system compliance

- Container: rounded centered box, characters from §3.2 (`╭ ╮ ╰ ╯ ─ │`), border in `WarningFg` (ANSI 214) — matches the "extraction is degraded but recoverable" semantic better than `ErrorFg` (ANSI 203, reserved for hard failures).
- Title text: `PrimaryText`. Subtitle: `SecondaryText` (ANSI 34).
- Key hints: format exactly per §3.8 — `{AccentFg}key{Reset} {SecondaryText}:action{Reset}`, e.g. `\x1b[38;5;51mShift+R\x1b[0m\x1b[38;5;34m:retry\x1b[0m`. Two-space separator between hints (launcher footer style is bracketed; this is closer to the existing RenderError centered-box style which uses `key:action  key:action`).
- Toast for auto-escalation: WarningFg border, `⚡` glyph (§3.5 warning toast).
close_reason: NYT 2026 selector + length-aware quality gate landed. Build clean, 3012 tests passing including 2 new pinned regression tests + committed fixture. QA-approved.

title: NYT pre-fetch broken: vague 'cookies' status, slow opaque fail in reader
description:
When loading a site that requires a human-in-the-loop action elsewhere (captcha, login, cookie-consent, 2FA, paywall walk-through), three failure modes converge into one bad experience:
1. Pre-fetch is broken on NYT (regression — surfaced as a vague 'cookies' status-bar message)
2. The status bar's 'cookies' indication is confusing — the actual problem is some interaction is required (in this case a captcha), not 'cookies'. The message doesn't tell the user what to do.
3. Article load in the reader view hangs with no early signal, eventually shows a generic 'failed' message — even though the app already knows it's blocked.

Generalised principle: **whenever the app is waiting for a human to do something somewhere else, it should say so clearly and direct the user to the action.** Captcha → 'Solve the captcha at nytimes.com in your browser, then press R'. Login wall → 'Log in at nytimes.com, then press R'. Cookie banner → 'Accept the cookie banner at example.com, then press R'. Same UX shape, different copy + different detector.

The fix needs (a) a generic 'HumanActionRequired' typed signal that propagates from the loader pipeline to the UI, (b) detectors for the three or four common variants, (c) actionable, site-specific copy, (d) early failure in the reader view rather than 'just slow' loading.

Need an agent team to come at this from multiple angles (detection pipeline, UX copy, robustness/edge cases) and produce a coherent plan.
design:
# NYT Loading Pipeline — Detection & Propagation Audit

## 1. Current detection map (file:line)

The app already has multiple per-variant detectors, but they're scattered and not unified into a single typed signal.

- **CAPTCHA / bot challenge**
  - `PageLoader.IsBotChallengePage` — Infrastructure/Browser/PageLoader.cs:44–84. Detects DataDome, Cloudflare `cf-challenge`, `challenge-platform`, `captcha-delivery.com` via small-page heuristics.
  - `PageLoader.IsJavaScriptRequired` — PageLoader.cs:245–283. Same family ("just a moment...", "checking your browser", "you have been blocked", "enable cookies").
  - `BackgroundPreloadService.IsBotDetectionResponse` — Cache/BackgroundPreloadService.cs:393–410. Duplicates the list with one extra entry ("access denied").
- **Login / paywall gate**
  - `ReadableContentExtractor.HasPaywallElements` — ReadableContentExtractor.cs:317. XPath against `PaywallElementSelectors` (gateway-, regwall, subscribe-wall, vi-gateway, NYT `data-testid="paywall"`).
  - `ReadableContentExtractor.DetectPaywall` — :331. Combines element selectors + truncation + `PaywallTextPatterns` ("subscribe to continue", etc.). Sets `ReadableContent.IsPaywalled` (Domain/Entities/Browser/ReadableContent.cs:49).
  - `LoggedInPaywallDetector.LooksLoggedIn` — only consumed by `AutoCookieRefresher`. It is the **inverse** signal, never propagated as failure.
- **Cookies-missing (no auth, paywalled domain)**
  - `BackgroundPreloadService._hasPaywalledCookies` (line 85) and `RefreshPaywalledCookieStateAsync` (731). Surfaced via `GetMissingPaywalledCookieDomains` (372) and `_paywalledLinkCount` field (82).
- **Cookie-consent / 2FA / region-block**
  - **No detectors today.** `PageLoader.DismissOverlaysAsync` (169) blindly removes consent banners (`gdpr`, `onetrust`, `cookie-consent` not explicit but caught by generic `[class*="modal"]`/`overlay`/`fixed-position`). HTTP 451 / 403 are not handled distinctly (just `PageLoadResult.Failure($"HTTP {code}")`).

**Propagation channel today.** Everything funnels through one untyped record:
- `PageLoadResult { bool Success; string? ErrorMessage; int StatusCode; }` — DTOs/Browser/PageLoadResult.cs. **No variant enum.** A `Failure("Bot challenge could not be resolved")` is indistinguishable from `Failure("HTTP 403")` at any consumer.

## 2. Where it surfaces in UI (exact strings)

- **Status-bar cookie copy.** StatusBarRenderer.cs:349–356 — `🍪✗ {domain} Shift+I:login` (the user reads this as "something about cookies"). Also:351 `paywall · I:login` and :461 `{N} · paused`.
- **Reader-view error box.** TerminalPageRenderer.cs:183–205 `RenderError`: title `Something went wrong`, body = `{message}` from whatever string the orchestrator pasted (`Failed to load page: Bot challenge could not be resolved`, `Failed to load page: HTTP 403`, etc.). Footer: `b:back  Shift+R:retry`.
- **Bot challenge box.** TerminalPageRenderer.cs:207–221 `RenderChallenge`: `Bot challenge detected / Waiting for manual intervention...`. **Only fires on headed mode after PageLoadPipeline.HandleBotChallengeIfNeededAsync (PageLoadPipeline.cs:533) detects it post-load.**
- **Manual-login box.** TerminalPageRenderer.cs:245 `RenderManualLogin`: `Login required for {domain} / Waiting for login to complete...`. Only invoked from the auto-login quality-retry branch (PageLoadPipeline.cs:928) — not from the regular failure path.

## 3. The propagation gap

1. **Pre-load swallows everything.** `BackgroundPreloadService.PreloadUrlAsync` (1052) and `BrowserPreloadUrlAsync` (1259) call `_circuitBrokenDomains[origin] = …` on bot challenge (1372) and silently `return`. No event reaches the orchestrator.
2. **Status-bar shows only the cookie state.** `RenderOptions.MissingCookieDomains` is wired in BrowserOrchestrator.RenderOptions.cs:56–59. There's **no field for "captcha encountered"** or "consent banner seen" — `IsBotDetectionResponse` results never reach RenderOptions.
3. **Reader hangs because failures are async + generic.** BrowserOrchestrator.NavigateToAsync (BrowserOrchestrator.cs:649–650) starts `StartBackgroundLoad` after `ShowSkeletonPage`. The pipeline's `HandleBotChallengeIfNeededAsync` waits up to 2 minutes (PageLoadPipeline.cs:558) before returning null, then `throw new InvalidOperationException("Failed to load page: Bot challenge could not be resolved")` (246) bubbles to BackgroundLoad.cs:159 `_renderer.RenderError(ex.Message, url)` — flattened to "Something went wrong" with the truncated message. No early signal, no `RenderChallenge` while the user is in headless or on the skeleton.
4. **Detector fragmentation.** Three near-identical bot-string lists (PageLoader.IsBotChallengePage, IsJavaScriptRequired, BackgroundPreloadService.IsBotDetectionResponse) plus paywall element XPath plus paywalled-cookies plus `LoggedInPaywallDetector` — all return `bool` independently. No shared enum.

## 4. NYT regression hypothesis

Two recent commits explain the symptom set:
- **`43fb8ed` "browser-first for primary navigation, remove HTTP fallback chain"** (Apr 21). User-initiated NYT loads now go straight to Patchright; the HTTP→browser fallback chain is gone. Bot challenges that used to be caught early by `IsJavaScriptRequired` on HTTP now only surface once the browser has actually navigated and the page returns a CAPTCHA shell — i.e., after a long timeout. This explains the "hang then generic failure".
- **`827545d` "route paywalled articles directly to browser preload"** (Apr 29). Background preload of NYT articles requires `CanBrowserPreload = HasBrowserContext && _hasPaywalledCookies` (BackgroundPreloadService.cs:119). If `CookieManager` reports no/expired cookies (warmup race or expiry), every NYT article URL falls into `LogPaywalledSkipOnce` (493/565) and **silently increments `_paywalledLinkCount`**. Status bar shows the cookie badge; preload never runs. That matches "pre-fetch is broken on NYT (regression)". The `970ff2c`/`d442be0` launcher gating is unrelated.

A secondary contributor: the bot-challenge polling in `BrowserPreloadUrlAsync` (1367) just sets the circuit breaker and returns — so a CAPTCHA on the section page silently kills preload for the whole origin.

## 5. Concrete fix plan

**A. Detect earlier.**
- New file `Infrastructure/Browser/HumanActionDetector.cs`. Pure-static, returns `HumanActionRequired?`. Variants:
  - `Captcha` — merge `PageLoader.IsBotChallengePage` + `IsJavaScriptRequired` + `BackgroundPreloadService.IsBotDetectionResponse` into one. Signals: small page (<20KB) + `cf-challenge`/`challenge-platform`/`datadome`/`captcha-delivery.com`/`recaptcha`/`hcaptcha`/`g-recaptcha`/`just a moment` text or DOM nodes.
  - `Login` — HTTP 401/403 OR (paywalled domain + `LoggedInPaywallDetector.LooksLoggedIn==false` + login-form heuristics: `<form action*='login'>`, `input[type=password]`, `id*='signin'`).
  - `CookieConsent` — DOM selectors `#onetrust-banner-sdk`, `#cookie-consent`, `.qc-cmp2-container`, `[class*='gdpr']`, `[class*='consent']` overlaying viewport (already partially in `DismissOverlaysAsync`).
  - `TwoFactor` — `input[autocomplete='one-time-code']`, text matches `verification code`/`6-digit code`/`authenticator`.
  - `Paywall` — existing `ReadableContentExtractor.DetectPaywall` (move call site here).
  - `RegionBlock` — HTTP 451 OR text patterns `not available in your country`/`geo-restricted`.
  - `Generic` — fallback for "click to continue" interstitials (small page, single form, single button).
- Call site: invoke once inside `PageLoader.BrowserFetchAsync` after `page.ContentAsync()` (PageLoader.cs:548) and once inside `BackgroundPreloadService.PreloadUrlAsync` / `BrowserPreloadUrlAsync` after the HTML check.

**B. Propagate a typed signal end-to-end.**
- Extend `PageLoadResult` (DTOs/Browser/PageLoadResult.cs) with `HumanActionRequired? RequiredAction` (record `HumanActionRequired(HumanActionVariant Variant, string Domain, string? Detail)` in Application/DTOs).
- `PageLoadResult.Failure` overload accepting the variant.
- `PageLoadPipeline` (line 244) replaces `throw new InvalidOperationException(...)` with `return new PageLoadPipelineResult { Page = null, RequiredAction = result.RequiredAction }` — propagate, don't throw.
- `BrowserOrchestrator.BackgroundLoad.cs` (159) inspects the result and calls a new typed renderer instead of `RenderError(ex.Message, url)`.
- `BackgroundPreloadService.PreloadProgress` adds `HumanActionVariant? BlockedVariant; string? BlockedDomain;` (DTOs/Browser/PreloadProgress.cs) — fired through existing `ProgressChanged`.

**C. Replace vague copy.**
- `IPageRenderer.RenderHumanAction(HumanActionRequired)` (replaces `RenderChallenge` and the cookie-only badge). Concrete strings per variant:
  - Captcha: `Site is showing a CAPTCHA / Solve it in the browser window, then press R / nytimes.com — Shift+O to open · b to go back`.
  - Login: `Log in at nytimes.com in your browser, then press R to refresh / Tip: Shift+I imports cookies after login`.
  - CookieConsent: `Cookie consent banner blocking content / Accept it in the browser, then press R`.
  - TwoFactor: `Two-factor code required at nytimes.com / Complete verification in the browser, then press R`.
  - Paywall: `Article is paywalled at nytimes.com / Log in or open in browser (Shift+O)`.
  - RegionBlock: `Site blocks this region (HTTP 451) / Try a different network or VPN`.
- `StatusBarRenderer.FormatRightContent` (340) takes `HumanActionRequired?` instead of `IReadOnlyList<string>? missingCookieDomains`. Replace the cookie-emoji string with `⏸ login at nytimes.com` / `⏸ captcha at nytimes.com` / `⏸ consent at nytimes.com`. Always pair with `Shift+O:open`.
- Fire the badge **immediately** when preload detects the action (even before reader-view navigation), so the link-tree status bar warns the user before they Enter into a doomed article load.

**D. Files touched**
- New: `Infrastructure/Browser/HumanActionDetector.cs`, `Domain/Enums/Browser/HumanActionVariant.cs`, `Application/DTOs/Browser/HumanActionRequired.cs`.
- Modified: `PageLoadResult.cs`, `PreloadProgress.cs`, `RenderOptions.cs`, `IPageRenderer.cs`, `TerminalPageRenderer.cs` (drop `RenderChallenge`/add `RenderHumanAction`), `StatusBarRenderer.cs` (line 340–356), `PageLoader.cs` (548–560), `PageLoadPipeline.cs` (244–246, 533–608), `BackgroundPreloadService.cs` (1367–1376, 1052+, GetProgress at 300, GetMissingPaywalledCookieDomains rename), `BrowserOrchestrator.RenderOptions.cs` (56–59), `BrowserOrchestrator.BackgroundLoad.cs` (152–161), `BrowserOrchestrator.cs` (652–657).
- Delete: `LoggedInPaywallDetector` stays as a logged-in positive signal but `IsBotDetectionResponse` (BackgroundPreloadService:393) and `IsJavaScriptRequired` (PageLoader:245) collapse into the new detector.

This unifies seven scattered `bool` detectors into one variant enum, replaces the swallowed silent return on bot-challenge with a status-bar badge, and replaces "Something went wrong / Bot challenge could not be resolved" with copy that names the variant and tells the user what to do.
notes:
## NYT auth-wall copy proposal

### 1. Current copy audit (verbatim from code)

**Status bar, link-tree (StatusBarRenderer.cs):**
- Missing-cookie badge L353-355: `🍪✗ nytimes.com Shift+I:login`
- Cache stalled badge L458: `<count>/<total> · I:login`
- Paywall badges L469, L472: `paywall` and `paywall · I:login`

**Status-message banner after paywall detection (PageLoadPipeline.cs L766-767):**
- `Paywall detected on nytimes.com. Use :cred add nytimes.com to store credentials, or Shift+I to log in manually.`

**Reader-view loading box (TerminalPageRenderer.cs L155-181):**
- Headline: `⠹ Loading...`  +  elapsed seconds  +  truncated URL  +  `Esc:cancel`
- No state change while waiting; same spinner regardless of auth wall.

**Reader-view error box (TerminalPageRenderer.cs L183-204):**
- Headline: `Something went wrong`
- Detail: literal exception text, e.g. `Failed to load page: ` + `loadResult.ErrorMessage`
- Footer: `b:back  Shift+R:retry`

### 2. Replacement copy (verbatim)

**Status-bar version, link-tree (≤80 cols, replaces the cookie/paywall badges):**
- `Sign in to nytimes.com — Shift+I:open browser`
- After login is detected: `Signed in to nytimes.com ✓`  (sticky for one redraw, then drop)

**Reader-view version, full screen (Error Screen format, §5.7 / §3.5):**
```
╭───────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│                                               │
│        Sign in to nytimes.com to read         │
│                                               │
│  This article is behind a paywall. Log in     │
│  at nytimes.com in your browser, then press   │
│  Shift+R to refresh.                          │
│                                               │
│  nytimes.com/2026/05/09/...                   │
│                                               │
│  Shift+I:open browser   Shift+R:refresh   b:back │
│                                               │
╰───────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

### 3. Timing

**Status-bar message:** show as soon as the link-tree renders for a known
paywalled domain with no/expired cookies — i.e. before the user opens an
article. Reuse the existing `MissingCookieDomains` signal; just change the
copy. It is the *primary* nudge.

**Reader-view message:** today the spinner runs until `RenderError` fires.
Switch the spinner box to the auth-wall box on the **earlier** of:
1. `IsPaywalled == true` is detected on the response (immediate; don't wait
   for full pipeline failure), OR
2. `4 s` elapsed AND the destination host is in `MissingCookieDomains`
   (predictive — we already know we'll fail).

The generic `Something went wrong` should never appear for paywalled domains
we recognise.

### 4. Edge cases & tone

(a) **Site-agnostic paywall (non-NYT, no stored creds):** swap host name in:
`Sign in to <domain> to read` / `This article is behind a paywall. Log in at
<domain> in your browser, then press Shift+R to refresh.` Same template,
host substituted — concrete without being NYT-specific.

(b) **Recovery confirmation:** on the next successful load after login, fire
a Toast (§3.5, info, `✦ Signed in to nytimes.com`) for one redraw, and drop
the status-bar nudge. The toast tells the user "this stuck — next time will
just work."

### 5. Design-system compliance

- Reader headline `Sign in to nytimes.com to read` in **PrimaryText** (not
  ErrorFg — this is an actionable wall, not a system failure). Box border in
  **WarningFg** (ANSI 214) to match §5.8 Bot Challenge precedent.
- Body in **SecondaryText**.
- Domain `nytimes.com` rendered in **AccentFg** in both status-bar and box.
- Key brackets `Shift+I`, `Shift+R`, `b` in **AccentFg** (ANSI 51); `:open
  browser`, `:refresh`, `:back` in **SecondaryText** — per §3.8 hint format.
- Status-bar version drops the 🍪 emoji (confusing) and the bare `paywall`
  word (non-actionable). Lead with the verb.

## Robustness review

Reframed around the **human-in-the-loop (HITL) block** family: captcha, login wall, cookie-consent, 2FA, paywall walk-through, region-block, click-to-continue interstitials. Surface area: `LoggedInPaywallDetector`, `ReadableContentExtractor.{HasPaywallElements,DetectPaywall,PaywallTextPatterns}`, `CachingPageLoader`, `BackgroundPreloadService` (`_hasPaywalledCookies`, `MinPaywalledWordCount=500`), `PageLoader.IsBotChallengePage`, 3-layer HTTP→Selenium→headed pipeline.

**Design rule:** when variant confidence is low or detectors disagree, fall back to generic "Something on **{domain}** needs your attention — open it in a browser, then press Shift+R." Misidentification cost is high (user hunts for wrong UI, concludes app is broken); generic copy is cheap. Reserve specific copy for high-confidence hits (CAPTCHA iframe + known vendor, or paywall element + paywalled host).

### False positives (detector says HITL when none required)
1. **Article quotes paywall/captcha copy.** Media-criticism piece quoting "subscribe to The Times" or "verify you are human" trips text patterns. *Mitigation: require element OR (text + truncated body + paragraph count below threshold), never text alone.*
2. **Authenticated DOM ships hidden paywall classes.** NYT logged-in DOM still contains `gateway`/`meter-` nodes; that's why `CachingPageLoader` only evicts when `!ForceBrowser`. *Mitigation: gate verdict on element visibility/text + low word count; short-circuit when account-link signal present.*
3. **Anonymous header anchors look like profile links.** `/account` or `/sign-in` anchors render in anonymous headers. *Mitigation: only fire HITL on `IsPaywalledDomain` AND positive paywall signal — never infer from absence.*

### False negatives (HITL present, detector misses)
4. **Free-quota soft gate.** 200 with 600 visible words and a soft gate after paragraph N slips past `MinPaywalledWordCount=500`. *Mitigation: combine word count with element presence + article-body/total-doc word ratio.*
5. **JS-rendered captcha or paywall.** HTTP layer sees a hydrated shell, no gate markup. *Mitigation: when paywalled host returns word count between 500–800, force Selenium re-fetch.*
6. **Variant misidentification — captcha vs cookie banner vs region-block.** Cloudflare interstitials, OneTrust banners, and geo-block pages all return 200 with non-article markup; `IsBotChallengePage` only catches one shape. *Mitigation: classifier emits `{Variant, Confidence}`; when confidence < threshold, surface generic "Action required on {domain}" copy — DO NOT guess. The cost of wrong variant copy ("solve the captcha" when it's a cookie banner) outweighs the win of being specific.*
7. **Redirect to interstitial.** NYT redirects unauth requests to `/subscription/...`. *Mitigation: detector inspects `result.Url` (final URL is now stored), treats known interstitial paths as HITL regardless of body.*

### Propagation hazards
8. **Cascading variants.** Site shows cookie banner first; user accepts; captcha then appears. The pipeline must re-detect on each retry, not cache the first verdict. *Mitigation: HITL verdicts are URL+timestamp scoped; Shift+R clears the verdict and re-runs the full detector chain — detector is a function of `(html, url, attempt#)`, never sticky state.*
9. **Async pre-fetch verdicts arrive late.** Verdict tagged to URL N arrives when reader is on N+1. *Mitigation: key verdicts by normalised URL; reader subscribes by current URL and drops stale.*
10. **Parallel link races.** First failure must not flip a domain banner before others land. *Mitigation: aggregate over a window; require ≥2 distinct URLs on the same domain before showing domain-level HITL.*
11. **Cache poisoning + retry masking.** `_cache.Put` runs unconditionally on `result.Success`; layer-3 headed-Chrome success can also overwrite the layer-1 HITL signal silently. *Mitigation: gate `Put` on `!HasHitlMarkers && !IsAuthInterstitialUrl`; bubble a `HitlResolvedAfterManualAction` flag through `PageLoadResult` so the toast says "Solved — refreshed."*

### UX hazards
12. **Cloudflare auto-resolves in 5–10 s.** Firing "Solve the captcha" too early is wrong — the page recovers on its own. *Mitigation: do not surface captcha copy until ≥8 s elapsed AND `cf_chl` markers still present on a re-poll; spinner stays generic until then.*
13. **Already in headed mode.** User is in front of real Chrome; layer-3 already opened a window. *Mitigation: when `BrowserSession.IsHeaded == true`, copy becomes "The captcha is in the Chrome window — solve it there, then press Shift+R" (no "open browser" hint, no Shift+I).*
14. **Offline / transient blip.** DNS or 429 should not produce HITL copy. *Mitigation: branch on exception type vs gate-detected; require either positive HITL marker OR two consecutive auth-coded failures before the actionable banner — single network errors get "Retrying…".*
15. **User IS on the auth/captcha page.** Rendering nyt.com/auth/login or a CF challenge URL and flagging it is a tautology. *Mitigation: suppress banner when current URL matches HITL path on same domain; expired cookies get distinct "Session expired — re-authenticate" copy.*
close_reason: Merged in 7e31056: typed HumanActionRequired pipeline + variant-aware copy + speed/clarity fixes. QA PASS-WITH-CAVEATS (live link-tree badge unreachable in CI, verified via in-process renderer tests). 6 fix commits address all QA punch-list items: _lastBlockedAction clears on success/origin-change, BrowserPreloadUrlAsync publishes typed verdict, MaxBoxContentWidth 46->56, PollForChallengeResolutionAsync removed (verdict emits on first detection).

title: Rewrite GCS bucket setup: inline FormField, prerequisite gate, GCP probe, in-flow create
description:
