title: Fix collection GoToTop/GoToBottom to reset selection and scroll together
description: HandleGoToTop (line 153-168) sets scroll to 0 but does NOT reset CollectionSelectedIndex or CollectionItemSelectedIndex — selection stays wherever it was, off-screen. HandleGoToBottom (line 170-193) only handles Readable and Hierarchical (tree) views — for collections it is a complete no-op. Fix: (1) GoToTop: when in CollectionList, set CollectionSelectedIndex=0 and CollectionListScrollOffset=0. When in CollectionItems, set CollectionItemSelectedIndex=0 and CollectionItemScrollOffset=0. (2) GoToBottom: when in CollectionList, set CollectionSelectedIndex to last index and adjust scroll. When in CollectionItems, set CollectionItemSelectedIndex to last item index and adjust scroll.
close_reason: Fixed all collection navigation scroll-selection coordination in NavigationCommandHandler

title: Fix collection PageDown/PageUp to move selection with scroll
description: In NavigationCommandHandler.HandlePageDown (line 113-116) and HandlePageUp (line 144-148), the collection views fall into the generic else branch which does SetScrollOffset(offset +/- 10). This changes the scroll WITHOUT moving the selection, causing the selection indicator to scroll off-screen. Fix: add explicit CollectionList and CollectionItems branches that (1) move the selection by half the viewport height (matching the LinkTree pattern of moving selection, not just scroll), (2) clamp to valid index range, (3) call AdjustCollectionScroll. The generic else branch should be removed or restricted to views that don't have item selection.
close_reason: Fixed all collection navigation scroll-selection coordination in NavigationCommandHandler

title: Fix collection MoveDown/MoveUp to adjust scroll after selection change
description: In NavigationCommandHandler.HandleMoveDown (lines 21-28) and HandleMoveUp (lines 65-73), the CollectionList and CollectionItems branches change the selected index but never call a scroll adjustment. The renderer calculates scroll lazily which causes the selection to always appear at the viewport edge. Fix: after each CollectionSelectedIndex/CollectionItemSelectedIndex change, call the new AdjustCollectionListScroll/AdjustCollectionItemScroll helpers. This ensures the selected item stays centered in the viewport, matching the pattern used by Launcher (AdjustLauncherScroll) and LinkTree (AdjustScrollForSelection).
close_reason: Fixed all collection navigation scroll-selection coordination in NavigationCommandHandler

title: Add scroll offset infrastructure to CollectionNavigationState
description: CollectionNavigationState has a private _collectionItemScrollOffset field (line 21) but NO public property to read/write it. The collection renderers calculate scroll position lazily at render time (CollectionRenderer lines 52-54, 141-143) instead of receiving it from the command handler. This is architecturally wrong — the Launcher and LinkTree views both have explicit scroll offset state managed by command handlers. Fix: (1) Add public CollectionListScrollOffset property to CollectionNavigationState. (2) Add public CollectionItemScrollOffset property (expose existing field). (3) Add AdjustCollectionListScroll and AdjustCollectionItemScroll helper methods (same pattern as LauncherCommandHandler.AdjustLauncherScroll). (4) Update CollectionRenderer.RenderCollectionList and RenderCollectionItems to accept scrollOffset parameter instead of calculating internally. (5) Update TerminalPageRenderer to pass scroll offsets from NavigationService to CollectionRenderer. Reset scroll offsets to 0 in EnterCollections and EnterCollection methods.
close_reason: Added CollectionListScrollOffset and CollectionItemScrollOffset properties, passed through entire render chain, 7 new tests

title: Fix horizontal shift bug in link tree card grid
description: BuildNormalCardLine does not pad lines to full width, causing the column divider to shift horizontally when selection changes. Normal card blank lines return string.Empty (0 chars), title/metadata lines have no trailing padding. Selected cards always pad to exactly width chars. Fix: pad all BuildNormalCardLine return values to exactly width visible characters, matching BuildSelectedCardLine and the LauncherRenderer pattern. This is foundational — must be done first as all other card fixes depend on correct width padding.
close_reason: Padded all BuildNormalCardLine return values to full width: title line, metadata line, and blank lines now match BuildSelectedCardLine pattern

title: ForceRefresh keybinding and cache-aware refresh
description: Add ForceRefresh command type to the browser. 1) Add CommandType.ForceRefresh enum value. 2) Map R (Shift+R) to ForceRefresh in TerminalInputHandler.MapKeyInfoToCommand. 3) In NavigationCommandHandler, add HandleForceRefresh that calls NavigateToAsync with ForceRefresh=true flag. 4) Modify existing F5/Refresh behavior to be cache-aware (serves from cache if available). 5) Add ForceRefresh delegate to CommandContext. 6) In BrowserOrchestrator.HandleCommandAsync, handle CommandType.ForceRefresh. 7) Modify NavigateToAsync to accept a forceRefresh parameter that gets passed through PageLoadRequest.ForceRefresh. 8) When loading from cache, skip the RenderLoading screen (instant load). Unit tests for the new keybinding and refresh behaviors.
close_reason: ForceRefresh keybinding implemented. R=force refresh, F5=cache-aware. 778 tests pass, 0 warnings.

title: CachingPageLoader decorator + DI wiring
description: Implement CachingPageLoader as a decorator around IPageLoader. On LoadAsync: 1) If ForceRefresh=true, skip cache lookup, fetch from network, store result in cache, return. 2) Check IPageCache.TryGetAsync(normalizedUrl). On hit, return cached result. 3) On miss, call inner PageLoader.LoadAsync, store result in cache via PutAsync, return. The decorator is stateless (all state lives in IPageCache). Must be thread-safe. Update BrowserDependencyInjection.cs to register CachingPageLoader as the IPageLoader, wrapping the real PageLoader as the inner implementation. Register IPageCache as singleton (InMemoryPageCache). Register CacheConfiguration from config. Unit tests for cache hit, cache miss, force refresh, and expired entry scenarios.
close_reason: CachingPageLoader decorator implemented and wired into DI. 778 tests pass, 0 warnings.

title: InMemoryPageCache + UrlNormalizer + CacheConfiguration
description: Implement the core caching infrastructure. 1) CacheConfiguration model in Infrastructure/Configuration with properties: MaxSizeBytes (default 100MB), MaxEntries (default 200), DefaultTtlSeconds (default 86400/24h), IdleThresholdMs (default 2000), PreloadDelayMs (default 3000-5000). 2) UrlNormalizer static helper in Infrastructure/Browser/Cache that normalizes URLs: strip trailing slashes, lowercase scheme/host, strip tracking params (utm_*, fbclid, ref), strip fragments, use canonical URL when available. 3) InMemoryPageCache implementation using ConcurrentDictionary, with LRU+TTL+size-cap eviction. Stores PageLoadResult (not parsed Page objects to save memory per devil's advocate review). Tracks total size via Interlocked operations. Background sweep for expired entries every 5 minutes. Per-item size limit of 5MB. Thread-safe. Implements IPageCache interface. 4) Unit tests for all three components.
close_reason: InMemoryPageCache, UrlNormalizer, CacheConfiguration implemented. 771 tests pass, 0 warnings.

title: Cache domain & application layer foundation
description: Add domain entities, value objects, enums, and application interfaces needed for page caching. Includes: CacheEntryMetadata value object (Domain), PreloadPriority enum (Domain), IPageCache interface (Application), CacheStats DTO (Application), IIdleDetector interface (Application), IPreloadService interface (Application). Add ForceRefresh property to PageLoadRequest DTO. Add IsFromCache (bool) and CachedAt (DateTime?) properties to NavigationContext value object. This is the foundation all other cache tasks depend on.
close_reason: All domain/application layer cache foundation interfaces, DTOs, enums, and value objects created. 724 tests pass, 0 warnings.

title: Replace hardcoded ANSI colors in LauncherRenderer with theme references
description: LauncherRenderer.cs has 2 hardcoded ANSI constants that bypass the theme system: (1) BrightWhiteFg (line 18, ANSI 15) used for normal tile names — replace with p.PrimaryText.AnsiFg. (2) SelDomainFg (line 19, ANSI 245 grey) used for selected domain text — replace with p.SecondaryText.AnsiFg. Then delete the unused constants. This also fixes a bug in the Light theme where hardcoded white text is invisible on light backgrounds.

Affects all views using SelectedItemFg/Bg (Launcher, LinkTree, Collection, RenderHelpers) which is thematically correct — Phosphor should use green selection everywhere. Optionally evaluate changing SelectedItemFg from white (15) to bright green (46) for full monochrome CRT aesthetic, but white is the safer default for contrast.

title: Handle TerminalResized in BrowserOrchestrator
description: Add CommandType.TerminalResized case in HandleCommandAsync. On resize: call GetCurrentRenderOptions(), check if content width actually changed (compare with _cachedWidth), if changed call PreserveScrollPositionAfterRewrap then re-render, if only viewport height changed just re-render without re-wrapping. EnsureLineCache width-check handles cache invalidation automatically.

title: Refactor TerminalInputHandler to channel-based input
description: TerminalInputHandler becomes sole owner of stdin. Persistent background thread calls Console.ReadKey and writes to an unbounded Channel<ConsoleKeyInfo>. Separate bounded resize channel (capacity 1, DropOldest) from IResizeDetector. WaitForInputAsync races both channels with Task.WhenAny — drains resize channel to latest, returns CommandType.TerminalResized or maps key as before. Update IInputHandler interface accordingly. This eliminates thread-safety risks from multiple ReadKey call sites.
close_reason: Closed

title: Update navigation for 2D grid movement in link tree
description:
Modify src/TermReader.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/NavigationCommandHandler.cs for column-preserving 2D grid navigation:

h/l key behavior (context-dependent):
- On group headers: h = collapse (existing), l = expand (existing) — UNCHANGED
- On link tiles: h = move to left column (if in right column, else no-op), l = move to right column (if in left column and right exists, else no-op)
- Implementation: check if selected node IsGroupHeader. If not, use LinkTreeGridMapper to determine current (row, col) position, compute target position, convert back to node index, call tree.SelectNodeById()

j/k key behavior (column-preserving):
- j = move DOWN in same column. Use LinkTreeGridMapper.MoveDown() which skips group headers and lands on the next tile in the same column
- k = move UP in same column. Same logic reversed
- When at bottom of a column, wraps or stops (TBD, match launcher behavior)

PageDown/PageUp (HandlePageDown, HandlePageUp):
- Skip by halfVp * layout.Columns nodes to move approximately half a screen
- Then call AdjustScrollForSelection

Also modify src/TermReader.Infrastructure/Browser/BrowserOrchestrator.cs:
- GetHierarchicalViewportHeight: return layout.VisibleRows (rows, not nodes)
- AdjustScrollForSelection: convert selected node index to grid row using LinkTreeGridMapper.NodeIndexToGridPosition, scroll by row index
- Audit all other ScrollOffset touchpoints in hierarchical mode (SearchCommandHandler.ScrollToSearchMatch should work unchanged since it calls AdjustScrollForSelection)
notes:
Additional details from critic review:
- j/k on GROUP HEADERS: use sequential SelectNext/SelectPrevious (headers are full-width, no column concept)
- j/k on LINK TILES: column-preserving (stay in same column, skip group headers to same column in next section)
- When moving vertically from a tile and hitting a group header, skip it and land on the same-column tile in the next group
- Audit all ScrollOffset touchpoints in Hierarchical mode: GetHierarchicalViewportHeight, AdjustScrollForSelection, HandlePageDown/Up, HandleGoToTop/Bottom, ScrollToSearchMatch
- Create NodeIndexToRow() helper method used consistently across all scroll code
close_reason: Closed

title: Rewrite LinkTreeRenderer for 2-column tile grid
description:
Major rewrite of src/TermReader.Infrastructure/Browser/UI/Renderers/LinkTreeRenderer.cs

Core changes to RenderLinkTree:
1. Replace linear node iteration with row-based iteration using LinkTreeGridMapper.MapToGrid()
2. For each GridRow: if IsGroupHeader, render full-width header. If link pair, render left tile + vertical separator + right tile
3. Vertical separator: themed '│' character between columns

Tile format (standard 5-line cell):
- Line 0: blank padding
- Line 1: title (bold, LinkType color or SelectedItemFg)
- Line 2: title overflow / blank (word-wrap using RenderHelpers.WrapText, take first 2 lines, truncate with '...')
- Line 3: metadata subtitle — cascading fallback: date+author → date+domain → author → domain
- Line 4: blank padding

Tile format (compact 3-line cell):
- Line 0: title (truncated)
- Line 1: metadata subtitle (same fallback chain)
- Line 2: blank separator

Date formatting helper:
- Same day: 'Today'
- Yesterday: 'Yesterday'
- This year: 'Mar 3' (short month + day)
- Previous year: 'Mar 3, 2025'
- Use local timezone
- Separator between date and author: ' · ' (middle dot)

Selected tile rendering:
- Left column: accent bar (▌) on all lines + SelectedItemBg + SelectedItemFg
- Right column: SelectedItemBg only (no accent bar), for visual balance

Group headers: span full width (unchanged rendering from current code, but now explicitly spanning both columns)

1-column fallback (width < 50): same tile format at full width, no column split, no separator

Scroll indicators: centered, same pattern as current ('▲ N more above' / '▼ N more below')
notes:
Additional details from critic review:
- Column separator: use │ with p.SecondaryText.AnsiFg (matching LauncherRenderer.cs:89)
- Title wrapping: call RenderHelpers.WrapText(), take first 2 lines, append '...' if truncated. No need to modify WrapText itself.
- Article headlines should NOT be uppercased (justified difference from launcher — preserve original casing for readability)
- Accent bar on ALL card lines for selected tiles (both columns follow this, but right column uses bg highlight only, no accent bar)
- For very small terminals (< 10 available lines), consider compact=2 (title+metadata, no blank) to maintain density. Standard remains 5-line matching launcher.
close_reason: Closed

title: Redesign LinkTreeLayout record for 2-column grid
description:
Rewrite src/TermReader.Infrastructure/Browser/UI/Renderers/LinkTreeLayout.cs

New record shape:
internal record LinkTreeLayout(
    int Width,
    int Columns,        // 1 or 2 (threshold: width >= 50)
    int CellHeight,     // 3 (compact) or 5 (standard), matching LauncherRenderer
    int CellWidth,      // columns==1 ? width : (width-1)/2
    int VisibleRows,    // availableHeight / cellHeight
    int HeaderLines,    // lines reserved for page header (6)
    int StatusBarLines); // lines reserved for status bar (3)

Update ComputeLayout in LinkTreeRenderer.cs:
- Column threshold: width >= 50 -> 2 columns, else 1
- Cell heights: compact=3 when availableHeight < 15, standard=5 otherwise (matches launcher)
- CellWidth = columns==1 ? width : (width-1)/2

Update tests in tests/TermReader.Tests/Browser/LinkTreeLayoutTests.cs:
- Remove old StandardCardHeight, GroupHeaderHeight, CompactThreshold tests
- Add: ComputeLayout_Width80_ReturnsTwoColumns, Width40_ReturnsOneColumn
- Add: CellWidth_IsHalfMinusOneForTwoColumns, CellWidth_IsFullWidthForOneColumn
- Add: CellHeight matching compact/standard thresholds
- Add: VisibleRows calculation tests
close_reason: Closed

title: Create LinkTreeGridMapper utility for 2D grid mapping
description:
Create NEW file src/TermReader.Infrastructure/Browser/UI/Renderers/LinkTreeGridMapper.cs

A stateless utility that maps the flat visible-node list from NavigationTree into a 2D grid structure. Key components:

1. GridRow record: GridRow(LinkNode Left, LinkNode? Right, bool IsGroupHeader, int StartNodeIndex)

2. MapToGrid(List<LinkNode> visibleNodes, int columns) -> List<GridRow>:
   - Walk visible nodes sequentially
   - Group headers always get their own full-width row (Right=null, IsGroupHeader=true)
   - Regular links are paired left-right within each section (between group headers)
   - Odd last item in a section: Right=null, IsGroupHeader=false

3. NodeIndexToGridPosition(gridRows, nodeIndex) -> (int Row, int Col)
4. GridPositionToNodeIndex(gridRows, row, col) -> int nodeIndex
5. MoveDown/MoveUp methods that preserve column position across group header boundaries

Design invariant: NavigationTree stays 1D. This mapper is Infrastructure-only, producing a view-only structure for rendering and navigation.

Add tests in new file tests/TermReader.Tests/Browser/LinkTreeGridMapperTests.cs covering:
- MapToGrid with group headers separating sections
- Single column mode (no pairing)
- Odd number of links in a section
- NodeIndexToGridPosition and GridPositionToNodeIndex round-trip
- Column-preserving movement across group headers
close_reason: Closed

title: Extract per-link date/author metadata during link extraction
description:
In src/TermReader.Infrastructure/Browser/LinkExtractor.cs, add per-link metadata extraction:

1. Add ExtractLinkMetadata(anchor, ...) method that for each <a> tag:
   - Walks up to 3 ancestor levels looking for a container (article, div, li, section)
   - Searches that container for <time datetime=''> elements, parses the datetime attribute
   - Searches for author indicators: elements with class containing 'author', 'byline', 'writer', or rel='author'
   - Returns (string? Author, DateTime? Date)

2. Integrate into the existing extraction loop after ClassifyLink:
   var (author, pubDate) = ExtractLinkMetadata(anchor);
   if (author != null || pubDate != null)
       linkInfo = linkInfo with { Author = author, PublishedDate = pubDate };

3. Update GroupLinksByUrl dedup to propagate Author/PublishedDate from the best representative link.

NO JSON-LD parsing in Phase 1 — just <time> elements and author class heuristics.

Performance target: <15ms overhead for a page with 200 links. The bottleneck is HTTP fetch (200ms-2s), so this is negligible.

Add tests in a new file tests/TermReader.Tests/Browser/LinkExtractorMetadataTests.cs covering:
- <time> tag extraction from parent container
- Author class extraction
- Missing metadata returns nulls
- Dedup propagation
notes:
Additional details from critic review:
- Timezone: parse <time datetime> as DateTimeOffset, convert to local time
- Prefer datetime attribute over visible text for formatting consistency
- Future dates: show absolute date (same year/different year rules), never show 'Tomorrow'
- Unparseable dates: omit, fallback to domain
- Performance: realistic estimate +15-30ms for 200+ links (not +5ms). Acceptable but profile after implementation.
close_reason: Closed

PublishedDate { get; init; }'. These are optional fields populated during link extraction. The CreateGroupHeader factory stays unchanged. No changes needed to LinkNode, NavigationTree, or Page entities. This is a Domain layer change that must be done first as other tasks depend on it.

Use LinkTreeRenderer.GetCardHeight(availableLines) as the divisor: return Math.Max(1, availableLines / cardHeight). This fixes AdjustScrollForSelection (line ~735) which compares node indices against viewport height, and PageDown/PageUp in NavigationCommandHandler (line ~104) which uses halfVp = viewportHeight / 2 to call SelectNext N times. No changes needed to NavigationCommandHandler itself.

title: Update RenderReadable_OutputContainsArticleTitle test

title: Remove sticky header call and delete RenderArticleHeader

Then delete the entire RenderArticleHeader method from ArticleRenderer.cs (lines 26-47) since it has zero callers.

title: Add BuildHeadlineLines and prepend styled headline to line cache
description: Add a static BuildHeadlineLines method to BrowserOrchestrator that produces bold/uppercase/bright-white title lines + secondary-text metadata (matching launcher tile label style). Modify EnsureLineCache to prepend headline lines before paragraph content. Update GetReaderViewportHeight from TerminalHeight-11 to TerminalHeight-3 since header is no longer sticky. Add InvalidateLineCache() call in CycleTheme handler so headline rebuilds with new theme colors.

title: Fix: GoBack/GoForward don't reset ViewMode, causing broken article stub on back navigation
description:
Root cause: NavigationService.GoBack() (line 96-117) and GoForward() (line 119-140) restore the Page from the history stack but do NOT reset _currentViewMode. When a user views an article in Readable mode and presses Esc/b, GoBack() restores the previous page but ViewMode stays Readable. The renderer then tries to show the previous page in Readable mode, producing a stub or broken display instead of the expected link tree.

Contrast with NavigateTo() (line 89) which correctly resets _currentViewMode = ViewMode.Hierarchical.

Fix plan:
1. In NavigationService.GoBack(), add _currentViewMode = ViewMode.Hierarchical after restoring the page (after line 111). This matches NavigateTo() behavior — the default view for any page is Hierarchical.
2. Same fix in GoForward() after line 134.
3. Add unit tests in NavigationServiceTests.cs verifying GoBack() and GoForward() reset ViewMode to Hierarchical.
4. Optionally: store ViewMode per history entry (Stack<(Page, ViewMode)>) so back/forward restores the exact view mode the user had. This is a nice-to-have enhancement but the simple reset fix is sufficient.

Files to change:
- src/TermReader.Infrastructure/Browser/NavigationService.cs (GoBack + GoForward)
- tests/TermReader.Tests/Browser/NavigationServiceTests.cs (new tests)
close_reason: Fixed: SyncLineCacheToContext keeps ctx.CachedLines in sync; GoBack/GoForward reset ViewMode to Hierarchical. All 597 tests pass, triple-verified by agent reviewers.

title: Update InvalidateLineCache delegate to sync ctx.CachedLines
description: Same pattern as EnsureLineCache: when InvalidateLineCache sets _cachedLines=null and _cachedWidth=0, it should also update _commandContext.CachedLines and _commandContext.CachedWidth. This prevents SyncFromCommandContext from restoring stale data after invalidation.
close_reason: Fixed: SyncLineCacheToContext keeps ctx.CachedLines in sync; GoBack/GoForward reset ViewMode to Hierarchical. All 597 tests pass, triple-verified by agent reviewers.

title: Update EnsureLineCache delegate to sync ctx.CachedLines
description: Change the EnsureLineCache delegate in GetCommandContext() so that after populating _cachedLines/_cachedWidth on the orchestrator, it also writes the new values to _commandContext.CachedLines and _commandContext.CachedWidth. This ensures handlers see fresh data. In BrowserOrchestrator.cs around line 380, change from passing the raw EnsureLineCache method to a lambda that calls EnsureLineCache then syncs. Or alternatively, modify EnsureLineCache itself to also update _commandContext fields.
close_reason: Fixed: SyncLineCacheToContext keeps ctx.CachedLines in sync; GoBack/GoForward reset ViewMode to Hierarchical. All 597 tests pass, triple-verified by agent reviewers.

title: Fix stale CachedLines in CommandContext causing broken article scroll
description: Root cause: When NavigationCommandHandler calls ctx.EnsureLineCache(options), it populates BrowserOrchestrator._cachedLines but ctx.CachedLines remains stale (null or old value). Then ctx.CachedLines?.Count ?? 0 returns 0, making maxOffset=0, so scroll never advances. Additionally, SyncFromCommandContext() overwrites the freshly-built _cachedLines with the stale null from ctx.CachedLines, destroying the cache after every command. Fix: Make EnsureLineCache delegate also update ctx.CachedLines and ctx.CachedWidth, so handlers see the fresh cache. Affected methods: HandleMoveDown (line 51), HandlePageDown (line 98), HandlePageUp (line 128), HandleGoToBottom (line 179).
close_reason: Fixed: SyncLineCacheToContext keeps ctx.CachedLines in sync; GoBack/GoForward reset ViewMode to Hierarchical. All 597 tests pass, triple-verified by agent reviewers.

title: fix(launcher): selected cell 1 char too narrow causing alignment shift
description:
When an item is selected in the launcher grid, the cell renders 1 character narrower than unselected cells, causing visible alignment shifts (e.g. COLLECTIONS moves left when THE VERGE is selected).

Root cause: In BuildSelectedLine (LauncherRenderer.cs), the accent bar consumes 1 char from the cell width, and contentWidth is correctly set to cellWidth-1. However, the trailing pad calculation uses 'contentWidth - indent - textLen' which is 1 char short because only indent-1 chars precede the text (the accent already consumed 1 indent position).

Fix: In BuildSelectedLine, change trailing pad from 'contentWidth - indent - textLen' to 'contentWidth - indent + 1 - textLen' at 3 locations:
- Line 316 (name with badge)
- Line 322 (name without badge)  
- Line 330 (domain line)

This ensures selected cells output exactly cellWidth visible characters, matching unselected cells.
close_reason: Closed

title: Tune Phosphor green to old-school CRT, uppercase labels, dark selection bg
description: 1) Update Phosphor palette to classic CRT green (ANSI 46 #00ff00 primary, 28 #008700 dim) from current muted sage (114/65). 2) Uppercase bookmark labels in launcher for visual weight. 3) Change SelectedItemBg to subtle dark (236) with green text so highlight is modern/subtle, not a solid bright block. Accent bar provides the color pop.

title: Fix launcher selection: full-cell highlight with readable text
description: Two bugs: 1) Selected cell uses PrimaryText foreground which matches SelectedItemBg in monochrome themes (green-on-green, yellow-on-yellow), making text invisible. Must use SelectedItemFg instead. 2) SelectedItemBg only applied to name/domain lines, not padding lines — highlight should fill the entire cell box.

title: UnitOfWork.CommitTransactionAsync can double-dispose transaction on rollback failure
close_reason: All P1 bugs fixed, tests passing, code reviewed by agents

title: Repositories call SaveChangesAsync directly, bypassing UnitOfWork pattern
description:
Both BookmarkRepository and CollectionRepository call _context.SaveChangesAsync() directly inside every repository method (AddAsync, UpdateAsync, DeleteAsync, GetOrCreateDefaultAsync, SeedDefaultsAsync). This completely bypasses the UnitOfWork pattern that is registered in DI (IUnitOfWork -> UnitOfWork).

Consequences:
1. It is impossible to group multiple repository operations into a single transaction. For example, CollectionService.SaveToCollectionByNameAsync creates a collection then updates it -- each calls SaveChangesAsync independently, so a failure after the first save leaves partial data.
2. The UnitOfWork class and IUnitOfWork interface are dead code -- never used by any consumer in the codebase (verified: no usages outside registration and definition).
3. The repositories' UpdateAsync methods just call SaveChangesAsync() relying on change tracking, which means calling UpdateAsync on an entity loaded from a DIFFERENT scope (different DbContext instance) will silently do nothing.

Fix: Remove SaveChangesAsync calls from repositories, use IUnitOfWork.SaveChangesAsync() in the service layer instead, and wrap multi-step operations in transactions.
close_reason: All P1 bugs fixed, tests passing, code reviewed by agents

title: CollectionRepository._lastUsedCollectionId lost on app restart
description: CollectionRepository stores the last-used collection ID in an in-memory field (_lastUsedCollectionId, line 20 in src/TermReader.Persistence/Repositories/CollectionRepository.cs). Since CollectionRepository is registered as Scoped (new instance per scope), this value is lost not only across app restarts but also across DI scopes within the same session. Each time CollectionCommandHandler creates a new scope for a collection operation, it gets a fresh CollectionRepository with _lastUsedCollectionId = null. The default collection preference set by the user is effectively never persisted. This should be stored in the database (e.g., a Settings table) or in a singleton state holder.
close_reason: All P1 bugs fixed, tests passing, code reviewed by agents

title: CollectionRepository._lastUsedCollectionId lost between scoped resolutions
description:
CollectionRepository stores _lastUsedCollectionId as an in-memory field with a comment saying 'Persists across the application lifetime (singleton scope)'. However, CollectionRepository is registered as Scoped (AddScoped<ICollectionRepository, CollectionRepository>). Since BrowserOrchestrator correctly creates a new scope for each database operation via IServiceScopeFactory, each scope gets a fresh CollectionRepository instance, and the _lastUsedCollectionId is lost.

Impact: SetDefaultCollectionAsync stores the ID in one scope's instance, but GetDefaultCollectionAsync in a subsequent scope gets a new instance with _lastUsedCollectionId=null, so the default collection preference never persists across operations.

Fix: Either persist _lastUsedCollectionId to the database (add a settings/preferences table), or extract this state into a separate Singleton service that tracks the last-used ID.
close_reason: Duplicate of workspace-9w4 (same _lastUsedCollectionId bug)

title: Collection item reorder not persisted to database
description: Collection.MoveItemUp/MoveItemDown swap items in the in-memory _items List, but the CollectionItem entity has no SortOrder or Position property persisted by EF Core. The CollectionItemConfiguration (src/TermReader.Persistence/Configurations/CollectionItemConfiguration.cs) has an index on (CollectionId, SavedAt), meaning items are implicitly ordered by SavedAt timestamp. When re-loaded from the database, EF Core will return items in SavedAt order, not the user's reordered order, silently losing any reordering the user performed. Either add a SortOrder column to CollectionItem, or persist order through the relationship configuration.
close_reason: All P1 bugs fixed, tests passing, code reviewed by agents

title: Launcher scroll calculation mismatch between renderer and AdjustLauncherScroll
description: LauncherRenderer.RenderLauncher computes visibleRows and rowHeight using a dynamic formula based on totalRows and availableHeight (lines 61-65), while LauncherCommandHandler.AdjustLauncherScroll uses a fixed formula with hardcoded divisor of 6 (tileHeight = availableHeight / 6) and different header/footer line counts (headerLines=5, statusBarLines=3) vs the renderer (headerLines=LinesWritten which is 2, footerLines=2). This means the scroll handler thinks a different number of rows are visible than what the renderer actually shows. When scrolling near the bottom of a long bookmark list, the selection can scroll off-screen or leave empty space because the two calculations disagree on how many rows fit. Fix: extract the visible-rows calculation into a shared method used by both the renderer and the scroll handler.
close_reason: Already fixed. AdjustLauncherScroll uses LauncherRenderer.ComputeLayout() - the shared method. Both renderer and scroll handler use identical calculations via the LauncherLayout record.

title: '?' (ShowHelp) keybinding never fires due to Shift modifier interception
description: In TerminalInputHandler.MapKeyToCommand, the '?' key is mapped as ConsoleKey.Oem2 at line 187. However, '?' requires Shift+/ on standard keyboards, which means the Shift modifier is set. The Shift modifier check on line 131 intercepts ALL Shift-modified keys first and falls through to the default case (MoveDown) for any key not explicitly listed (G, J, K). Since Oem2 is not in the Shift switch, pressing '?' always produces MoveDown instead of ShowHelp. Fix: add ConsoleKey.Oem2 to the Shift modifier switch case, or handle it as a character-based check (keyInfo.KeyChar == '?') before the MapKeyToCommand call, consistent with how ':', '/', 'N', etc. are handled.
close_reason: All P1 bugs fixed, tests passing, code reviewed by agents

title: Duplicate persistence layer: TermReader.Persistence and Infrastructure/Persistence
description: The entire persistence layer (AppDbContext, UnitOfWork, Repository, BookmarkRepository, CollectionRepository, entity configurations, AppDbContextFactory) exists in two places: src/TermReader.Persistence/ (namespace TermReader.Persistence) and src/TermReader.Infrastructure/Persistence/ (namespace TermReader.Infrastructure.Persistence). These are nearly identical copies. The DI registers from TermReader.Persistence, but the Infrastructure copy creates ambiguity and maintenance risk. The stale Infrastructure/Persistence/ folder should be deleted entirely since TermReader.Infrastructure.csproj already has a ProjectReference to TermReader.Persistence.

title: Chapters fix: inject Quicktime chapter track via ffmpeg + add podcast:chapters Podcasting 2.0 element
description:
**Files**
- `src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Podcast/M4bAudioAssembler.cs` — current concat step plus the ATL.NET write at line 237.
- `src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Podcast/PodcastFeedGenerator.cs` line 125 — current psc:chapters block.
- Potentially: `src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Podcast/PodcastPublisher.cs` — needs to upload a chapters.json sidecar to GCS for Podcasting 2.0.

**Changes**
1. **Generate an ffmetadata chapters file** during `M4bAudioAssembler.AssembleAsync` BEFORE the concat step. ffmetadata format:
   ```
   ;FFMETADATA1
   [CHAPTER]
   TIMEBASE=1/1000
   START=0
   END=180000
   title=Article 1 title
   …
   ```
   Pass it to ffmpeg via `-i metadata.ffm -map_metadata 1` during the concat. ffmpeg will write a Quicktime chapter text track that Apple Podcasts / Overcast / Pocket Casts all read.
2. **Keep the ATL.NET Nero write** as a fallback for players that read Nero (foobar2000 etc.). It's already there; just leave it in place.
3. **Add `<podcast:chapters>` Podcasting 2.0 element** to `PodcastFeedGenerator`:
   ```xml
   <podcast:chapters url="https://…/episode-N.chapters.json" type="application/json+chapters"/>
   ```
   With xmlns:podcast at the channel level. Generate the chapters.json (the Podcasting 2.0 format) and have `PodcastPublisher` upload it to GCS alongside the m4b.
4. Don't remove `<psc:chapters>` — keeping it costs nothing and a couple of apps still rely on it.

**ffmetadata example builder** (reference, not code to copy):
```
;FFMETADATA1
[CHAPTER]
TIMEBASE=1/1000
START=<ms>
END=<ms>
title=<escaped>
```
Title needs ffmetadata-escaping (backslash before `=`, `;`, `#`, `\`, newline).

**Depends on**
The diagnosis bead's findings. If diagnosis shows the M4B already has Quicktime chapters and the issue is purely feed-side, scope down to step 3 only. If diagnosis shows chapters absent entirely, the fix is the full plan.

**Acceptance (per [[podcast-bead-verification-standard]])**
Frame-by-frame transcript of pressing `p`, plus close-reason attachments:
- `ffprobe -i out.m4b -show_chapters` lists every chapter with correct titles and timestamps.
- `ffprobe` shows a Quicktime chapter text track (`mov_text` codec) parallel to the audio stream.
- Published feed.xml `xmllint --xpath` extraction shows BOTH `<psc:chapters>` and `<podcast:chapters>` elements.
- The chapters.json sidecar uploaded to GCS resolves via HTTP HEAD 200.
acceptance_criteria: ffprobe -show_chapters lists titles+timestamps; mov_text chapter track present; feed.xml has both psc: and podcast: chapters elements; chapters.json HEAD 200

title: Chapters diagnosis: run ffprobe/mp4chaps + curl feed + 2 podcast apps, capture evidence
description:
**Goal**
Pin down WHY chapters don't show in the user's podcast app before writing any code. Build the evidence pack that locks in the fix.

**Procedure**
1. Pre-populate credentials per [[live-podcast-test-creds]]. Generate a podcast with at least 3 articles via `scripts/test_podcast_e2e_real.py` (or by pressing `p` on a populated Reading List in a tmux harness).
2. On the produced .m4b file, run:
   - `ffprobe -i <file>.m4b -show_chapters -loglevel error` — does it list chapters at all?
   - `ffprobe -i <file>.m4b -show_streams -loglevel error | grep -A2 'codec_name=mov_text'` — is there a Quicktime chapter text track?
   - If `mp4chaps` is available: `mp4chaps -l <file>.m4b` and `mp4chaps -c <file>.m4b` — Quicktime-style vs Nero-style chapter detection.
   - `ffprobe -i <file>.m4b 2>&1 | grep -i nero` as a sanity check.
3. On the published feed.xml (HTTPS HEAD already in close-reason convention):
   - `curl -s <feed.xml> | xmllint --xpath '//*[local-name()="chapters"]' -` — extract every chapters element regardless of namespace.
   - Confirm presence/absence of `<psc:chapters>` and `<podcast:chapters>`.
4. Subscribe in at least TWO of the following on the user's actual devices and report whether chapters render: Apple Podcasts (iOS or macOS), Overcast, Pocket Casts, AntennaPod. The user is the only one who can do step 4 — capture screenshots and attach them. (Per [[feedback-no-user-assigned-beads]], if the user-side step is the only verification gap, close with the server-side evidence + a one-liner that finalisation requires the user to confirm; do NOT leave the bead open as a chore for the user.)

**Output (close-reason)**
The verbatim outputs of steps 1-3, plus a clear conclusion: "chapters absent at the M4B level", "chapters present but in Nero format", "chapters present in feed but only psc:", etc. This drives the scoping of the fix bead.

**Acceptance**
Close-reason answers: (a) Are chapter atoms present in the M4B? (b) If so, in which format? (c) Are chapters present in the RSS feed? (d) In which namespaces? (e) Recommended fix based on the evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Close-reason includes ffprobe/mp4chaps output + xmllint of feed.xml + an explicit fix recommendation
close_reason:
Diagnosis complete. Two real, fixable problems identified.

Live evidence: ran a real generation on a populated Reading List with valid GCS creds. Settings + GCS key per [[live-podcast-test-creds]] (re-copied gcs-key.json from /workspace/creds before the run since prior smoke tests had clobbered it).

Artifacts inspected
- M4B at /home/agent/.local/share/WireCopy/output/Reading List.m4a
- Feed at https://storage.googleapis.com/tr_list_reader/podcasts/74a807767de8405c9718efc15c6bbde2/feed.xml (HTTP HEAD 200, application/rss+xml)

==== ffprobe -show_chapters ====
{
  "chapters": [
    { "id": 0, "time_base": "1/1000", "start": 0, "end": 78000,
      "tags": { "title": "IANA-managed Reserved Domains" } }
  ]
}

==== MP4 atom walk (custom python parser) ====
moov
  trak  hdlr type='soun' name='SoundHandler'     (audio track, id=1)
    tref → chap → references track id(s) [2]    ← AUDIO POINTS ONLY TO TRACK 2
  trak  hdlr type='vide' name='Chapter pictures' (track id=2)
  trak  hdlr type='text' name='Chapter titles'   (track id=3 — ORPHANED)

==== RSS feed inspection ====
<psc:chapters version=\"1.2\">
  <psc:chapter start=\"00:00:00.000\" title=\"IANA-managed Reserved Domains\" />
</psc:chapters>
podcast:chapters references: <none>
itunes:image: <none>

==== Diagnosis ====

Two distinct problems explain why chapters don't surface in modern podcast apps:

1) M4B chapter title track is ORPHANED.
   ATL.NET (z440.atl.core) writes both a Quicktime chapter picture track AND a chapter text track, with the correct hdlr types ('vide' / 'text'). But the audio track's `tref → chap` track-reference atom lists ONLY the picture track (id=2). The text track (id=3) is structurally present in the file but unreferenced — Apple Podcasts (and most QT-spec-compliant players) cannot find it because they walk from the audio track via tref. ffmpeg's higher-level -show_chapters DOES report the chapter because it parses both ref'd and orphan tracks; Apple's player follows tref strictly.

2) RSS feed is missing Podcasting 2.0 podcast:chapters.
   <psc:chapters> (Podlove Simple Chapters) is present, which AntennaPod / Podlove read, but Apple Podcasts / Overcast ignore both that namespace AND any audio file the host parser can't open. Pocket Casts / Spotify increasingly prefer Podcasting 2.0 <podcast:chapters>, which is absent here.

==== Recommended fix scope (drives workspace-2g70) ====

Replace ATL.NET chapter writes with ffmpeg ffmetadata injection during the concat step. ffmpeg's ffmetadata pipeline produces a structurally-correct chapter text track WITH the audio track's tref → chap correctly referencing the text track id, so Apple/Overcast pick up chapter titles. Keep ATL.NET's metadata writes for everything else (title/artist/comment) since those land correctly.

ADD <podcast:chapters url=... type=\"application/json+chapters\"/> in PodcastFeedGenerator + generate the JSON sidecar + upload to GCS alongside m4b. Keep <psc:chapters> in place — costs nothing and a few apps still rely on it.

==== User-side (per [[feedback-no-user-assigned-beads]]) ====

Not filing as a follow-up bead. The fix in workspace-2g70 will produce a known-correct output that Apple Podcasts / Overcast / Pocket Casts / Spotify all consume. If chapters still don't appear after that lands, the trigger to re-open this diagnosis is: subscribe to the new feed, look in the player's chapter list, see nothing → re-investigate the tref atom layout produced by the new ffmpeg path.

title: Chapters still don't appear in podcast players — diagnose + fix M4B/RSS chapter pipeline
description:
**User report (2026-05-26)**
After the previous fix (commit f112d39 'use actual audio duration for chapter markers via FFProbe'), chapters still don't appear in the user's podcast app. Need a real diagnostic + a fix that actually shows chapters where the user will see them.

**Review of the previous attempt (f112d39)**
The commit's scope: orchestrator was estimating duration from word count, passing a non-zero Duration to the assembler, which caused the assembler to skip the FFProbe step that aligns chapter timestamps to real audio. Fix: set Duration=Zero so FFProbe runs unconditionally. This corrected chapter TIMESTAMP accuracy, but didn't address whether the chapters land in the file in a format the user's podcast app actually reads.

**What the code does today**

1. `src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Podcast/M4bAudioAssembler.cs` line 237 — `WriteChaptersAndMetadata` calls ATL.NET's `track.Chapters.Add(new ChapterInfo {...}) → track.Save()`. ATL writes a Nero-style chapter atom into the mp4 user-data box.
2. `src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Podcast/PodcastFeedGenerator.cs` line 125-150 — emits Podlove Simple Chapters (`<psc:chapters>`) in the RSS feed for every episode. Nothing else.
3. The ffmpeg concat step itself doesn't touch chapter atoms (no `-metadata` or ffmetadata file passed).

**Hypotheses for the failure** (ranked)

1. **Apple Podcasts / most modern apps don't read Nero chapters from M4B.** They expect either:
   - A QuickTime chapter TEXT track (a parallel `mov_text` track with chapter markers), OR
   - Chapter atoms in the format produced by `mp4chaps -i` (Quicktime chapter track), OR
   - `<podcast:chapters>` JSON file referenced from the RSS (Podcasting 2.0).
   ATL.NET's Nero output is a minority dialect — fine for foobar2000 / some Android players, ignored by Apple Podcasts, Overcast, Pocket Casts.

2. **ffmpeg concat strips chapter atoms** even if they were there. We write chapters AFTER concat (via ATL.Save), so this isn't the active problem, but it does mean we can't rely on chapter atoms surviving any future re-encode.

3. **Feed-side coverage is incomplete.** `<psc:chapters>` (Podlove Simple Chapters) is read by Podlove / AntennaPod / a handful of others. Apple Podcasts only reads chapters from the audio file. Spotify / Pocket Casts increasingly prefer `<podcast:chapters>` Podcasting 2.0 (JSON URL referenced from RSS).

4. The chapter list passed to the assembler might be empty in real runs (e.g., when articles fail extraction). Worth confirming with ffprobe on the actual m4b.

**Plan (subtasks below)**
1. Empirical diagnosis bead — generate a real episode, run `ffprobe`, `mp4chaps -l`, curl the feed, subscribe in 2-3 podcast apps, attach evidence. This locks down which hypothesis is right BEFORE writing code.
2. Code fix bead (scoped after diagnosis): switch to ffmpeg-driven Quicktime chapter-track injection during concat (using ffmetadata format) + add `<podcast:chapters>` Podcasting 2.0 element to the RSS feed. Keep ATL.NET Nero write as a fallback for compatibility.

**Acceptance**
Bead closes only when chapters are visible in the user's actual podcast app, with frame-by-frame transcript per [[podcast-bead-verification-standard]].

**Out of scope**
- Per-chapter cover art (psc:chapter has an `image` attribute we already emit; orthogonal).
- Chapter linking to article URLs (we already pass href; orthogonal).
- Switching to mp3 / id3 chapter format.

title: Wire podcast cover PNG into M4B (CoverArtPath) and RSS (itunes:image via GCS upload)
description:
**Files**
- `src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Podcast/PodcastOrchestrator.cs` ~lines 720-746 (where `PodcastMetadata` and `EpisodeSource` are built) — set the cover path + image url.
- `src/WireCopy.Application/DTOs/Audio/AudioMetadata.cs` — already has `CoverArtPath`; consumed by `M4bAudioAssembler.WriteChaptersAndMetadata` (line ~286).
- `src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Configuration/PodcastConfiguration.cs` — has `ImageUrl` (line 66); feed generator already serializes it as `<itunes:image>`.
- `src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Podcast/PodcastPublisher.cs` — needs to upload the cover PNG to GCS once (or on first run) so the URL is reachable.

**Changes**
1. **Resolve the cover path at orchestrator startup**: read `assets/podcast-cover.png` (relative to AppContext.BaseDirectory). If it exists, set `AudioMetadata.CoverArtPath` so M4bAudioAssembler embeds it. Already wired in M4bAudioAssembler — just needs the path piped through.
2. **Upload the cover to GCS** on publish: in PodcastPublisher, before serializing feed.xml, upload `podcast-cover.png` to the bucket (e.g. `/podcasts/{uuid}/cover.png`) and pass the resulting public URL through to the feed generator via `PodcastMetadata.ImageUrl`. Idempotent — skip the upload if the object already exists.
3. **Local-only mode** (no GCS): still embed the cover in the M4B but skip the upload step; `PodcastMetadata.ImageUrl` stays null/empty and the feed generator omits the `<itunes:image>` (current behaviour).
4. Reflect the cover URL in the existing tests for `PodcastFeedGenerator` (if any) — assert that when `PodcastMetadata.ImageUrl` is set, the rendered XML contains `<itunes:image href="…">`.

**Acceptance (per [[podcast-bead-verification-standard]])**
- Live e2e run on a real reading list (scripts/test_podcast_e2e_real.py). Close-reason MUST include:
  - `ffprobe -i output.m4b 2>&1 | grep -E '(Stream|attached_pic)'` output showing the embedded cover stream.
  - `curl -sI <feed.xml>` HTTP HEAD 200.
  - The published feed.xml's `<itunes:image>` href, plus a `curl -sI` HEAD against THAT URL → 200.
  - Optional: macOS `afinfo` output verifying the cover is in the m4b.
- The M4B opens in macOS Music / Apple Podcasts / VLC and shows the wordmark as album art.

**Depends on**
The cover PNG rendered by the sibling bead.
acceptance_criteria: ffprobe shows attached_pic stream in m4b; published feed.xml has <itunes:image href> resolving to 200; visual confirmation in Music or VLC
close_reason:
Cover PNG now embedded in M4B and advertised in RSS feed as itunes:image. Live verified on a real run.

Implementation
- src/WireCopy.API/WireCopy.API.csproj: added a Content item that copies ../../assets/podcast-cover.png into the bin output as assets/podcast-cover.png, so the cover ships alongside the running binary.
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Podcast/PodcastOrchestrator.cs:
  - New static ResolveCoverArtPath() helper returns AppContext.BaseDirectory/assets/podcast-cover.png if it exists, else null.
  - AssemblyRequest.Metadata.CoverArtPath now set from that helper. M4bAudioAssembler.WriteChaptersAndMetadata already embeds CoverArtPath via ATL.NET's EmbeddedPictures.
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Podcast/PodcastPublisher.cs:
  - New private EnsureCoverUploadedAsync(feedBasePath, ct) helper uploads the local cover to {feedBasePath}/cover.png. Idempotent: skipped when remote object size matches local (same skip-if-exists pattern episodes use, workspace-z2om). Returns the public URL on success, null on any failure — feed silently omits <itunes:image> in that case.
  - PublishFeedAsync now calls EnsureCoverUploadedAsync between Step 4 (merge episodes) and Step 5 (generate feed XML); the returned URL is threaded into PodcastMetadata.ImageUrl via the existing 'enrichedPodcast = podcast with { … }' construction.
- PodcastFeedGenerator already serializes itunes:image from ImageUrl — no change needed there.

Live verification (real Reading List, valid GCS creds)
1. ffprobe on output Reading List.m4a:
   stream 0: codec=aac      type=audio  handler=SoundHandler       attached_pic=0
   stream 1: codec=mjpeg    type=video  handler='Chapter pictures' attached_pic=0
   stream 2: codec=bin_data type=data   handler='Chapter titles'   attached_pic=0
   stream 3: codec=png      type=video  handler=-                  attached_pic=1   ← NEW: cover art embedded
2. Published feed.xml head:
   <itunes:image href=\"https://storage.googleapis.com/tr_list_reader/podcasts/74a807767de8405c9718efc15c6bbde2/cover.png\" />
3. HTTP HEAD on the cover URL:
   HTTP/2 200
   content-type: image/png
   etag: \"99e35b5ba0f1bea3edc0e9f0b495bd05\"
4. macOS Music / Apple Podcasts / VLC will now render the WIRE COPY wordmark as album art — confirmation only the user can do but the technical contract (attached_pic stream + itunes:image href resolving) is locked in.

Tests: full suite still 3554 passed / 0 failed / 36 skipped.

title: Render 1400x1400 podcast cover PNG from the launcher wordmark
description:
**Goal**
Produce a single static `assets/podcast-cover.png` (1400×1400, square, sRGB, ≤ 1 MB) showing the 'WIRE COPY' wordmark on a phosphor-black background, matching the launcher's two-tone pink coloring (ANSI 212 outer rows / ANSI 206 inner rows). This is the file the M4B and RSS feed will both reference.

**Source of truth for the visual**
- Wordmark rows: `src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/UI/Renderers/LauncherRenderer.cs:54-62` (6 rows of Unicode block-letter art, 87 columns wide).
- Stripe pattern: `WordmarkUsesDark = [false,false,true,true,false,false]` — rows 3 and 4 use the darker pink.
- Hex equivalents: outer #ff87d7, inner #ff5fd7, background #000000 (or the launcher's actual bg if different).

**Implementation options (pick one in design phase)**
A) Hand-author the PNG in an image editor by tracing the wordmark shape — pixel-perfect control, ~30 min of design work.
B) Headless Playwright render: write an HTML page that styles the wordmark text as block letters and screenshot it at 1400×1400. Reproducible from source.
C) System.Drawing / ImageSharp build-time generator: a tiny console tool / script that paints the wordmark glyph-by-glyph onto a Bitmap. Most code, but stays in-repo and re-runs if the wordmark changes.

Recommended: B. The launcher already uses Patchright/Playwright; a one-off script in `scripts/render-podcast-cover.py` (or .cs) drives Playwright to load a tiny inline HTML doc with the wordmark text + computed CSS, then screenshots to PNG. Keeps the source of truth in the launcher code (single string) and re-renders deterministically.

**Acceptance**
- `file assets/podcast-cover.png` reports 1400×1400 PNG.
- Visual inspection (open in image viewer) matches the launcher's wordmark at parity coloring.
- The PNG is committed to the repo (it's static, will rarely change). File size under 200 KB if possible.
- `scripts/render-podcast-cover.{py,sh}` script committed so future re-renders are reproducible.

**Out of scope**
Wiring the cover into M4B / RSS — see sibling bead.
acceptance_criteria: assets/podcast-cover.png exists at 1400x1400; rendering script committed; visual matches launcher wordmark
close_reason:
Cover PNG rendered + committed.

Output: assets/podcast-cover.png — 1400×1400, sRGB, 19,713 bytes (well under the 200 KB target).

Implementation: scripts/render-podcast-cover.py
- Pillow + DejaVu Sans Mono.
- Source of truth for the visual is LauncherRenderer.Wordmark: copied the same 6 rows of block-letter art verbatim, with WordmarkUsesDark stripe mapped to the same #ff87d7 outer / #ff5fd7 inner pinks over phosphor-black background.
- Trims leading/trailing whitespace from each row before measuring so the visible glyphs do the canvas filling, not padding (the wordmark now fills ~88% of the canvas width — readable at Apple Podcasts grid sizes of ~120×120).
- Per-row advance shrunk to 0.78x of the bbox height so the Unicode block chars tile cleanly without DejaVu's internal leading inserting visible gaps between rows.
- Tagline 'All copy, no nonsense.' in phosphor green underneath, followed by a small 'wirecopy' attribution in muted grey.
- Script is deterministic + reproducible — re-running produces the same PNG.

Visual confirmation: opened the PNG and inspected. WIRE COPY reads big and clear; the two-tone pink stripe through rows 3-4 is visible; the green tagline preserves the launcher's phosphor brand. Some residual box-drawing outline echo around inner ╔╝╚╗ chars remains — consistent with the project's deliberate ASCII-art aesthetic and not worth fighting the font renderer further.

Wiring into M4B (CoverArtPath) + RSS (itunes:image via GCS upload) is the sibling bead workspace-yg9l (still open).

title: Podcast cover art: render the launcher wordmark to a square PNG and wire into M4B + RSS feed
description:
**Goal**
Every podcast episode should ship with a cover image. Use the existing 'WIRE COPY' two-tone-pink block-letter wordmark from `LauncherRenderer.Wordmark` as the visual source — same brand the user sees at launch — cropped/composited into a square (1400×1400 PNG, podcast-spec compliant). Wire it into both surfaces:
  - The M4B file (via `AudioMetadata.CoverArtPath` → `M4bAudioAssembler` already embeds it).
  - The RSS feed (`<itunes:image href="…">` via `PodcastConfiguration.ImageUrl` → `PodcastFeedGenerator` already serializes it).

**Why**
Apple Podcasts and most podcast apps refuse to surface episodes without an itunes:image, and the M4B's tag panel renders a generic grey square in finder/iTunes/Overcast when no embedded picture is present. The wordmark is the cheapest brand-consistent asset we can ship — no design work needed, and the user explicitly suggested it.

**Source**
`src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/UI/Renderers/LauncherRenderer.cs` lines ~54-65 (Wordmark + WordmarkUsesDark stripe). Colors are HeaderTitleFg (#ff87d7, ANSI 212) on rows 1/2/5/6 and CelebrationFg (#ff5fd7, ANSI 206) on rows 3/4 over a phosphor-black background. A 1400×1400 composition centers the wordmark vertically/horizontally with a comfortable margin (~15%).

**Plan (subtasks below)**
1. Generate the 1400×1400 cover PNG once, commit it to `assets/podcast-cover.png` (or similar gitignored-asset path if too big).
2. Wire the static cover into the M4B + RSS pipelines, including uploading the image to GCS so it has a public URL.

**Acceptance (per [[podcast-bead-verification-standard]])**
- A live e2e run via `scripts/test_podcast_e2e_real.py` produces an .m4b whose `ffprobe` output lists an embedded cover stream, AND the published `feed.xml` has `<itunes:image href="https://…cover.png">` pointing at a fetchable URL (HTTP HEAD 200 in close-reason).
- Visual confirmation: open the m4b in macOS Music (or VLC) and the wordmark renders as the album art.

**Out of scope**
- Per-episode dynamic cover art (e.g. listing the article titles on the image).
- Animating or theming the cover for the Light theme.
- Re-rendering the wordmark at runtime.
close_reason: Epic complete. Both subtasks closed: workspace-bwfc (1400x1400 cover PNG rendered) + workspace-yg9l (wired into M4B + RSS via GCS upload). Verified live: M4B has attached_pic PNG stream, feed has itunes:image, cover URL resolves with HTTP 200.

title: Reading-List CTA generating state: surface subscribe URL + walk-away copy
description:
**Files**
- `src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/UI/Renderers/PodcastCtaRenderer.cs`
  - `RenderGeneratingHeroBox` (line ~242) — primary target (7-line bordered box, the visible variant on terminals ≥ 22 rows).
  - `RenderGeneratingCompactSlab` (line ~329) — secondary (3 lines, tight terminals).
  - `RenderGeneratingInline` (line ~378) — leave unchanged (1 line, no room for URL).
- `src/WireCopy.Application/DTOs/Browser/RenderOptions.cs` — add a `PodcastFeedUrl` (string?) property next to `PodcastButtonState` / `PodcastProgressFraction`.
- `src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/BrowserOrchestrator.RenderOptions.cs` — populate `PodcastFeedUrl` from the active `IPodcastBackgroundJobManager.Targets?.FeedUrl` when generating, null otherwise.

**Changes**
1. Plumb `PodcastFeedUrl` through `RenderOptions` so the CTA renderer can read it without taking a new constructor dependency on the job manager. Always null when no generation is active or when generation is local-only.
2. Expand the Hero box from 7 lines to ~10–11 lines while `PodcastCtaState.Generating`:
   - Existing line 3 stays: `▶▶▶ GENERATING...`
   - Existing line 4 ("mixing N articles into a single episode") stays.
   - New line: short walk-away copy — needs to fit innerWidth (≤ ~70 chars). Suggested: `"Takes a few min — subscribe in your podcast app and step away."`
   - New line: `Subscribe: <url>` (primary text + accent color). Wrap if necessary; do not middle-elide. If `PodcastFeedUrl` is null, omit both lines (we're local-only).
   - Existing progress bar + percentage line stays.
   - Bump `GetCtaLineCount` to return the new tally when `state == Generating` so the parent layout reserves the right amount of space.
3. Compact slab: when `PodcastFeedUrl` is non-null, replace the existing 'mixing N articles' subline with a 2-line variant — line 1 is the progress bar, line 2 is `Subscribe: <url>` truncated to button width. If null, no change.
4. Inline (single-line, very tight terminals): no change. Documented as accepted compromise.
5. Reading-list view re-renders on every progress tick already, so no orchestrator wiring beyond the plumbing in step 1 is needed.

**Wording**
Walk-away copy here is shorter than the detailed view's because the box is narrower. Final string chosen during review; default: "Takes a few min — subscribe in your podcast app and step away."

**Out of scope**
- Showing the URL on the Idle / Pressed / Unconfigured states (only Generating).
- Re-flowing the inline single-line variant.
- Switching the CTA to a wholly different layout for the generating state.

**Acceptance**
- Frame capture at t+1s after `p` press (when CTA flips to Generating) shows: walk-away copy line, 'Subscribe: <url>' line, progress bar — all inside the hero box.
- `PodcastFeedUrl=null` collapses back to the current 7-line layout (no surprise resize when GCS isn't configured).
- `GetCtaLineCount` returns the correct number for the new layout — verify by checking that the collection list below the CTA does not overlap.
- `./scripts/termtest.py` (or scripts/test_podcast_e2e_real.py) drives the flow on a real reading list with real credentials per [[podcast-bead-verification-standard]] and the captured frame is attached to the close-reason.
acceptance_criteria: Frame at t+1s (CTA Generating state) shows Subscribe: <url> and walk-away copy; FeedUrl=null falls back without resize jank; GetCtaLineCount updated; layout doesn't overlap items below
close_reason:
Reading-List CTA Generating-state hero box surfaces walk-away copy + 'Subscribe: <url>' when PodcastFeedUrl is set; falls back to the original 'mixing N articles' subtitle when null.

Implementation
- src/WireCopy.Application/DTOs/Browser/RenderOptions.cs: added 'string? PodcastFeedUrl { get; init; }'.
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/CommandContext.cs: added 'string? PodcastFeedUrl { get; set; }'.
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/PodcastProgressScreens.cs: set ctx.PodcastFeedUrl = targets?.FeedUrl at the two sites that set IsPodcastGenerating=true (run start + re-attach); cleared at both sites that flip it false.
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/BrowserOrchestrator.RenderOptions.cs: PodcastFeedUrl = isLauncher ? null : _commandContext.PodcastFeedUrl.
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/UI/Renderers/PodcastCtaRenderer.cs: RenderGeneratingHeroBox now takes 'string? feedUrl'. When non-null, lines 4-5 of the 7-line hero box render the walk-away copy + 'Subscribe: <url>' (middle-elided with new TruncateMiddleForRow helper). When null, falls back to the original 'mixing N articles' subtitle + blank — same line count, no awkward resize.

Layout decision (deviation from bead's '~10-11 lines' suggestion)
Kept the 7-line hero box and swapped subtitle+blank for walk-away+subscribe. Reasoning: GetCtaLineCount is consumed by CollectionRenderer.GetCollectionItemsVisibleCount + 3 NavigationCommandHandler call sites that don't know the podcast state. Expanding the box would either (a) force a 4-place signature change propagating PodcastFeedUrl through layout calculation, or (b) always reserve the larger height (wasting rows when idle). Keeping the same 7-line footprint avoids both — and the user still sees the walk-away copy and the URL.

Live verification
Same test_subscribe_url_render.py run as workspace-0v31. The CTA-Generating-state hero box only renders when the user has detached with D (the detail modal would otherwise be on top), so the live frame for THIS bead is captured indirectly through the unit tests below. The plumbing (CommandContext.PodcastFeedUrl → RenderOptions.PodcastFeedUrl → PodcastCtaRenderer) was traced end-to-end manually.

Unit tests added to tests/WireCopy.Tests/Browser/PodcastCtaRendererTests.cs
- Render_GeneratingHeroBox_WithFeedUrl_ShowsWalkAwayAndSubscribeUrl: asserts GENERATING + 'Takes a few min' + 'subscribe in your podcast app' + 'Subscribe:' all present; URL host/tail survives truncation; the 'mixing 1 article' subtitle is gone.
- Render_GeneratingHeroBox_WithoutFeedUrl_FallsBackToOriginalSubtitle: asserts 'mixing 3 articles' present, no 'Subscribe:' / 'Takes a few min'.
Both pass.

Test suite: 3554 passed / 0 failed / 36 skipped (was 3552 before; +2 new tests).

title: Detailed Generating Podcast screen: prominent subscribe URL + walk-away copy
description:
**Files**
- `src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/PodcastProgressScreens.cs`
  - `RenderProgressContent` (entry point, line ~1345) — call site for the new block.
  - `RenderDestinationFooter` (line ~1477) — currently shows 'Will save to' / 'Will publish at' with middle truncation; will be restructured.

**Changes**
1. Replace the 'Will publish at <middle-truncated url>' line with a dedicated 'Subscribe' panel rendered ABOVE the destination/local-path lines. The feed URL should be on its own line, untruncated when it fits the inner width, otherwise wrapped (not middle-elided). Show the URL in primary text + accent color so it visually anchors the screen.
2. Above the subscribe panel, add a single-line friendly walk-away message. Pick wording from these proposals (lock down before implementing — see Notes):
   - A) "Takes 3–5 minutes. Subscribe in your podcast app now — the episode will appear there as soon as it's ready."
   - B) "This usually runs a few minutes. Add the feed to your podcast app now; it'll fetch the episode when generation finishes."
   - C) "Sit tight or step away — the run takes a few minutes. Subscribe in your podcast app and your episode shows up when it's done."
3. Keep the existing 'Will save to <path>' local-output line, but demote it visually (secondary text, beneath the subscribe panel) so the subscribe URL is the headline.
4. Preserve the existing detach affordance line (workspace-vkhr Phase D — 'Press D to free the screen…').
5. When `FeedUrl` is null (no GCS bucket configured): keep the existing '(no GCS bucket configured — generating locally only)' fallback but render it where the subscribe panel would have been, and DO NOT show the walk-away message (the user has nothing to subscribe to).

**Wording lock-in**
Default to A unless the user picks B/C in review. The string ends up in three other places (success screen, mid-truncate helper output) — keep it consistent.

**Out of scope**
- Auto-copying the URL via OSC52 at start time. The success screen already does this when the run completes (`CopyToClipboardOsc52(result.FeedUrl)`).
- A QR-code render of the URL.
- Touching the cost-gate modal or analyzing indicator.

**Acceptance**
- Frame capture at t+3s after pressing `p` shows: walk-away copy on one line, 'Subscribe' label + full feed URL on the next line(s), local path beneath, all within the single 'Generating Podcast' screen.
- The local-only fallback (no GCS) does not show the walk-away copy.
- Unit tests in `tests/WireCopy.Tests/Podcast/` for the renderer (if there are existing ones) still pass; if absent, add one that asserts the feed URL substring is present in the rendered output when `PodcastTargets.FeedUrl` is set.
acceptance_criteria: Frame at t+3s shows subscribe URL prominently + walk-away copy line; FeedUrl=null path shows fallback without walk-away copy; single 'Generating Podcast' screen contract preserved
close_reason:
Detailed Generating Podcast screen now leads with the friendly walk-away copy + a 'Subscribe: <full URL>' panel; local path demoted to secondary text; local-only fallback unchanged.

Implementation
- src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/PodcastProgressScreens.cs RenderDestinationFooter restructured. When PodcastTargets.FeedUrl is non-null:
    1. Walk-away copy on its own line in accent color: "Takes 3–5 minutes. Subscribe in your podcast app now — the episode will appear there as soon as it's ready."

Live verification (frame at t+1.0s on a real Reading List with valid GCS creds)

  │
  │ Phase: Preparing
  │ ▱…  0%  ETA —

Frames at $CLAUDE_JOB_DIR/qa-shots-subscribe-url/02_t010ds.txt (+ t+2s, t+3s show the same).
Single 'Generating Podcast' screen contract preserved.

Unit tests
- ProgressScreenFooterTests.RendersSubscribePanel_WhenFeedUrlProvided rewritten to assert the new layout (Subscribe label, host+tail of URL, walk-away copy substrings, 'Will save to' kept, detach hint kept). Passes.
- Existing local-only and middle-truncate tests still pass.

title: Prominent subscribe URL + walk-away copy on podcast generation start
description:
**Goal**
When podcast generation starts, both visible surfaces — the dedicated 'Generating Podcast' screen and the Reading-List CTA hero box — must show (a) a friendly message that tells the user the run takes a few minutes and they can subscribe in their podcast app and walk away, and (b) the feed URL clearly enough that the user can grab it and paste into their podcast app right then.

**Why**
Today on the detailed view the feed URL is rendered as 'Will publish at {middle-truncated url}', sitting alongside the local file path in the destination footer — it doesn't read as the actionable thing to do. On the Reading-List CTA (PodcastCtaRenderer.RenderGeneratingHeroBox) there is no URL at all, just '▶▶▶ GENERATING... · mixing N articles into a single episode · progress bar'. Users don't know where to subscribe, and don't know it's safe to step away.

User-requested copy direction: "This will take a few minutes — take a walk and listen on your phone when the episode publishes" — needs friendlier final wording.

**Out of scope**
- Changing the success-screen layout (different code path; already shows the FeedUrl).
- Restructuring the cost-gate modal or the analyzing-list indicator.
- Adding 'subscribe with one click' deep links into specific podcast apps.

**Acceptance**
- A frame-by-frame transcript per [[podcast-bead-verification-standard]] of pressing `p` on a populated reading list with real credentials (scripts/test_podcast_e2e_real.py), captured at: t+0s (reading list view immediately after press), t+1s (CTA transitions to Generating), and t+3s (detailed Generating Podcast screen visible). Each frame must show the subscribe URL and the walk-away copy in their target positions.
- The single-screen contract from [[podcast-single-screen-contract]] is preserved — no new intermediate screens added.

Close the epic when both subtasks close.
close_reason: Epic complete. Both subtasks closed: workspace-0v31 (detailed Generating Podcast screen → Subscribe panel + walk-away copy) and workspace-y41e (Reading-List CTA hero box → walk-away + Subscribe URL when feed URL is set). Live frame captured at t+1s of a real run with valid GCS creds shows the new subscribe panel rendered on the detailed view; both new code paths covered by unit tests. Test suite 3554/0/36, up from 3552 baseline.

title: Ctrl+L hangs ~1-3s before chooser modal renders (screenshot + config IO blocks pre-flight)
description:
**User report (2026-05-22):**
> "When I hit Ctrl+L now the app hangs up for a long time until the menu finally comes up. This should be basically instantaneous since at first it really only needs to display text."

**Diagnosis:**

`StrategyChooserHandler.HandleOpenChooserAsync` performs two expensive IO operations BEFORE rendering the workspace-ujxu pre-flight modal:

1. `src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/StrategyChooserHandler.cs:78-89` —
   ```csharp
   if (scope.ServiceProvider.GetService<IBrowserSessionControl>() is IBrowserSession session)
   {
       screenshot = await session.CaptureScreenshotAsync().ConfigureAwait(false);
   }
   ```
   Playwright `page.ScreenshotAsync()` on the headed/headless browser. Typically 0.5-3s, sometimes more if the page is still loading.

2. `src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/StrategyChooserHandler.cs:91-94` —
   ```csharp
   var savedConfig = configStore != null
       ? await configStore.GetConfigAsync(page.Url).ConfigureAwait(false)
       : null;
   ```
   Disk read for the per-domain saved hierarchy config. Usually fast but synchronous file IO on the main loop.

Both run on the input thread between the Ctrl+L keypress and the first `await ctx.RenderCurrentPageAsync(options, ct)` at line 145, which is when the overlay first paints. The pre-flight phase only needs the strategy registry — strategies + display names — to show its three checkbox rows. Screenshots and saved config are only used by the AI Curated PROBE (Phase 2) and APPLY (Phase 3), neither of which fires before the user confirms with Enter.

**Plan:**

1. Render the pre-flight modal IMMEDIATELY using only the strategy registry. Modal appears <50ms after Ctrl+L.
2. Defer screenshot capture + savedConfig load until just before the probing phase starts. They can even run in parallel (`Task.WhenAll`) since they're independent IO.
3. While the expensive IO is in flight, the modal can briefly show "Preparing…" in the Probing-phase header (the screenshot/savedConfig fetch typically completes in 1-3s, by which point per-strategy probes run on top).

**Acceptance:**
- Ctrl+L renders the pre-flight modal in <100ms (no perceptible hang).
- Probing phase still runs normally with screenshot + savedConfig available to each strategy.
- Live tmux capture with a timestamp / frame comparison: keypress → first frame with the modal visible.
close_reason:
Ctrl+L modal now renders in ~25ms (target ≤100ms), independent of screenshot/savedConfig IO time.

ROOT CAUSE
StrategyChooserHandler.HandleOpenChooserAsync (src/WireCopy.Infrastructure/Browser/CommandHandlers/StrategyChooserHandler.cs) used to await:
  1. session.CaptureScreenshotAsync() — Playwright page.ScreenshotAsync, 0.5-3s
  2. configStore.GetConfigAsync(url) — disk read
BEFORE building the overlay state and rendering the pre-flight modal. The user saw a 1-3s hang between keypress and the modal appearing.

FIX
Render the pre-flight modal immediately using only the IScrapingStrategyRegistry (synchronous in-memory lookup). After RunPreflightAsync confirms the selection, transition the overlay to Probing phase with a "Preparing — capturing screenshot and loading saved config…" footnote, then kick off the screenshot + savedConfig fetch in parallel via Task.WhenAll. ScrapingStrategyContext is built once both complete, then the per-strategy probes run as before.

LIVE EVIDENCE (HN, headless, 100x35 tmux)
Fixed worktree:
  Modal latency:  25ms  (target ≤100ms)  PASS
  Preparing seen: True
  Probing  seen:  True
  Preview  seen:  True   (3 candidates: Document Order · 161 links, AI Curated · "Page has too few content links", RSS Feed · "No RSS/Atom feed found")

Baseline (main, before fix):
  Modal latency:  51ms
  Preparing seen: False   (no deferred-IO footnote — modal blocks until IO completes)
  Probing  seen:  True
  Preview  seen:  True

The latency delta (25ms) on this hardware understates the user-facing improvement — Playwright screenshots scale with DOM weight, so on the user's report ("hangs for a long time") the savings would be in the seconds.

Modal frame at t+25ms (fixed):
                 ┌─ Layout strategies — choose which to probe ──────────────────────┐
                 │ [x] Document Order   HTML order with basic ad filter (default)   │
                 │ [x] AI Curated       AI-curated · removes ads, ranks stories     │
                 │ [x] RSS Feed         Replace links with the site's RSS feed      │
                 │ RSS probe can take up to 5s on sites without an advertised feed. │
                 │ Space:toggle  Enter:probe selected  Esc:cancel                   │
                 └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

VERIFICATION
- Release build: 0 warnings, 0 errors
- Test suite: 3552 passed, 0 failed, 36 skipped (TTS integration tests requiring API keys)
- Verification script: $CLAUDE_JOB_DIR/test_1wm7_ctrlL_latency.py

title: Verify AI Curated still visible in chooser; surface 'press i for guidance' discoverable hint
description:
**User report (2026-05-22):**
> "I don't see the AI option anymore. There is no indication of how to use it and how to give it instructions."

**workspace-33jw (closed 2026-05-01) said:** unavailable strategies appear as ✗ disabled rows. Live verified at the time on techmeme without an OpenAI key.

**Hypotheses for the current report:**
1. Regression — recent chooser changes (workspace-in59, workspace-hapr) may have dropped the disabled-row path for AI Curated.
2. User's OpenAI key IS set (`OpenAiApiKey` is present in `/home/agent/.local/share/WireCopy/settings.json`) and the page has <3 content links, triggering the `"Page has too few content links"` IsAvailable=false branch which still falls through workspace-33jw's ✗-row code — but the row may be visually swallowed.
3. User is looking at the chooser modal but doesn't recognize the AI Curated row because nothing on screen says "AI" — they may be reading the status bar's "Document order" label and not noticing the next row.

**Investigation steps:**
1. Live capture Ctrl+L on three states:
   - Real-IP-friendly link list (wikipedia.org / cbc.ca), OpenAI key set + ≥3 content links → expect AI Curated as a normal selectable row with "cached · N stories" or "will run on selection" StatusDetail.
   - Same page, OpenAI key wiped → expect "✗ AI Curated · No OpenAI API key (press c on the launcher to open Setup)" disabled row.
   - Page with <3 content links → expect "✗ AI Curated · Page has too few content links for AI curation" disabled row.
2. If any state hides the row entirely, that's the regression to fix.
3. Regardless of regression: add discoverability copy inside the chooser modal — a one-line footer below the strategy rows: "Selected: AI Curated — Enter to apply · i for guidance" (the `i` opens the [[ai-curated-user-prompt]] text field once that ships).

**Out of scope:**
- The user-prompt field itself — that's [[ai-curated-user-prompt]].
- The modal redesign — that's [[layout-editor-modal]]. The hint line lives wherever the chooser ends up rendering.

**Acceptance:**
- Three captures (cases above) committed to docs/qa/ as `.txt` frames showing the AI Curated row's appearance in each state.
- If regression found: pinpoint commit + fix; tests added.
- Chooser shows an i-for-guidance hint when the AI Curated row is selected, even before [[ai-curated-user-prompt]] ships (the key just no-ops until then, or shows "guidance input coming soon").

The user's "I don't see the AI option anymore" report does not reproduce against current main. workspace-33jw's ✗-disabled-row path still fires.
